title: Embeddedutvecklare
description:
##### **Om kunden**


Vår kund är ett internationellt högteknologiskt företag inom försvarsindustrin som utvecklar avancerade system och lösningar för ett säkrare samhälle. Verksamheten präglas av innovation, tekniskt kunnande och långsiktighet, där kvalitet och samarbete står i centrum. Här erbjuds en utvecklande miljö med stora möjligheter att växa både tekniskt och professionellt.

##### **Om tjänsten**


Nu söker vi en embeddedutvecklare som vill arbeta med avancerade system där kvalitet, säkerhet och teknisk precision är centrala delar av utvecklingen.


I rollen arbetar du med mjukvaruutveckling nära hårdvaran och är delaktig genom hela utvecklingsprocessen: från krav och design till implementation, test och verifiering. Utvecklingen sker främst i C/C++ i Linuxbaserade miljöer med fokus på robusta och hållbara embeddedlösningar.


Du blir en del av ett agilt team där samarbete och kunskapsdelning är en naturlig del av vardagen. Rollen erbjuder goda möjligheter att utvecklas tekniskt, ta större ansvar över tid och bidra i tekniska diskussioner tillsammans med erfarna kollegor. I tjänsten kan du även komma att ha internationella kontaktytor och samarbeta med team och funktioner i andra delar av verksamheten.

##### **Din kompetens**


Vi söker dig som har:

* Minst två års eftergymnasial utbildning inom relevant område
* Minst tre års erfarenhet av embeddedutveckling eller hårdvarunära mjukvaruutveckling
* Mycket goda kunskaper i C/C++
* Erfarenhet av utveckling i Linuxmiljö
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av agila arbetssätt, Yocto, DevSecOps eller arbete enligt flygstandarder såsom RTCA DO-178C.


Som person är du en lagspelare som trivs med att samarbeta och dela kunskap med andra. Du har ett ödmjukt förhållningssätt och uppskattar att arbeta i team där man stöttar varandra och utvecklas tillsammans. Rollen kan även innebära att stötta och vägleda mer juniora kollegor i tekniska sammanhang.

##### **Övrigt**


Lön: Fast månadslön


Omfattning: Heltid, tillsvidare


Start: Omgående med hänsyn tagen till din eventuella uppsägningstid


Placeringsort: Linköping


Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För denna tjänst kan krav på svenskt medborgarskap förekomma.


Vi behandlar ansökningar löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut, skicka din ansökan redan idag!

##### **Om oss**


Procruitment är Sveriges enda karriärbyrå inom IT, tech och engineering som genom proaktiv rekrytering förenar attraktiva arbetsgivare med människor som delar samma mål och ambitioner. Oavsett om du är en erfaren ledare eller om du ska ta ditt första steg ut i karriären hjälper vi dig att nå dina karriärmål - samtidigt som vi bryr oss om dig, din resa och att du mår bra längs vägen.  

  

Procruitment är stolta vinnare av Recruitment Awards och är utsedda till ***Årets Rekryteringsföretag*** och ***Årets Raket***samt ***Gasellföretag 2024*** *av* Dagens Industri*.*

title: Senior Frontend Developer TBA (fixed-term employment, parental leave cover)
description:
**About the Role**
------------------


As an Senior Frontend Developer, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining the websites that power our auction platforms. In this role, you'll build, optimise, and maintain critical user experiences that drive engagement on our online auctioning platform. Through a blend of strategic problem solving and coding, you'll contribute to positioning TBAuctions and its brands as leaders in the online auction industry.


You'll work closely with cross-functional teams across multiple locations, contributing to platform-wide technical decisions while growing your expertise in modern frontend architecture and user experience based optimisation.

*This is a fixed-term position to cover a parental leave. The employment is expected to last for 7 months, with a preferred start date in mid-June.*


You will be employed by Klaravik AB but will work with TBAuctions

**What You Will Do**
--------------------

### **Development & Delivery**

* Design, develop, and maintain Next.js websites to handle auction browsing, real-time bidding, user dashboards, and payment flows
* Write clean, performant code with a focus on user experience while keeping it reusable and maintainable
* Implement responsive deigns using Tailwind CSS, optimising for all devices
* Contribute to real-time features that power our live auction experiences

### **Performance & SEO**

* Optimise Core Web Vitals and implement strategic rendering patterns (SSR, SSG, ISR) to maximise performance and search visibility
* Build SEO-friendly page structures with proper metadata, structured data, and semantic HTML
* Implement and manage feature flags to enable safe, incremental rollouts and (in the future) A/B testing
* Monitor and improve Lighthouse scores, ensuring fast, accessible user experiences

### **Cloud & DevOps**

* Deploy and maintain applications within our Azure cloud infrastructure
* Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps
* Implement monitoring and error tracking to ensure application health and rapid issue resolution (Sentry, Grafana Cloud)
* Work with Docker to ensure compatibility between servers
* Work with Kubernetes to understand and diagnose our Next.js applications

### **Collaboration & Growth**

* Collaborate with local and international teams to deliver high-quality, scalable solutions
* Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to frontend patterns and standards
* Partner with product managers, designers, senior engineers, and architects to turn business needs into exceptional user experiences
* Share knowledge and support continuous improvement within the team

**What You Bring**
------------------

### **Technical Skills**

* Strong proficiency in React and Next.js with 3–5 years in frontend development
* Deep understanding of Tailwind CSS and modern styling approaches
* Solid grasp of SEO principles, including technical SEO, metadata management, and structured data
* Experience with feature flag systems (LaunchDarkly, Unleash, or similar)
* Familiarity with Azure services and cloud-native deployment patterns
* Understanding of performance optimisation techniques and Core Web Vitals
* Experience with CI/CD practices and tooling (Azure DevOps preferred)

### **Mindset & Soft Skills**

* A self-starter who doesn't wait to be told what to do, you spot opportunities and bring them to the team
* A genuine team player who believes the best work happens together
* A pragmatic, outcome driven approach to problem solving with user experience at the centre
* Naturally curious with a things around the web, how can we improve by learning
* Generous with your knowledge and ready to help colleagues succeed

**Nice to Have**
----------------

* Experience with real-time data handling (WebSockets)
* Familiarity with monitoring and observability tools
* Experience working as part of an agile team (Scrum)
* Understanding of accessibility standards (WCAG) and inclusive design
* Exposure to headless CMS platforms or composable architectures
* Experience with testing frameworks
* Experience mentoring other developers

### **Details**

* Remote work
* You need to have a valid right to work in Sweden. Unfortunately, we are not able to offer visa sponsorship for this role.
* Salary: to be agreed upon
* Start date: preferred mid June

title: Web Developer
description:
We’re looking for a
 **Web Developer** 
 to join us at
 **Telia** 
 .
   

  

 I’m
 **Katti Ramber,** 
**Head of Live & Event** 
 s, and I’m searching for someone ready to grow with us. We’re a supportive, forward‑thinking team focused on building engaging, scalable streaming experiences across web and Smart TV platforms for millions of users.
   

  

 The team behind
 **Telia Play** 
 consists of passionate experts in Streaming & TV, shaping the future of entertainment in the Nordics. Telia Play is used by millions of users, and we strive to make content discovery seamless while delivering outstanding sports, live events, and on‑demand experiences.
   

  

 We are now looking for a
 **Web Developer** 
 to strengthen our
 **Live & Events (L&E)** 
 area and help us continue building best‑in‑class web and Smart TV experiences
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
 Play a key role in designing, building, and maintaining modern web applications used at scale by millions of users, with a strong focus on performance, reliability, and user experience.
   

  

* Build and evolve front‑end solutions for web and Smart TV platforms, supporting a large and diverse user base
* Collaborate closely with designers, product owners, and backend teams to deliver high‑quality streaming experiences
* Ensure excellent performance across devices and browsers
* Integrate backend APIs in a performant way (GraphQL and REST)
* Drive automation and quality, contributing to smooth and reliable release processes
* Work with CI/CD pipelines, Git/GitHub workflows, and code reviews to support sustainable development
* Contribute to architecture and best practices, continuously improving how we build and ship software
* Bring curiosity and new ideas, including exploring modern tooling, frameworks, and AI/LLM opportunities where relevant


**What will help you thrive in this role:**
 We’re looking for a Web Developer with strong technical expertise and a collaborative mindset, who enjoys taking ownership from idea to production.
   

  

**You have:**
* 3+ years of experience working with React (or equivalent frameworks)
* Strong knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, and responsive design
* Experience building scalable front‑end applications used by large user bases
* Solid experience with API‑driven architectures
* Confidence working with Git, GitHub workflows, CI/CD, and code reviews
* A strong focus on code quality, testing, and long‑term maintainability
* The ability to take ownership and proactively suggest improvements


**Bonus if you have experience with:**
* Smart TV platforms or streaming/TV products
* Accessibility best practices and SEO
* AWS (Terraform / CloudFormation)
* Monitoring and observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, OpenSearch)
* GraphQL
* Performance optimization for low‑end devices with memory and CPU constraints


**Our tech stack**
 Some of the technologies we work with:
   

  

* React / SolidJS / NextJS
* TypeScript
* Storybook
* Custom‑built design system
* GraphQL
* AWS


**Join us to shape smarter living for people, businesses, and the planet**
 At Telia, we’re a team of 15 000 colleagues across the Nordics and Baltics, passionate about technology and connectivity. As a
 **Leading Telecommunications Operator** 
 &
 **Climate Leader** 
 , we drive change with large-scale, impactful projects while building a sustainable, inclusive world.
   

  

 Here, you can
 **grow your skills, lead meaningful projects, and thrive in a supportive, diverse environment.** 
 Whether you’re taking your first step or your next, Telia is a place to grow, belong, and make a real impact.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**For Lithuanian applicants only: monthly salary range for this position - 3600-5400 Eur (gross) + annual performance bonuses** 
 . Although it may vary depending on your skills and experience.
   

  

**Ready to make better happen?**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **June 1.** 
 Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!
   

  

 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner** 
 . Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by
 **professional skills and growth potential.** 
 We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where
 **your uniqueness is our strength.**
**Join us and let’s make better happen - together!**
**More about our culture:** 
 www.telia.lt/karjera

title: Software Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 As a Senior Software Engineer at Legora you will build, review and ship amazing products helping legal professionals all over the world in their day-to-day job. You’ll get the chance to work across the entire tech stack with clear ownership and autonomy.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Architect and implement features end-to-end for our web platform
* Take ownership of our codebase, balancing high-quality code, security and speed of development
* Work with technologies such as React, Node.js, AWS, Azure and Kubernetes
* Review code and mentor junior colleagues to support them in their development, making the team even better!
* Drive continuous improvement and challenge us, the industry and yourself


**Who You Are**
* A passionate engineer with a several years of experience building great products using cutting-edge technologies
* You feel comfortable working across the entire tech stack, with focus on backend development
* You’re autonomous and take pride in the code you write
* You’re a problem solver who’s not afraid to come up with creative solutions and think holistically about software architecture
* Someone who enjoys being in the office 5 days a week. A key component of Legora’s success has been in-person collaboration and we want you to be part of that.
* Experience in infrastructure and integrations are a big plus


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Backend Software Engineer
description:
We at Umain are looking for
 **Senior Backend Engineers** 
 with a genuine enthusiasm for building robust solutions that power digital experiences — engineers ready to bring passion, creativity, and dedication with them.
   

  

 The role is broad but centered on backend technologies, offering the opportunity to work on a variety of projects for both Swedish and international companies.
   

  

 We work with many different tech stacks, but most of our projects are written in
 **Kotlin, Go, or TypeScript** 
 . Our approach is to use the tools best suited for each project, ensuring our teams are not locked into a single stack.
   

  

**We Have a Proven Track Record**
* 400M+ total app downloads
* 50M+ monthly active users
* Backends handling tens of thousands of requests per second
* Globally recognized, multi-market applications


 If working at scale with technology that truly makes an impact excites you, we believe you'll be a great fit at Umain.
   

  

**What We Offer**
* A chance to work on a range of diverse and unique projects that explore and push the boundaries of what's possible.
* A fun and fair work environment where you have the opportunity to expand your skills through unique challenges and fantastic colleagues.
* A personal coach, and a setting where you can grow professionally and personally.
* Being part of multidisciplinary teams, participating in agile teams, being an important player in a highly ambitious setting.


 As a Senior Backend Developer at Umain, your role will involve:
   

  

* Building high-performance backend systems using modern languages like Go, NodeJS, Python, and Kotlin to power innovative experiences
* Using modern backend frameworks such as Fiber, Ktor, Express and FastAPI to create robust solutions.
* Designing and implementing cloud-native solutions across AWS, GCP and Azure, using Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform) for resource management
* Building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines with containerized deployments and scaling strategies
* Developing robust data models and database architectures to support application requirements
* Participating in projects & teams alongside some of the largest and most forward-thinking brands on the planet


**Who You Are**
* You're a developer with a passion for innovation and a knack for problem-solving.
* You're eager to learn, grow, and take on new challenges.
* You value collaboration, diversity, and the opportunity to make a significant impact.
* You believe in the power of technology to shape the future.


 Why Umain?
   

  

**Career Framework**
 We believe in fairness and clarity, which is why we've established a transparent career framework to ensure equal pay and equal opportunities for all. Your growth and success are as important to us as they are to you. Together with your coach, you will build your own development plan and grow in your career.
   

  

**Work-Life Balance**
 At Umain, diversity is our strength. We understand that life doesn’t pause when you walk into the office. That's why we've cultivated an environment that supports an excellent work-life balance. We welcome team members from all walks of life, recognizing the richness that different backgrounds, ages, and life stages bring to our team. We're committed to supporting each other, encouraging a healthy work-life balance that respects your needs, whether it's taking care of your family, your pets, or yourself.
   

  

**Innovations And Learning**
 Technology is always evolving, bringing new challenges and opportunities for collaboration and learning. Our developers work closely with designers, analysts, testers and more to tackle these challenges head-on, sharing knowledge and growing together.
   

  

**Skills Over Degrees**
 We’re more interested in what you can do than in the diplomas you hold. Talent, drive, and creativity are what count at Umain. Here, you’ll find opportunities to grow, learn, and excel in your career, regardless of your educational background. If you have the ambition and commitment to succeed, we have the environment and resources to support your journey.
   

  

**Are you ready to SHAPE, SHIP, and SCALE with us?**
 If you're a developer who thrives on innovation, values a supportive and inclusive workplace, and is eager to make a tangible impact on the future, we’d love to hear from you. Let’s build something awesome together.
   

  

***Background check***
*Umain conducts a background check for final candidates in accordance with our* 
 internal policies
 *. The check is carried out only with your consent and is ordered by our CISO through our approved screening partner. The scope of the check depends on the role level. This step is performed late in the recruitment process and is used solely to support a secure and fact‑based hiring decision.*
 Umain is a digital product consultancy. We shape, ship and scale digital products at speed. Founded in Stockholm in 2001, we are a growing team of 100+ experts in digital product strategy, design, and technology.
   

  

 We are a part of Eidra, a group of top-notch companies specializing in strategy, creativity, and engineering. As a collective we guide leaders in making meaningful and lasting change.

title: Backend Software Engineer (Node.js) 💻
description:
Build scalable backend systems used across connected products and platforms in a global tech environment where reliability, security, and performance are at the core.
 



  





**About the role** 




 Wrknest is recruiting a Backend Software Engineer on behalf of an international technology company based in Stockholm. The company develops advanced connected platforms used by businesses and consumers worldwide, with a strong focus on networking, cloud infrastructure, and smart device ecosystems.
 



 In this role, you will join a highly skilled engineering team responsible for developing and maintaining core backend systems powering connected products and services. The position is ideal for someone who enjoys solving technically complex challenges, improving system reliability, and working across backend, cloud, embedded, and application environments.
 



 You will become part of a collaborative and product-focused engineering culture where quality, ownership, and continuous improvement are highly valued.
 



  





**Your future tasks** 




 ou will play an important role in designing and developing backend services that support large-scale connected systems and applications. The role involves close collaboration with several technical teams and offers the opportunity to influence both architecture and development processes.
 



  





 Examples of responsibilities include:
 


* Designing, building, and maintaining backend services using Node.js and TypeScript
* Developing secure, scalable, and maintainable APIs and workflows
* Contributing to architectural discussions and technical decisions
* Reviewing code and supporting high engineering standards and release quality
* Investigating production issues and implementing robust solutions
* Collaborating with teams within embedded, web, mobile, product, and design
* Improving reliability, observability, and developer experience across services



  





**We are looking for someone who** 




  





**Requirements** 



* At least 5 years of professional experience working with Node.js and TypeScript
* Minimum 3 years of experience working with SQL databases such as PostgreSQL
* Experience developing software for connected devices, infrastructure products, networking systems, or similar complex environments
* Good understanding of web security, including OWASP, OAuth, and browser security fundamentals
* Strong debugging skills and experience with defensive programming
* Professional experience with Git, Unix/Linux, and Docker-based environments
* Ability to work independently and take ownership of technical solutions
* Strong communication skills in English, both written and spoken
* Valid Swedish work permit and residence in Sweden at the time of application



  





**Meritorious** 



* Experience with Rust or Go
* Experience with gRPC
* Experience with WebRTC
* Experience with DevOps, CI/CD, and build pipelines
* Experience using AI tools and LLMs to improve engineering workflows and productivity



  





 The client values engineers who are curious, solution-oriented, and motivated by building reliable products in technically advanced environments. You are comfortable collaborating across teams while also taking ownership of your own work.
 



  





**Other information** 




 Start: According to agreement
 



 Location: Stockholm, Sweden
 



 Employment type: Full-time
 



 Work model: Hybrid
 



  





**Benefits** 



* Competitive compensation package
* Occupational pension and insurance package
* Wellness allowance
* Flexible working hours
* Access to modern technical equipment and products



  





 Please note that a background check will be conducted in the final stage of the recruitment process.
 



  





**About the client** 




 The client is an international technology company developing advanced platforms and connected solutions used globally within networking, infrastructure, and smart technology. Their products combine software, cloud services, and hardware to create scalable and reliable ecosystems used by millions of users worldwide.
 



 The company is driven by innovation, technical excellence, and long-term product development, offering employees the opportunity to work on impactful technology in an international environment.
 



  





**About Wrknest Engineering** 




 Wrknest Engineering specializes in helping engineers and technicians find their next career step. We collaborate with forward-thinking companies and offer both recruitment and consulting assignments within technology and industry, always with people at the center.
 



 We believe in building long-term relationships and strive to be an engaged and attentive partner. Through us, you gain the opportunity to be part of technological advancements that shape tomorrow’s society, in roles where your expertise makes a real difference.
 



 Our ambition is for you to thrive, grow, and feel proud of your work. Read more at
 
 www.wrknestengineering.se
 
 .

title: Staff Software Engineer
description:
**Company Overview**
 Metrea delivers effects-as-a-service to national security partners across five domains and more than a dozen mission areas—including airborne ISR, electronic warfare, secure communications, aerial refueling, special mission aviation, aerial firefighting, and advanced simulation.
   

  

 Wherever we operate, we build vertically integrated full stacks of capability—designing, building, and operating turnkey solutions that let customers scale capacity while benefiting from continuous cycles of innovation. With operators and engineers under one roof, we close the gap between lab and field—what we call connecting design with effect.
   

  

 Metrea’s solutions are built for elegance: effective, efficient, and evolving. This approach enables our partners to do more with less and achieve outsized, asymmetric advantage against rapidly evolving threats.
   

  

 Headquartered in Washington, DC, Metrea has facilities across the United States, the United Kingdom, Europe, and beyond.
   

  

**Group Overview**
 The Digital and Synthetic Capability Unit (D&S) is committed to providing mission-driven information solutions that seamlessly bridge the digital and physical realms. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies and advanced platforms, we empower operational readiness and elevate situational awareness across diverse domains—including air, maritime, and space.
   

  

**Position Summary**
 In this role, you will lead front‑end development for our large-scale simulation platform. You will build user‑friendly, responsive controls that can be adjusted during runtime, enabling seamless switching between multiple layers of information. You will collaborate closely with product designers and a cross-functional team of engineers to ensure a smooth, intuitive and powerful user experience. You will join the community of D&S front-end developers that provides mentoring and technical growth. You will report to the Engineering Manager.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Lead the development of the UI for a large-scale simulation application
* Build user‑friendly controls that can be adjusted during runtime, enabling seamless switching between multiple layers of information
* Ensure a smooth, responsive and intuitive user experience
* Establish architectural patterns and own decisions around component structure, state management, code organisation, and scalability
* Ensure code quality and maintainability, promoting good engineering practices
* Collaborate within and beyond the team, articulating technical issues to non-technical team members
* Regularly mentor other less experienced engineers and help with on-boarding new engineers to your team


**What You Bring**
* 3+ years of demonstrable experience in an active software development role focused on web applications
* Deep practical expertise in React, including component library development and integration
* Ability to translate Figma designs into high-quality, pixel-accurate React components
* Strong frontend architecture skills: establishing patterns and owning decisions around component structure, state management, code organisation, and scalability
* Experience of team delivery in an Agile software development environment, ensuring code quality and maintainability
* Strong organisational skills: able to work independently; proactive approach to task delivery and documentation
* Great interpersonal and collaboration skills; able to articulate technical issues to non-technical team members
* Drive to learn constantly and further your knowledge


**In addition to the above, any of the following would be beneficial:**
* Experience with geospatial, data visualization or simulation applications
* Familiarity with game development technologies and practices
* Understanding of database technologies


**Benefits**
* Friskvård of 5K annually
* Parental leave
* 5% occupational pension (swe. tjänstepension),
* 30 days of leave
* Bonus Scheme
* Personal development opportunities


**Work Authorization / Security Clearance**
 The employee will have the legal right to work in the Sweden and will be able to produce right to work documentation.
   

  

**AAP/EEO Statement**
 Metrea Simulations is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or other characteristics protected by law.
   

  

**Position Type and Expected Hours of Work**
 This is a full-time non-exempt position with typical working hours of Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Hours and work shifts may change in accordance with department and business needs. Exempt Employees must have the ability to be on-call and available, as business needs require. Non-Exempt employees may be required to work over 40 hours per week with approval from the department manager.
   

  

**Work Environment**
 This job operates in an office setting
   

  

**Travel**
 This role may require occasional travel to other Metrea offices.
   

  

 Our Firmware
   

  

 At Metrea, our single core value is Rooted in Humility, and our four cornerstone attributes are Entrepreneurial, Systematic, Discerning, and Over-Deliver. These form what we call our Teammate Firmware. Just like technical firmware connects software and hardware, our Firmware is the constant interface between our mission and our people. It defines how we show up, how we work together, and how we solve complex problems.
   

  

 Our team Firmware creates a web-like, hyper-collaborative, dynamically hierarchical way of working that helps us adapt quickly, communicate openly, and distribute decision-making to where expertise actually lives. It enables groups to self-organize around hard problems, shift fluidly as priorities evolve, and operate with the trust, curiosity, and discipline required in a complex mission space. This foundation allows us to deliver elegant, effective solutions and uphold our purpose: protecting our precious inheritance.
