 Är du rätt person för uppdraget, eller vill du rekommendera en stark kandidat? Tveka inte att kontakta oss.
   

  

 Ansök gärna redan idag, urval och intervjuer sker löpande
   

  

 Ansök genom att bifoga ditt CV (vi vill inte ha något personligt brev) och besvara urvalsfrågorna. Frågorna utgår från kravprofilen för den här rekryteringen och dina svar ligger till grund för det urval som sker i processen. Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.
   

  

 Vänligen notera att krav och eventuella meriter ska vara uppfyllda vid ansökningstillfället.

title: Travel Consultant
description:
Amex GBT is a place where colleagues find inspiration in travel as a force for good and – through their work – can make an impact on our industry. We’re here to help our colleagues achieve success and offer an inclusive and collaborative culture where your voice is valued.
   

  

 We are looking for a highly skilled and experienced Travel Consultant to join our client team. Consultants work in a high-paced service environment to book travel and accommodation for our top clients.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Create and complete travel arrangements (air, hotel, car, rail)
* Ensure reservations are built according to client standards and preferences
* Act as a trusted advisor by offering informed and insightful recommendations that provide the best traveler experience
* Handle a wide range of bookings from simple ones to complex bookings with potentially non-standard and multi-destinations, ticket exchanges and complex fares
* Make changes or solve any issues that might occur during or before the travel
* Assignments include both routine and non-routine work
* Benefit from comprehensive training by experienced colleagues and use our social intranet to always stay up to date


**What We're Looking For**
* 2 or more years’ experience working as a Travel Consultant
* Advanced knowledge of Amadeus
* A genuine passion for high-quality customer service — we care about our customers and it’s important to us that you do too
* Good verbal and written communication skills both in Swedish and English
* Strong team working skills
* Be curious and enjoy continuously evolving working place
* Willingness to learn and grow!


***Successful candidate must be a Sweden resident***
**Location**
 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**The #TeamGBT Experience**
 Work and life: Find your happy medium at Amex GBT.
   

  

* Flexible benefits are tailored to each country and start the day you do. These include health and welfare insurance plans, retirement programs, parental leave, adoption assistance, and wellbeing resources to support you and your immediate family.
* Travel perks: get a choice of deals each week from major travel providers on everything from flights to hotels to cruises and car rentals.
* Develop the skills you want when the time is right for you, with access to over 20,000 courses on our learning platform, leadership courses, and new job openings available to internal candidates first.
* We strive to champion Inclusion in every aspect of our business at Amex GBT. You can connect with colleagues through our global INclusion Groups, centered around common identities or initiatives, to discuss challenges, obstacles, achievements, and drive company awareness and action.
* And much more!


 This role is for CWT, a member of the American Express Global Business Travel family of companies.
   

  

 All applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to age, sex, gender (and characteristics related to sex and gender), pregnancy (and related medical conditions), race, color, citizenship, religion, disability, or any other class or characteristic protected by law.
   

  

 Furthermore, we are committed to providing reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities. Please let your recruiter know if you need an accommodation at any point during the hiring process. For details regarding how we protect your data, please consult the Amex GBT Recruitment Privacy Statement.
   

  

 Click Here for Additional Disclosures in Accordance with the LA County Fair Chance Ordinance.
   

  

**What if I don’t meet every requirement?** 
 If you’re passionate about our mission and believe you’d be a phenomenal addition to our team, don’t worry about “checking every box;" please apply anyway. You may be exactly the person we’re looking for!

title: Backend Engineer - Java
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.
   

  

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*
**Your Profile (Must-have Requirements)**
* Strong Java development skills, with experience in data platform or data engineering contexts
* Practical experience with at least one JVM-based data processing framework — Flink experience is a plus; Beam, Dataflow, or Spark also relevant
* Comfortable with SQL and cloud data analytics platforms, particularly BigQuery
* DevOps is part of your day-to-day: you work with cloud infrastructure, containerised applications, and are familiar with Kubernetes basics
* Experience working with data engineering pipelines in Scala and/or Python
* You write quality code and understand what it means to ship reliably in a production environment
* You can work autonomously in an ambiguous environment and move quickly without waiting to be directed


**Meritorious**
* Prior experience with large-scale pipeline migrations
* Familiarity with cost optimisation in cloud data processing workloads


**Application deadline:** 
 14-05-2026
   

  

**About Us**
 We currently employ experienced consultants in areas such as System Development, AI, Machine Learning, Testing, Cloud, Infra DevOps, and IT Project Management.
   

  

 Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?
   

  

 We simply offer more than just a high salary and greater freedom; that’s just the beginning. With us, you’ll find the work environment and opportunities you deserve. Welcome to Deploja, where the grass is indeed greener!
   

  

**Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!**
 🚀 Exciting assignments with our many clients. We focus on long-term projects, which gives you the opportunity to create value for the client while also developing yourself.
   

  

 🌟 A secure monthly salary – together we’ll agree on a base salary depending on your assignments.
   

  

 🚗 Company car – we encourage you to choose an electric car for a more sustainable future.
   

  

 💼 Occupational pension and great opportunities for salary conversion.
   

  

 🌴 Vacation – Would you like more than 30 days of vacation, perhaps 60 days?
   

  

 🏥 Private health insurance, accident insurance, and life insurance for increased security.
   

  

 📚 Skills development – you set your own budget and choose the training you wish to attend.
   

  

 🕑 Quality of life – Are you struggling to balance everyday life and wish to work a little less while still earning as much, if not more?
   

  

*We handle applications continuously, and due to the high workload, it's especially important to include a short motivation for the position, explaining how you meet the requirements.*

title: Ledningstekniker
description:
Vill du vara med och bidra till ett hållbart samhälle genom att säkerställa att vi har ett väl fungerande VA-ledningsnät, samtidigt som du får jobba utomhus och nära samhällets vardag? Då är detta rollen för dig.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som Ledningstekniker arbetar du både självständigt och tillsammans med kollegor för att säkerställa att ledningsnätet i Vallentuna och Knivsta kommun fungerar och ständigt förbättras. Du kommer arbeta med avhjälpande och förebyggande underhåll på våra dricks-, spill- och dagvattenledningsnät. I rollen ingår även vattenmätarhantering, serviceärenden gentemot våra kunder och ledningsnätsrelaterade utredningar. Du kommer att ha goda möjligheter att påverka ditt arbete eftersom vi är en lärande organisation med stor vilja att förbättra i alla delar av verksamheten.
   

  

 I rollen som Ledningstekniker hos oss kommer du bland annat få möjligheten att:
   

  

* Utvecklas och själv kunna påverka Roslagsvattens utveckling inom ledningsnätsunderhåll och digitalisering i fält.
* Utbildas inom ett brett spektrum av kompetenser, från läcksökningsutbildning till utbildningar i säkra schakter, livsmedelshantering med mera.
* Delta i planering av enhetens underhållsprojekt och därmed få stor möjlighet att påverka hur projekten utformas och utförs.
* Arbeta i en grupp med mycket god sammanhållning och laganda


 Efter en tids upplärning kan beredskapstjänstgöring ingå i arbetsuppgifterna.
   

  

**Om dig**
 Vi söker dig som har några års arbetslivserfarenhet av praktiskt arbete inom VA- och/eller annat underhållsarbete. Du är lösningsorienterad och drivs av att ständigt förbättra både din egen kompetens och arbetsplatsen i stort. Självklart är du noggrann och ansvarstagande. Du förstår att din insats är avgörande för att vårt ledningsnät ska må så bra som möjligt – så länge som möjligt och motiveras av detta.
   

  

 Då arbetet bitvis är tungt är en god fysisk kondition och ett utpräglat säkerhetstänk en förutsättning. Du ser det som självklart att verka för en god arbetsmiljö för dig och dina kollegor och när du ser en risk är du inte rädd för att belysa den och föreslå lösningar.
   

  

 Som representant för Roslagsvatten ute på fältet möter du flera av Roslagsvattens intressenter vilket ställer krav på ett serviceinriktat och kommunikativt agerande, både gentemot kunder, entreprenörer och arbetskamrater.
   

  

**Krav**
* Gymnasieutbildning
* B-körkort
* Grundläggande IT-kunskaper
* God svenska i tal och skrift


**Meriterande**
* Tidigare erfarenhet av att arbeta i kommunal VA-verksamhet
* Tidigare erfarenhet av att arbeta med förebyggande och avhjälpande underhåll
* Utbildning Arbete på väg
* Utbildning Heta arbeten
* Utbildning Livsmedelshygien dricksvatten


**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
* Friskvårdsbidrag samt möjlighet till friskvård på arbetstid
* 29 semesterdagar samt möjlighet att semesterväxla
* Subventionerade luncher med Edenreds lunchkort
* Årlig bonus baserat på bolagets resultat


**Praktisk information**
 Tillträde: Höst 2026
   

  

 Placering: Utgår ifrån Smidesvägen 5, Vallentuna
   

  

 Arbetstid: 07:00 – 15:45.
   

  

 Anställning: Tillsvidareanställning på heltid, med tillämpning av sex månaders provanställning. Med anställning följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras.
   

  

 För frågor gällande tjänsten kontakta Platschef Niklas Högborn på niklas.hogborn@roslagsvatten.se. Vid frågor om rekryteringsprocessen är du välkommen att kontakta HR-partner Sarah Mellqvist på sarah.mellqvist@roslagsvatten.se.
   

  

 Urval och intervjuer sker löpande. Tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag.
   

  

 Sista ansökningsdag 2026-06-05

title: Product Operations Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**About The Role**
 As a
 **Product Operations Manager** 
 , you’ll sit at the heart of how Legora scales its product. You’ll help us turn complexity into clarity by solving stakeholder alignment challenges, enabling cross‑functional collaboration at scale, and ensuring our teams can ship high‑quality AI products — fast and smoothly.
   

  

 This is a front‑row role at the edge of our product and release engine. You’ll work closely with Product, Engineering, Design, Marketing, Customer Enablement, and Client Support to ensure launches land smoothly, insights flow back into product decisions, and operational friction never slows us down.
   

  

 You’ll also be hands‑on with AI‑driven problem solving: using modern AI tools, building and defining agents, and designing systems that help teams make better decisions and move faster. If you enjoy connecting dots across teams, designing scalable systems, and being the person who makes complex things work — this role is for you.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Own product operations at scale: create clarity across fast‑moving teams by defining processes, rituals, and tooling that keep Product, Engineering, and Go‑to‑Market aligned as we grow.
* Be the connective tissue between teams: proactively identify stakeholder misalignment, surface trade‑offs, and drive resolution across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Customer Enablement, and Client Support.
* Operate at the front edge of releases: coordinate complex, multi‑track launches; manage timelines, dependencies, and communications; and ensure every release is impactful, predictable, and well‑understood across the company.
* Solve problems with AI: leverage AI tools and analytics to uncover insights, automate workflows, and build or define agents that help teams scale decision‑making, triage issues, and learn faster from customers.
* Translate signal into action: synthesize data, feedback, and operational insights into clear narratives that shape roadmaps, priorities, and product strategy.
* Drive product excellence: partner with Product to define success metrics, track adoption and product health, and recommend improvements grounded in both qualitative and quantitative insights.
* Design scalable systems: build and continuously improve processes for release management, incident handling, feedback loops, and cross‑functional execution.
* Lead through ambiguity: manage high‑severity incidents and escalations, advocate for tech‑debt reduction, and help teams focus on the highest‑impact problems.


**What We’re Looking For**
* A strong generalist with 2–6 years of experience, ideally with a background in Computer Science, Business, Data, or similar — and a deep interest in product, systems thinking, and how organizations scale.
* Demonstrated ability to manage complex stakeholder landscapes, influence without authority, and drive alignment across multiple teams.
* Comfort working with data and AI‑powered tools: experience with SQL and analytics platforms is a nice to have, but curiosity about using AI agents and automation to solve operational problems is a must have.
* A builder’s mindset: you enjoy designing processes from scratch, iterating quickly, and improving systems over time.
* Clear, confident communicator who can turn messy inputs into structured plans, decisions, and narratives.
* Thrives in ambiguity, moves fast with a bias for action, and cares deeply about making teams and products more effective.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Copywriter
description:
At Precis, we are a group of friendly experts in media buying, analytics & marketing science, creative and technology with a joint mission to help great companies thrive in the digital landscape.
   

  

 Are you curious to learn more about us? Read more about us here. For insights into what life is like at Precis and a peek at the perks of working with us, visit this page. In our Handbook, you can get a neat summary of who we are, our culture and our offices.
 **In** 
**this document** 
**, we have summarised what our recruitment process looks like. Take a look!**
**Join Our Team as a Dedicated Copywriter for a Leading Automotive Brand**
 We are seeking a dedicated copywriter to own the copy for performance marketing for our clients. As part of our talented team, including Art Directors, Motion Designers, Developers, and Project Managers, your expertise will contribute to producing digital campaigns.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing 💼**
* Ensure high-quality copywriting for global productions.
* Collaborate with teams to ideate and develop creative concepts.
* Provide creative advisory for new concepts to ensure brand consistency.
* Manage copy for global productions, including copy administration and quality control.
* Oversee translations to ensure accurate and effective communication across different markets.
* Represent the agency in a client-facing position, building strong relationships and delivering exceptional support.
* Develop campaign concepts and ads across various digital channels and formats: paid social, display, programmatic, video, websites, etc.
* Develop a messaging hierarchy that aligns with clients' media strategy.


**What We Are Looking For ✔️**
* Minimum of 2 years of experience in copywriting or content marketing for digital channels.
* Native Swedish speaker, and proficient in English.
* Proven experience in writing for various digital channels and formats.
* Track record of delivering global digital projects.
* Strong ability to proofread and edit own work, ensuring high-quality deliverables.
* Excellent organizational skills to manage and prioritize multiple projects.
* Attention to detail to ensure consistency in messaging and tone of voice across various channels and formats.
* Proactive in seeking feedback and willing to make necessary revisions to improve the final output.
* Motivated to be part of developing the next generation of digital agency
* [Preferred] Bonus if you are familiar with Figma


**What We Offer🌟**
*You can read our full offering in our* 
*handbook* 
*.*
* An intense learning environment where you will be able to grow and develop your skills and passions alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.
* A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.
* Transparent salary setting - you can find it below the job description on our website!
* 30 days paid leave, flexible public holidays, parental pay, a solid pension scheme and key insurance. Our different offices also offer different perks such as massage, health care contributions, free book orders, healthy snacks & great coffee, EAP programs and your choice of PC or Mac.
* A diverse environment with a high focus on inclusion and belonging. We speak 46 native languages around Precis, and our internal inclusion score is 4.5 out of 5.


 To make things as smooth as possible, please ensure your CV is in English.
   

  

**We update all candidates on their applications by email. If you don't receive an email from us, please check your spam folder.**
*We are committed to building an inclusive and fair workplace that attracts talent and advances its people, regardless of their background, experiences or perspectives. We believe that a diverse set of minds will continue to contribute to our innovative culture and our ability to reinvent ourselves constantly.*

title: Senior Business System Manager
description:
🌀 The Company
   

  

 Filigran, founded in October 2022, stands out in the cybertech ecosystem for its commitment to revolutionizing cyber threat management with a proactive approach. Its mission is to develop innovative open-source solutions designed to anticipate cyber threats, identify security gaps, and strengthen organizational security posture.
   

  

 Filigran solutions are now trusted by over 6,000 public and private organizations worldwide.
   

  

 🎯 The Role
   

  

 As
 **Business Systems Administrator,** 
 you will administer and optimize our Hubspot instance. You will be the go-to expert for Hubspot ensuring it is well-configured, well-integrated, and effectively adopted across teams. This is a high-impact, cross-functional role sitting at the heart of Sales, People, and Finance operations.
   

  

 💼 Your Responsibilities
   

  

**Systems Administration & Ownership**
* Own day-to-day administration, configuration, and optimization of HubSpot.
* Maintain data integrity, user access, workflows, and integrations with Hubspot.
* Serve as the primary point of contact for vendor relationships and support escalations.
* Partner with Operational stakeholders to map business processes to system capabilities.
* Document configurations, workflows, and training materials as needed.


**Reporting & Data Quality**
* Clean and organize data (historical and current/future) in Hubspot.
* Define and enforce data governance standards across Hubspot.
* Proactively identify and resolve data inconsistencies.


**Stakeholder Enablement**
* Train and support end-users across teams on system usage and best practices.
* Gather business requirements and translate them into Hubspot configurations.
* Act as a bridge between business teams and technical resources.


 🤝 Who You’ll Work With
   

  

 You will report to the Director of Revenue and Finance Operations. You will work closely with the VP of Strategy, Revenue Ops and IT Ops.
   

  

 🧬 Profile We’re Looking For
   

  

* 3–5+ years of experience in a Business Systems, Revenue Operations, or Systems Administrator role
* Hands-on experience with HubSpot (CRM administration, workflows, pipelines, reporting)
* Experience with another core tool (HRIS or ERP) is a plus.
* Strong attention to data quality and process design
* Ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously
* Strong interest in AI and its applications in cybersecurity
* Comfortable working with and leveraging AI-powered tools (LLMs, automation, copilots, etc.) to improve workflows and productivity
* Comfortable working in a remote, async-first environment
* Autonomy, ownership, and team spirit
* Excellent communication skills — you can translate technical concepts for non-technical audiences and vice-versa
* Fluent in English


 🌱
 **Why Join Filigran? More than just a job.**
 We’re a fast-growing, global, and fully remote company building open-source cybersecurity solutions, increasingly powered by AI, to help defense teams anticipate threats and act faster.
   

  

 ⭐
 **What we believe**
 We believe we do work that matters, uniting defenders into a global community to make security more open, resilient & collaborative.
   

  

 💻
 **How we work**
 We do work that matters by combining strong engineering standards with emerging technologies, including AI, to move faster and smarter.
   

  

 🧭
 **What guides us**
 We make our work matter by building a culture grounded in our CORE values of Cohesion, Openness, Responsibility, and Equity. The principles that guide how we make decisions, treat people, and grow together, especially when no one’s watching.
   

  

**💰 Compensation & Benefits**
* Competitive pay + equity - everyone shares in our success
* Remote-first, flexible, and balanced - work that fits your life
* Your setup, your choice - pick the gear that works for you
* Twice-a-year gatherings - we meet in person for regional and global offsites to connect, collaborate, and strengthen our culture beyond the screen


**🌍 Equal Employment Opportunity**
**We enable cybersecurity through inclusion - from code to culture.**
 At Filigran, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe diversity of our people make our products and our team stronger. We welcome talent of every background, identity, and lived experience, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or veteran status.
   

  

 What matters here is what you bring, not what you look like, where you’re from, or how you identify.
   

  

**🚀 Ready to Join Us?**
 Apply now and help us build the future of the cybersecurity ecosystem, together.

title: Software Engineer - Internal Tooling
description:
**Build something monumental for Healthcare!**
 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You care about making systems work where it really matters, behind the scenes, where complexity tends to hide. At your previous role, you were likely drawn to problems that others avoided: messy logic, unclear ownership, and workflows that didn’t quite hold together. You enjoy bringing structure to that - building systems that are not just functional, but reliable and easy to reason about.
   

  

 At Tandem, you’ll work at the intersection of product, operations, and business logic, owning and building the internal systems that power how we operate at scale. A key focus for this role is
 **billing and invoicing** 
 , where product usage, access, and real-world workflows need to translate into correct, auditable invoices.
   

  

 This is not just about payments, it’s about deeply understanding how our product is used in practice, how that maps to our business model, and building the systems that make it all work seamlessly. You’ll work closely with engineering, operations, and commercial teams to ensure that what we build internally is robust, scalable, and aligned with how Tandem grows.
   

  

 If you enjoy working close to the business, taking ownership of complex logic, and building systems that directly impact how a company runs - this is your kind of role.
   

  

 What You Will Do
   

  

* Own and build Tandem’s internal tooling and operational systems, with a primary focus on billing and invoicing.
* Design and implement systems that connect product usage and access control to invoicing logic (e.g. integrations with Stripe).
* Build workflows that ensure invoices are generated correctly based on real-world product behavior and customer setups.
* Develop and maintain integrations between internal systems, databases, and external platforms (e.g. billing, CRM, analytics).
* Work closely with operations and commercial teams to understand business needs and translate them into robust technical solutions.
* Improve and automate internal processes to reduce manual work and increase reliability.
* Build internal APIs and services that support operational workflows and scale with the company.
* Take end-to-end ownership, from problem definition and system design to implementation, monitoring, and iteration.


 What You Bring
   

  

* Experience as a software engineer working on backend systems or internal tooling.
* Strong ability to model and implement business logic in code, especially in complex or evolving domains.
* Experience working with APIs, integrations, and data flows between systems.
* Familiarity with cloud environments (e.g. AWS, Azure, or similar).
* A pragmatic mindset - you focus on building systems that actually work in practice.
* Interest in working close to the business and understanding how technical systems map to real-world operations.
* Strong ownership and the ability to drive work independently in a fast-moving environment.


 Bonus Points
   

  

* Experience working with billing, invoicing, or financial systems (e.g. Stripe or similar platforms).
* Experience building internal tools or backoffice systems.
* Familiarity with data modeling for usage-based systems.
* Experience applying automation or AI to operational workflows
* Experience in regulated environments (healthcare, fintech, etc.).


**Location**
 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

 Our interview process consists of 4 stages:
   

  

* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interview with some of our Engineers
* Interview with our CTO & Co-Founder
* Home case and presentation at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch, and a presentation to experience our our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.


**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 25 days/year of paid vacation
* Pension
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag)
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


**Culture at Tandem**
 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*

title: Work Abroad and Enjoy the Summer
description:
Live by the sea. Work with an international team. Build your future abroad.
   

  

 We're currently hiring multilingual Customer Support Specialists and Content Moderators for exciting opportunities in Greece and Portugal — with both remote and on-site positions available within the hiring country.
   

  

 If you speak one of the following languages, we'd love to hear from you:
   

  

 Dutch, French, German, Italian, Polish, Norwegian, Czech, Portuguese, Croatian, Spanish, Swedish, Finnish, or Danish.
   

  

 What We Offer
   

  

 Competitive salary + monthly performance bonuses
   

  

 Paid relocation package (flight + hotel accommodation)
   

  

 Welcome bonus
   

  

 Housing support & full relocation assistance
   

  

 International and inclusive work environment
   

  

 Career growth opportunities abroad
   

  

 The chance to live near some of Europe's most beautiful beaches and coastal cities
   

  

 Your Responsibilities
   

  

 Provide customer support via phone, chat, and email
   

  

 Assist customers efficiently and professionally
   

  

 Review and moderate online content when required
   

  

 Deliver a high-quality customer experience
   

  

 Collaborate with an international team
   

  

**Requirements**
 Good English skills + one of the listed languages
   

  

 EU citizenship or a valid work/residency permit for Greece or Portugal
   

  

 This is more than just a summer job — it's an opportunity to gain international experience, meet people from around the world, and enjoy life abroad.
   

  

 Apply now and start your next adventure.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 As an
 **Engineering Manager** 
 at Legora, you’ll lead by example—hands-on in the code while guiding a talented team of engineers. You’ll architect and deliver features that make legal professionals’ lives easier, drive technical excellence, and help shape a product used by forward-thinking law firms around the world. This is a role for someone who loves to build, mentor, and scale both people and systems.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Design and implement end-to-end features across our fullstack platform
* Contribute directly to the codebase while supporting the growth of your team
* Lead architecture discussions and technical decision-making
* Ensure high standards in code quality, security, and performance
* Collaborate closely with Product and Design to ship value quickly and reliably
* Review code, give feedback, and mentor engineers at all levels
* Champion best practices in development, DevOps, and continuous improvement
* Work with Node.js, React, Azure, AWS and Kubernetes
* Work every day to ensure your team is healthy and efficient


**Who You Are**
- A seasoned fullstack or backend engineer with 1-3 years of leadership experience

- Comfortable being hands-on while also setting technical direction

- Skilled in building scalable products and owning complex systems end-to-end

- Strong communicator who leads with empathy and clarity

- Passionate about mentoring others and helping teams grow

- Confident working across backend, frontend, and infrastructure

- Curious, pragmatic, and always looking for smarter ways to build and deliver

- But most importantly, you are someone who:

* “Gets stuff done” and understands building a successful company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
* Proactively and independently solves the most burning problems
* Strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are few
* Candidates will go through the same interview process as software engineering candidates (including a coding task and a technical interview), with additional interview steps at the end designed for the engineering manager role.
* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Investment Banking - Nordic Coverage - Analyst - Stockholm
description:
**Job Description**
 This is an exciting opportunity for someone who is motivated to work in a dynamic team and to contribute to our Investment Banking business.
   

  

 J.P. Morgan's Nordic Team is part of the Nordics Investment Banking franchise and based in Stockholm. It has a sound reputation with an exemplary track record and is recognised by clients, other advisors and competitors as one of the leading franchises in the Nordic market.
   

  

 As an Investment Banking Analyst in the Nordic team, you will work on the origination and execution of M&A and broader corporate finance transactions, covering a variety of different industry sectors. You will play a critical role in implementing the execution function including working with analysts and associates, as well as assisting more senior bankers. You will develop exceptional function skills in the major areas of investment banking and will demonstrate the ability to become a senior officer who is capable of generating and executing your own transactions.
   

  

 Joining here means you will become part of our inclusive teams and culture, unified by the firm’s mission and commitment to success.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Perform complex financial valuation, discounted cash flow and multiples-based research
* Prepare presentation books, management presentations, Board materials, Memorandums, and other presentation materials for use in M&A and capital markets transactions or strategic client dialogue
* Execute transaction (M&A / equity capital markets / debt capital markets): support due diligence, document preparation, negotiation etc and liaising with relevant counterpart at the client/ other advisers (accounting, legal etc.)
* Interact with senior team members and client management teams on a daily basis to execute transactions, discuss strategic alternatives and market condition
* Engage with and maintain regular dialogue with clients and potential clients
* Collaborate with J.P. Morgan industry and product teams
* Provide mentorship and supervision of projects


**Required Qualifications, Capabilities And Skills**
* Fluent in Nordic language skills
* Relevant work experience at international investment bank (M&A, industry teams, Leverage Finance), consulting firm, private equity, Big 4, or similar
* Proven academic background
* Proficient financial analysis and modelling skills
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to work well under pressure and tight deadlines
* Solid communication and project management skills
* M&A experience is highly preferred
* In-depth understanding of investment banking products
* Experience in dealing with clients and other transactional advisers
* Proficient in MS office applications


**ABOUT US**
 J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.
   

  

 We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.
   

  

**About The Team**
 J.P. Morgan's Global Banking business is one of the largest wholesale banking client franchises in the world. We serve clients, including corporations, governments, states, municipalities, healthcare organizations, education institutions, banks and investors.
   

  

 Global Investment Banking supports a broad range of corporations, institutions and governments by providing strategic advice, capital raising and risk management expertise.

title: Security Engineer (Detection & Response)
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 At Legora, we prioritise security. Always.
   

  

 We are looking for exceptional Detection & Response engineers to help us detect, investigate, and respond to threats as they continue to evolve. In this role you will have the opportunity to shape our technical security capabilities from the ground up and set the president for what modern threat detection and response practices should look like.
   

  

* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What You Will Be Doing**
* Lead Legora's cyber security incident response efforts across any domain from external attacks to insider threats.
* Develop and manage tooling to enhance detection, investigation and response efforts.
* Develop and maintain detection capabilities, playbooks and workflows to quickly identify, respond to, and remediate potential incidents.
* Drive continuous improvement, based on relevant incident response metrics.
* Coordinate with teams across the whole organization to plan for, and resolve incidents.


**Who You Are**
* You have 3+ years of software engineering with strong security drive, and/or
* 5+ years of detection engineering, incident response or threat hunting experience
* Expert knowledge and understanding of cloud based environments and operations
* Strong self-drive and motivated to build best-in-class solutions
* Exceptional team-player thriving on cross-team collaboration
* Experience working in a high-paced cloud-native scale-up environment
* Knowledge of modern threat detection technologies


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Content Writer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 The role
   

  

 We are looking for a Content Writer who can make Legora's communication stand out. This is a writing role first and foremost for someone who loves the craft, has a strong editorial voice, and can turn complex ideas into clear, sharp, human writing that cuts through.
   

  

 You will work at the intersection of Brand, Product Marketing and International Marketing, translating product capabilities and market context into compelling copy across client-facing touchpoints, campaigns and content. You will write a lot, across formats and contexts, and you will care deeply about every word.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who gets energy from writing: from finding the right angle, the right tone, the right words, and who can do that consistently at pace and to a highest standard.
   

  

 The responsibilities
   

  

**Writing & copy.** 
 Produce high-quality writing across formats, client communications, campaign copy, web, social, long-form content and more. Own the words from first draft to final output.
   

  

**Client communication.** 
 Write and elevate client-facing communication across touchpoints. Clear, warm, sharp and always on brand. Fast turnarounds without sacrificing quality.
   

  

**Tone & style.** 
 Bring Legora's voice to life consistently, human-first, distinctive and free of AI sameness. Push back when writing doesn't meet the bar.
   

  

**Storytelling.** 
 Turn product complexity and market context into narratives that are easy to read and hard to forget. Make the complicated feel clear and the dry feel interesting.
   

  

**Editorial quality.** 
 Uphold a high bar across everything that goes out. Edit and refine your own work and contribute to raising the standard across the team.
   

  

**Playbooks & guidelines.** 
 Contribute to writing guidelines and tone of voice documentation that help the wider team write consistently and well.
   

  

**Cross-functional collaboration.** 
 Work closely with Product Marketing and International Marketing to ensure copy is accurate, relevant and adapted where needed for different markets and audiences.
   

  

**AI-enabled workflows.** 
 Use AI tools to speed up drafting and iteration, but always bring your own voice, judgment and editorial instinct to the final output.
   

  

 What we are looking for
   

  

 You are an exceptional writer. That is the most important thing. You have a strong, clear voice, sharp instincts for tone, and the ability to adapt your writing to different contexts without losing quality or distinctiveness.
   

  

 You have a background in journalism, copywriting or editorial work, and are comfortable writing quickly and well under pressure.
   

  

 You are a prolific writer who takes pride in output, you produce a lot, you edit ruthlessly, and you are never satisfied with good enough.
   

  

 You are curious and deeply interested in language: in finding the right word, the right rhythm, the right angle. You read widely and it shows in your writing.
   

  

 You are collaborative and receptive to feedback, and comfortable working closely with brand, product and marketing teams.
   

  

 You are organised and able to manage multiple briefs at once, hitting deadlines without letting quality slip.
   

  

 Experience and background
   

  

**Experience.** 
 5+ years in writing, journalism, copywriting or editorial roles, ideally within tech, SaaS or a complex domain where clarity really matters.
   

  

**Writing portfolio.** 
 A strong body of work that demonstrates range, voice and the ability to make complex topics accessible and engaging.
   

  

**Journalistic foundation.** 
 Trained or experienced in journalism or editorial work, you know how to research, structure an argument and write to deadline.
   

  

**Tone & voice.** 
 Demonstrated ability to develop and maintain a distinctive tone across different formats and audiences.
   

  

**AI-native.** 
 You use AI tools to work faster, but your voice and editorial judgment are always in the driver's seat.
   

  

**Collaboration.** 
 Comfortable working cross-functionally, taking briefs from different teams and turning them into great writing.
   

  

**Ownership.** 
 You take a brief and run with it. You don't need hand-holding to produce excellent work.
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Talent Acquisition Partner [Fixed Term]
description:
Avalanche Studios Group are now looking for a Talent Acquisition Partner to join the People team on a temporary basis.
   

  

 You will partner up with managers across our organization to understand their hiring needs and team dynamics to build and scale our teams so we can support our projects in the best way possible. You will play a role in improving our TA processes and ways of working, collaborate with stakeholders and peers and contribute to our employer brand.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Own full-cycle recruitment processes across the entire organization.
* Collaborate with our People Partners and stakeholders to identify short and long-term hiring needs
* Managing communication with candidates and ensuring a strong candidate experience at all times
* Source directly and work proactively to build diverse, and long term, candidate pipelines
* Collaborate and share knowledge and practices with the Talent Acquisition and HR team
* Stay close to gaming industry trends in recruiting, compensation, and market dynamics to sharpen our hiring strategy


**Who You Are**
* Broad experience from recruiting for different disciplines within tech, bonus if this is within gaming.
* Experience of recruiting for roles that are required within game development, or a curiosity for the industry and the desire to gain a deeper understanding.
* Ability to understand specific role needs and source proactively to find the best talent for each position.
* You have an interest in best TA practices and enjoy looking for smarter ways to work that will help the teams reach their full potential.
* Strong drive, sense of ownership and a proactive mindset.
* Solid experience in partnering effectively with different teams and a variety of seniorities.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.


 Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.
   

  

**The Stockholm location**
 Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
   

  

**Our values**
 At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 To apply for this position, please register below and provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
   

  

 At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.

title: CEO - Richter Life Science
description:
*Please note: This role is with Richter Life Science. Norrsken Launcher is supporting the hiring process, but the successful candidate will be employed directly by Richter Life Science.*
****Join a world-class team of scientists and quickly scale a solution already used by 100s of the world’s leading universities to new industrial applications and processes. Build a unicorn with global impact.****
**About Richter Life Science**
 At Richter Life Science (RLS), we are making the invisible visible.
   

  

 Every major industry - healthcare, manufacturing, food, materials - depends on understanding what’s happening at a microscopic level. Yet much of that world remains invisible and impossible to monitor in real time.
   

  

 We are building a new category of sensors that change that. Our technology, Optotracing, uses fluorescent molecules that light up when they bind to specific targets - turning complex biological and material structures into measurable signals in real time. What once required complex lab work or guesswork can now be directly seen and acted upon.
   

  

****Why this matters****
* We’re still blind to the structures that drive some of the biggest problems in healthcare - biofilms - which make bacterial infections up to 1,000x more resistant to antibiotics. Without visibility, we can’t diagnose them or treat them effectively.
* The same blind spot exists in industry. From pulp & paper to biomanufacturing, critical processes are controlled without seeing what’s actually happening at the material level - leading to lost yield, higher costs, and suboptimal decisions.


 RLS was founded by world-leading Professor Agneta Richter-Dahlfors at Karolinska Institutet, built on decades of breakthrough research and patented technology. Today, the product is used by hundreds of organizations globally - from leading research institutions to industrial players - through our commercial arm Ebba Biotech. Backed by investors including Norrsken Launcher, RLS is built on the belief that the world becomes healthier, more efficient, and more sustainable when we can finally see what is hidden in plain sight.
   

  

****The opportunity ahead****
 Our ambition is to build a new global standard for how the chemistry of biological structures are measured, understood, and optimized. We are redefining how the world interacts with the microscopic layer - enabling real-time insight into structures that drive outcomes across healthcare and industry.
   

  

 With the technology proven in academia and early industrial use, and 650+ customers already generating recurring, high-margin revenue, we have a foundation that is commercial, repeatable, and defensible.
   

  

 The next 24 months are about scaling that foundation into a category-defining global company - building a category defining commercial organization, growing the existing business across academic and CRO segments, securing flagship industrial partnerships (e.g. pulp & paper), and developing the licensing track in medtech, starting with biofilm and AMR diagnostics. Beyond this lies broader optionality across food, agriculture, embedded biosensing, and a future software and data layer.
   

  

****Product****
 Optotracers are a platform technology: a single chemistry unlocking multiple high-impact verticals.
   

  

* Commercial today: Research reagents - Optotracers sold globally through Ebba Biotech, used in applications from biofilm research to neurodegeneration and biomass analytics - with fast turnaround and a structural cost advantage over legacy methods.
* Scaling now: Industrial process analytics - Real-time, fiber-level analysis of lignin and cellulose for process optimization in industries like pulp & paper. Validated in paid projects, paired with off-the-shelf instruments and a software layer for mills. Our route to scale runs through OEM partnerships.
* Next play: Medtech & diagnostics - A licensing model around biofilm detection, antibiotic susceptibility testing, and amyloid imaging - built for partners in diagnostics, pharma, and clinical research.
* Future platform plays: Embedded biosensing, food safety and shelf-life monitoring, and a broader reagents-plus-instruments-plus-data platform across multiple verticals.


****The role****
 Our founder, Professor Agneta Richter-Dahlfors, is transitioning to a dedicated scientific and technical role to focus on the research she has spent decades pioneering. We are seeking a CEO to lead RLS into its next chapter - turning proven technology and early traction into a focused, scalable commercial business.
   

  

 You will inherit a company with real customers, revenue, IP, and partners. What it needs now is focus, a commercial organization, and the discipline to translate scientific breakthroughs into repeatable revenue. As CEO, you will have full accountability for execution and performance, working closely with Agneta, the Ebba Biotech team, and the Board to realize RLS’s full potential.
   

  

**Core responsibilities:**
* Set strategy and own P&L: Choose where to play across research-tools, industrial, and medtech. Define strategy, priorities and targets. Own results, including unit economics and gross margin discipline.
* Sharpen focus: Turn a broad platform into clear priorities, sequencing, and a focused go-to-market strategy.
* Lead and scale the organization: Build the leadership team, hire key roles, and create a high-performance culture across the RLS group.
* Drive commercial growth: Scale the existing business, win flagship industrial customers and OEM partnerships, and convert paid testing into recurring revenue.
* Build the medtech licensing track: Translate the IP and clinical-research validation into licensing partnerships with diagnostics and pharma players.
* Fundraise and manage governance: Lead the next financing rounds and maintain strong Board and investor communication.
* Steward IP and partnerships: Work closely with the founder Agneta and counsel to expand the patent estate, and with OEMs and clinical partners to embed RLS into their workflows.


****Candidate profile****
**Essential experience:**
* A proven track record of scaling an early-stage commercial team into a structured, high-performing international company.
* Hands-on experience building, mentoring and leading cross-functional teams.
* Real comfort selling complex, science-heavy products in B2B / B2B2B environments where ROI-led value selling and long enterprise cycles are the norm.


**Preferred experience:**
* Background in life sciences, diagnostics, industrial analytics, or other deeply technical product categories.
* Experience structuring OEM partnerships, licensing deals, or platform-style monetization across multiple verticals.
* Success fundraising with institutional venture capital and working with boards.
* Previous experience navigating the transition from a founder-led team to a professionalized organization.


**Leadership style & values:**
* High ownership: You take full responsibility for outcomes end-to-end. You communicate risks early, solve problems proactively, and always "close the loop."
* Humble with a growth mindset: You are a lifelong learner who seeks feedback and admits mistakes quickly. You consistently prioritize the team and the mission over your own ego.
* Structure: You bring clarity to the organization. You implement goals, metrics, and the lightweight processes necessary to scale in a fast-paced environment.
* Integrity & courage: You challenge assumptions respectfully and speak up when it matters, ensuring the company stays aligned with its long-term strategy.
* Long-term team builder: You elevate others, invest deeply in relationships, and optimize for sustainable, long-term wins rather than short-term shortcuts.
* Communication: You communicate to create alignment, not just share information. You explain the “why,” turn goals into clear next steps, and default to transparency. In writing and in meetings, you make sure the decision is clear, ownership is explicit, and everyone knows what success looks like next.


****Success in 6-12 months looks like:****
* Focus established: A clear, board-aligned strategy on which verticals RLS owns first, with the resourcing to win them.
* Commercial operations at scale: A professionalized commercial engine driving step-change growth in research-tools revenue across academia and CROs.
* Industrial pilots converted: Flagship pulp & paper deployments live, an OEM commercial agreement signed, and a credible path to multi-mill rollout.
* Medtech track underway: Patent estate strengthened and first licensing conversations with diagnostics or pharma partners progressing toward agreement.
* Funded and resourced: Next round successfully raised, with a fully recruited leadership team in place.


****What we offer****
* The chance to lead a company with a 1:1 link between commercial success and global impact.
* A collaborative partnership with a world-class scientific founder who is ready to let you lead.
* Competitive salary and a significant equity incentive scheme
* Centrally located offices in Stockholm.


****How to apply****
 We are reviewing applications continuously. We welcome international candidates who hold or are eligible for a Swedish work permit. Swedish language skills not required.
   

  

 This is a full-time position based in Stockholm, Sweden.
   

  

 For questions about the role and process, please reach out to:
   

  

 Emma Kjellander (Norrsken Launcher) / emma@launcher.vc

title: Product Engineer — Payments Team
description:
Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.
 



 We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.
 



  





 The role
 



 Mentimeter is looking for a Product Engineer to join our Payments team and help shape how customers pay for and manage their subscriptions.
 



 The Payments team owns the checkout experience, billing systems, subscription lifecycle, and integrations with payment providers like Stripe. We work on reducing friction in critical customer journeys - from initial signup through renewal and cancellation. We're also thinking bigger: exploring more flexible billing models, improving how we handle invoicing and financial reporting, and building the infrastructure to support Mentimeter's growth.
 



 The technical landscape is distributed systems, event-driven architecture, and complex integrations. We partner closely with Product, Finance, Sales, and Support to balance customer experience with business requirements. This is a team that works together -pairing, mobbing, and bringing diverse perspectives to problems that matter.
 



 You'll have a genuine chance to influence Mentimeter's payments strategy and help define how hundreds of thousands of customers experience billing at our platform.
 



  





 The Team
 



 The Payments team is part of our Capabilities area, which builds critical platform experiences. We're a close-knit group: a product manager, an engineering manager, and backend-focused engineers who care about getting the details right. We work across the entire stack, but most of our work is backend, distributed systems, integrations, and data pipelines.
 



 We're collaborative by nature. We discuss big decisions openly, challenge ideas respectfully, and support each other. We're not a ticket-pushing team. We own outcomes, think about trade-offs, and take initiative to improve what we've built. We're also learning together - experimenting with AI adoption, refining how we work, and building skills we didn't have yesterday.
 



  





 What you'll do
 


* Design, build, and improve payment and billing experiences that reduce friction and delight customers
* Work on subscription lifecycle systems, pricing logic, and invoicing pipelines
* Integrate and maintain relationships with payment providers - understanding their APIs, limitations, and cost models
* Build systems that handle distributed transactions reliably, with observability and fault tolerance
* Collaborate across teams - understanding what Finance needs, what Sales is selling, what Support is hearing from customers
* Think about how we evolve our billing capabilities - moving beyond our current patterns to support new pricing models and business requirements
* Take initiative on projects that improve our systems, domain knowledge, or team practices
* Stay curious about payments, distributed systems, and AI adoption in software development



  





 Who you are
 



 You're comfortable working across the backend stack and think in terms of systems and trade-offs. You have real experience shipping backend services to production - you understand latency, observability, and knowing what to test.
 



 You're curious by nature. You ask good questions, learn quickly, and aren't intimidated by domains you haven't worked in before. You have opinions, you share them respectfully, and you're willing to be challenged in return.
 



  





 You think about outcomes, not tasks. You can take a fuzzy problem, break it down, and drive it to completion. You work well with others - you enjoy pairing, you communicate clearly, and you can represent the team in cross-functional conversations.
 



  





 This is a full-stack product engineering role with a heavy emphasis on the backend. The primary responsibilities will involve backend development in Ruby.
 



  





 The successful candidate will also contribute to and own frontend features. This is a perfect fit for an engineer who is passionate about scalable systems and comfortable working across the entire tech stack, while maintaining a main focus on backend architecture.
 



  





 It's a big plus if you have
 


* Experience with payments, billing, subscriptions, or Stripe - you understand the domain and its quirks
* Experience building systems that integrate with external APIs and handle eventual consistency
* Familiarity with distributed systems thinking - understanding event-driven architecture, idempotency, and failure modes
* Some infrastructure or platform thinking - you've thought about observability, monitoring, and operational excellence
* Experience working on products with internal stakeholders - Finance, Sales, Support - and understanding their needs
* Comfort pairing, mobbing, or working in collaborative code sessions
* Interest in AI adoption and how AI tools can improve our development process



  





 It's okay if you don't have
 


* Specific experience with payments or Stripe (domain knowledge is learnable)
* Deep infrastructure expertise (curiosity and willingness to learn matter more)
* Ruby experience (we value engineers who are problem solvers and have a growth mindset)



  





**How We Work With AI** 




 We're actively building AI into how we build software - and as part of the product itself. Every engineer has access to tools like Cursor, Claude, and Codex, and we encourage you to use them to pair on tricky problems, explore unfamiliar codebases and to prototype and experiment. We're not prescriptive about how we use them, but we are curious about what works and what comes next in how products get built. Our teams regularly share workflows, and lessons learned, and we treat AI adoption and fluency as a skill we're all developing together - both in
 *how* 
 we build and in
 *what* 
 we build for our customers.
 



  





**What Mentimeter Can Offer** 




 At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
 



 All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page
 



  





**AI and Hiring at Mentimeter** 




 At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
 


* AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
* There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
* Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.



 AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.
 



  





**Culture at Mentimeter** 




 At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
 



 Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
 



 Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

title: Executive Assistant - Stockholm
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 Our Executive Assistant function is part of our CEO Office to drive ultimate operational alignment across Legora. In this newly structured role, you will report directly to the incoming Chief of Staff to our CEO. Your day-to-day focus will be providing dedicated, high-velocity support to multiple VPs within the organization or one CXO.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Manage complex support across multiple leaders: Own calendar, travel logistics, and email for Executive and several VPs. You won't just manage their time; you will design it to reflect shifting priorities and strategic focus, using AI to optimize their productivity and your own workflow.
* Coordinate internal and external meetings across multiple time zones - making sure every touchpoint is prepared, purposeful and followed up
* Lead planning and execution of key team moments - including recurring meetings, offsites, workshops and informal gatherings.
* Triage inbound requests, protect focus time and help communication flow smoothly across the team.
* Partnering closely with one or more stakeholders leads to flag blockers, support decision-making and help the team stay one step ahead.
* Provide operational support to keep projects, initiatives and team collaboration on track.
* Take initiative to improve the way the team operates - spotting friction points and building better systems.
* Review expense reports of those you support and their direct reports
* Book travel for those you support as needed using our travel management system


**What You Bring**
* A proactive, problem-solving mindset - you act before issues arise and take full ownership.
* Experience from a technology and/or scale-up start-up environment
* Deep attention to detail and a love of structure - you make things faster, smoother, clearer.
* Strong communication skills, confident in handling sensitive or high-stakes communication.
* High discretion and maturity - you know how to navigate different stakeholders and build trust.
* Comfort with tools like Google Workspace, Slack and Notion - and a quick learner when new systems come in.
* A genuine desire to support others and make the team better - you take pride in the work behind the scenes.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done.
