* har ett genuint intresse för cybersäkerhetsområdet och håller dig uppdaterad inom teknik och nyheter från omvärlden.


 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Övrigt
   

  

 En tjänst, tillsvidare, heltid, med tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Provanställning kan bli aktuellt. Försäkringskassan kan komma att göra viss ekonomisk personkontroll innan anställning. För den här tjänsten krävs svenskt medborgarskap
 **.** 
 I den här rekryteringen kan arbetspsykologiska test användas som en del i urvalsarbetet. Placeringsort: Sundsvall, Östersund, Stockholm, Karlskrona eller Trelleborg.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om Försäkringskassan som arbetsgivare? Besök fk.se/jobbahososs
   

  

 Kontaktpersoner
   

  

 Chef: Greger Älgemon (för frågor om tjänsten). HR-specialist: Ahmad Mohsen, 010-113 45 24, ahmad.mohsen@forsakringskassan.se (för frågor om rekryteringsprocessen). Fackliga representanter är för ST Magnus Näslund, Saco-S Amelie Johansson, Seko Eliza Grundström. Dessa nås via vår växel 08-786 90 00.
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 24 maj. Ladda gärna upp dina examensbevis, betyg och intyg i samband med din ansökan.
   

  

 Har du skyddade personuppgifter eller ingen tillgång till bank-id ringer du den HR-specialist som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.
   

  

 Vi ska i allt vi gör, agera med utgångspunkt i principen om alla människors lika värde, rättssäkerhet och med god service.

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 As an Account Executive SMB at Legora, you will play a key role in driving our growth by managing the full sales cycle — from qualifying leads to running demos and closing deals. You’ll engage inbound prospects, nurture relationships, and collaborate closely with Sales and Marketing to align on campaigns and optimize conversion.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Own inbound leads of prospects. Manage top of funnel conversion through qualification, demo, through close.
* Manage full sales cycles of opportunities. Run tight discovery calls and need assessments to evaluate fit against our ideal customer profile. Your job is to connect dots quickly and accurately.
* Nurture leads. Keep momentum alive with smart, value-driven follow-up that guides prospects through the early stages of the journey.
* Partner with Sales & Marketing. Align with campaign priorities and collaborate closely with GTM stakeholders to drive results
* Keep the pipeline clean. Log activity in Salesforce and enable clear forecasting and reporting.
* Surface market insight. Feedback what you hear on the frontlines to help us sharpen messaging, positioning, and targeting.
* Support events & campaigns. Engage leads pre- and post-event, helping make sure our campaigns generate real commercial outcomes.


**We’re Looking For**
* 3-4 years experience in a customer facing Sales or a Sales Associate role. Ideally in SaaS, tech, or financial services. A SDR, BDR background is beneficial.
* Familiarity with tools like Salesforce, Outreach, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator.
* Strong communication skills: persuasive, concise, and human in writing and in speech.
* Highly organized and comfortable managing multiple outreach streams at once.
* A curious, motivated learner with strong instincts for prioritization.
* Resilience and drive: you take feedback well, act fast, and don’t let a no slow you down.
* A collaborative mindset: you’re team-first and know how to hand off, sync up, and help others win.
* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What's In It For You**
* The opportunity to launch your GTM career at a legal AI company with true product–market fit.
* Direct exposure to senior sales leadership and the chance to learn by doing: fast.
* Structured onboarding, regular coaching, and clear paths for growth.
* A fast-moving environment where your insights shape how we scale.
* Competitive base salary, uncapped variable compensation, and equity upside.
* Centrally located offices in Stockholm, London, and NYC, designed as a space for you to do your life's work.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

description:

 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.

If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**About The Role**
*As a Partnerships Associate, you will help build and scale one of Legora’s most important growth channels: strategic partnerships. Your focus will be on developing and activating relationships with
 **key ecosystem partners and industry stakeholders** 
 to drive distribution and revenue growth.*
 This is a cross-functional role where you’ll work closely with GTM (sales), Marketing, and Legal Engineering to turn partnerships into tangible outcomes - from activation programs to closed deals.
   

  

 You’ll thrive in this role if you enjoy combining relationship-building with execution, are comfortable with ambiguity, and enjoy turning ideas into structured, repeatable initiatives. The role is well suited for someone who is proactive, commercially minded, and motivated by owning initiatives end-to-end in a fast-moving environment.
   

  

**What makes this role unique:** 
 you’ll work on an untapped growth channel with clear impact from day one, combining hands-on execution, cross-functional collaboration, and global relationship-building with real ownership of outcomes.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Identify, evaluate, and secure partnerships across legal networks and associations.
* Build and manage partner relationships, ensuring strong engagement and clear goals.
* Plan and execute partner initiatives, including pilots, webinars, and joint efforts.
* Work across teams to convert partnership opportunities into revenue.
* Represent Legora in partner interactions, including events and conferences.
* Contribute to improving how we build and scale partnerships.


**What You Bring**
* 1–3 years of experience in a faced paced and high-performance environment (e.g. scale-up, consulting, or similar).
* Strong execution skills and ability to drive projects with multiple stakeholders.
* High ownership and a proactive, solution-oriented mindset.
* Excellent communication skills in English (additional languages are a plus).
* Attention to detail with the ability to manage both strategic and operational work.
* Curiosity and ability to quickly understand new industries, partners, and customer needs


**What We Offer**
* At Legora, you’ll have the chance to build long-term, strategic relationships with some of the world’s leading law firms - working on truly impactful problems from day one.
* You’ll be part of a fast-scaling, entrepreneurial environment where your ideas matter and your growth is a priority.
* As an early member of the Partnerships team, your work will be visible, meaningful, and closely connected to both our clients and our product evolution.
* You’ll collaborate with senior leadership and cross-functional teams, helping shape not just customer outcomes but how we engage and support legal professionals across the globe.
* We offer a competitive salary, a high-upside equity program, and full support for relocation to our centrally located Stockholm office - designed for focus, energy, and great collaboration.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Sales Engineer (AI-Enabled)
description:
Evam builds mission-critical technology for the people who keep society safe. Our software is used by police, ambulance, fire & rescue, and other public safety organisations to manage complex, time-critical operations. Every day, Evam supports first responders handling hundreds of thousands of life-threatening incidents.
 



  





 We currently serve customers across the Nordics and the United Kingdom and have achieved 200%+ annual growth for the past three years. To support our continued expansion, we are now looking for a Sales Engineer based in Sweden to support our commercial activities on a global market.
 



  





 We are on a path to become the leading company in the public safety industry, and we are looking for people who want to be part of that journey and thrive in a high-paced environment. Our team will grow substantially in the coming years, offering you the opportunity to be part of something big from the very beginning.
 



  





 As a Sales Engineer at Evam, you will have access to the latest AI tools to support you in your role — from intelligent tender analysis to automated procurement workflows and technical sales support.
 



  





 Prior experience in the industry is not a requirement, and we encourage applications from candidates with diverse professional backgrounds.
 



  





**The Role:** 




  





 As a Sales Engineer at Evam, you will act as the technical counterpart to the commercial sales team throughout the sales process. You will help customers understand the value of Evam’s platform through demonstrations, technical discussions, solution design, and procurement responses.
 



  





 You will work closely with Sales Executives, Product Management, and Delivery teams to support enterprise sales opportunities across multiple markets. The role combines technical expertise with strong communication and presentation skills, requiring the ability to explain complex functionality in a clear, commercial, and customer-oriented way.
 



  





 You will also play a central role in developing and managing Evam’s internal AI tools used by the commercial organisation to improve sales workflows, tender management, and operational efficiency.
 



  





 The role supports Evam’s commercial activities globally and includes occasional international travel.
 



  





**What you will do:** 




  




* Support the commercial team in customer meetings and enterprise sales processes
* Deliver customer demonstrations tailored to operational workflows and customer needs
* Configure and maintain demo environments and technical sales setups
* Translate technical functionality into clear customer value and business impact
* Support public procurement processes by writing and coordinating tender responses
* Analyse inbound procurement opportunities and identify strategic fit
* Work closely with Product and Delivery teams to shape customer solutions
* Develop and improve Evam’s AI-powered sales and tender management tools
* Support technical evaluations, workshops, and proof-of-concept activities
* Act as a trusted technical advisor during complex enterprise sales cycles



  





**Who you are:** 




  





 You enjoy working in the intersection between technology, communication, and sales. You are comfortable presenting technical solutions to both operational users and senior stakeholders, and you understand how to adapt messaging depending on the audience.
 



  





 You are structured, analytical, and curious about customer workflows and operational challenges. Most importantly, you know how to use technical knowledge to support commercial outcomes.
 



  





**We believe you have:** 




  




* Experience in a technical pre-sales, sales engineering, solution consulting, or similar customer-facing technical role
* Experience presenting and demonstrating software solutions to customers
* Strong communication and presentation skills in English
* Ability to understand operational workflows and translate them into customer value
* Experience working with enterprise software, SaaS, or technically complex systems
* Experience writing or supporting bids and public procurement responses is considered a strong advantage
* Strong interest in AI tools, automation, and how AI can support commercial organisations
* A structured and analytical way of working
* A higher education degree, preferably in engineering, computer science, IT, or a related field



  





**Why Evam:** 




  





 At Evam, you will work on technology that directly impacts public safety and emergency response. You will join a fast-growing company, work with highly skilled colleagues, and play a key role in expanding Evam across the Nordic market.

title: Lead Engagement Manager SMB
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The role**
 Legora is scaling rapidly across EMEA, serving hundreds of small and mid-sized law firms and corporate accounts across the region. Collectively, this segment represents one of our fastest growing customer bases and the opportunity ahead is even larger. We're looking for a strategic operator who can define what best-in-class post-sale engagement looks like at scale, building a standardised, data-driven model that matches the size of the opportunity.
   

  

 As Lead Engagement Manager, SMB, you will have a rare opportunity to define and own a high-impact function, designing scalable, data-driven engagement models, automation, and repeatable processes that deliver consistent, high-quality outcomes across the full portfolio. You won’t just manage customer relationships; you will shape how we engage, retain, and grow one of our most important segments. The right person in this role will turn scale into a strategic advantage, building a model with measurable, demonstrable ROI that becomes a case study in how to do SMB engagement right.
   

  

 You will be a key driver of Legora’s post-sale strategy and a central influence on how we capture the full potential of our fastest-growing segment, delivering measurable, scalable impact across EMEA.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Own a growing SMB portfolio end-to-end, driving onboarding, adoption, retention, and expansion with real commercial accountability.
* Operationalize customer lifecycle insights, using behavioral and usage data to inform segmentation, prioritization, and engagement plays that improve activation, retention, and growth.
* Design and implement scalable processes and automation to serve a high-volume segment without compromising quality or time-to-value.
* Build and refine health scoring, engagement metrics, and operational dashboards that make outcomes predictable and measurable.
* Lead renewal and expansion conversations with confidence, bringing commercial sharpness to every customer interaction and identifying opportunities to deepen and grow accounts over time.
* Partner closely with cross-functional teams to ensure customer insights directly influence roadmap and go-to-market strategy.
* Help shape scalable post-sales processes that blend high-touch relationships with data-driven operational excellence, developing enablement materials and self-serve resources that drive consistent product adoption across the portfolio.
* Define and evolve the SMB Engagement playbook, laying the foundation for future hires you may eventually mentor or lead.


**Who You Are**
* Experienced in scaling customer-facing or post-sale functions within high-growth B2B SaaS environments, ideally with exposure to legal, professional services, or similarly complex enterprise workflows.
* A systems thinker who designs for leverage, prioritizing automation and process before adding headcount.
* Comfortable with data and technology, skilled at defining metrics, using tools effectively, and operating from structured dashboards.
* Confident in leading retention and expansion conversations with strong commercial instincts, skilled at engaging stakeholders across multiple levels of seniority and navigating objections with credibility and composure.
* Motivated by ownership and impact, eager to shape a function rather than inherit one.
* Open to relocating to Stockholm or currently based there, with a desire for close, in-person collaboration in a high-performance office environment.


**What We Offer**
* The opportunity to found and scale Legora’s post-sale engagement function in EMEA.
* A role with real ownership, visibility, and influence across product, go-to-market, and support functions.
* Collaboration with leadership and cross-functional teams to build systems that shape customer outcomes.
* A fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment where excellence is rewarded, and growth is exponential.
* Competitive compensation, meaningful equity participation, and a Stockholm office designed for focus and high performance.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Bilmekaniker / Service Technician - Upplands Väsby
description:
**What To Expect**
 At Tesla, our Service Technicians are the backbone of the Service operation, supporting our mission to building a world of amazing abundance.
   

  

 We’ve created one of the most innovative vehicles ever made, and as a Service Technician you will help ensure an equally innovative service to our customers. To succeed at Tesla, you must be energetic, highly organized, and smart working. You should have a passion for the brand and the ability to build on your technical skill set and thrive in a team environment.
   

  

 Are you up for the challenge?
   

  

 We offer:
   

- A dynamic & fast-paced environment where inclusion, learning & collaboration are key to success

- The chance to work with innovative technology, advanced tools and software

- Ongoing training and development to help you grow your skills and career

- A competitive compensation and benefits package

- A safe, clean and fun workplace

**What You'll Do**
 Service, repair and improve: Working with Tesla specific software and advanced tools you will be performing repairs and maintenance on all types of Tesla vehicles.
   

  

 Driven: Efficient in completing repairs and driven to exceed expectations on quality and productivity.
   

  

 Become proficient to perform all common repairs and some complex repairs autonomously
   

  

 Ensure top quality: Fix it right first time, we trust you to inspect our vehicles diligently before return and pre-delivery.
   

  

 Collaborate: Working across teams (local and over the region) you will be sharing support and best .practices in order to further improve the service experience.
   

  

 Develop: Setting you up for success our dedicated in-house training teams offer a blended learning
   

  

 strategy to help develop your technical skills. And by also sharing your own skills and knowledge with the team, you’re a part of driving the whole group forward.
   

  

**What You'll Bring**
 Technically experienced: You have professional experience performing vehicle service and repairs working within either automotive, aviation, marine or similar industries.
   

  

 Technically qualified: You have relevant certification to perform vehicle repairs in Sweden. You will self-manage your technical expertise development.
   

  

 A good communicator: You speak and write English well and know how to get your point across clearly and respectfully.
   

  

 Safe to drive: We require you to hold a driver’s license.
   

  

 Flexible: You may work in shifts to support your team, which may include weekends, morning and evening shifts. You are willing to travel when needed.
   

  

 Eligible: to work in Sweden.

title: Supplier Accountant
description:
We are looking for a driven Supplier Accountant. You will be part of the Accounts Payable team, which consists of 8 people divided into four teams in Sweden, Denmark, the UK and the Netherlands. Accounts Payable is part of Northern Europe's finance department, which has 65 people. The role will focus on Swedish properties (Mall of Scandinavia, Westfield Taby Centrum and Nacka Forum). The position is based in Stockholm and starts immediately.
 



  





**Key objectives** 




 To ensure accurate, timely, and efficient management of accounts payable processes for Swedish properties (Mall of Scandinavia, Westfield Täby Centrum, and Nacka Forum), while fostering strong supplier relationships, driving process harmonisation across Northern Europe, and contributing to the successful transition to SAP S/4 HANA. The Supplier Accountant will play a pivotal role in maintaining financial integrity, supporting operational excellence, and enabling continuous improvement within the regional finance team.
 



  





**What you will be doing…** 



* Manage accounts payable for assigned shopping centres or management companies
* Provide on‑site financial support by working regularly from our centres
* Record and reconcile bank transactions accurately
* Process invoices, including manual handling when required
* Support month‑end closing, reconciliations, and reporting
* Handle payment reminders and follow‑ups with suppliers
* Act as a key contact for supplier queries and relationships
* Contribute to process improvements and system development, including SAP S/4 HANA transition
* Participate in regional projects to harmonise accounts payable processes.



  





**What skills, experience, and knowledge will you need to be successful?** 



* Good knowledge of SAP (S4 / HANA)
* Good skills in Excel
* 3 - 5 years of relevant work experience
* Meticulous and detail-oriented with the ability to work towards deadlines
* Fluent in English, both spoken and written
* Experience working in a larger, global company is a plus
* Accounting knowledge is a plus but not required



  





**Who are we?** 




 At Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW), we develop and operate a portfolio of sustainable, premium, mixed-use real estate assets across Europe and the United States. Driven by our purpose to Reinvent Being Together, we are committed to accelerating urban regeneration and rethinking how we live together in cities. Today, 2,000 dedicated individuals are working together to reshape the future of real estate and shopping centres by creating innovative, engaging spaces that truly redefine community connections.
 



  





 Our flagship destinations are more than just places; they're the heartbeat of the community. We empower our employees to make a real impact through volunteering, DE&I programs, and leading ESG efforts. URW is at the forefront of experiential entertainment, constantly pushing boundaries to craft unforgettable in-person and digital experiences. By surprising and delighting our customers, we create magical moments and reinvent togetherness in every interaction.
 



  





 Within our Northern Europe Region - spanning the Netherlands, Denmark, Sweden, and the UK - we own and manage eight exceptional assets. These include Westfield Mall of the Netherlands, Stadshart Amstelveen, Westfield London, and Westfield Stratford City.
 



  





**As an international company, our primary internal language is English. Therefore, we kindly ask that you submit your application and resume in English.** 




  





**Why work for us:** 




 We put people's development and learning at the heart of what we do. We'll help you master your role, build your technical skills, and take advantage of top-notch management and leadership programs. From face-to-face workshops to our global online learning resources, we encourage you to make learning a part of your daily routine.
 



  





 We offer a fantastic bonus scheme, and many other incentives, including company health insurance is available at a reduced tariff.
 



  





 Want to know more about what it's like working at URW? Visit our site
 
 https://www.urw.com/join-us/life-at-urw
 




  





**What Matters to Us:** 




 At URW, our purpose is to
 **Reinvent Being Together** 
 , reimagining how people and communities connect, socialise, and experience dynamic moments in unique and meaningful ways.
 



  





 We are proud to promote equal opportunities and embrace diversity in profiles, experiences, and skills. At URW, we believe that diversity is the cornerstone of our success, and we are dedicated to fostering an environment that respects, values, celebrates, and leverages individual differences to their fullest potential.
 



  





 We evaluate each application with fairness and without discrimination based on disability, age, gender, origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristics. Our commitment is to provide equal access to our services for everyone. If you require any adjustments for your interview, please let us know in advance, so we can support you accordingly.
 



  





 As part of our commitment to maintaining a safe and trustworthy working environment, we conduct comprehensive security checks on all employees prior to their start date. These checks may include identity verification, employment history, and criminal record screening, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
 



  





**Join us and be part of a team that's making a real difference in your local communities!**

 At Legora, we prioritise security. Always.
   

  

 We are looking for exceptional Detection & Response engineers to help us detect, investigate, and respond to threats as they continue to evolve. In this role you will have the opportunity to shape our technical security capabilities from the ground up and set the president for what modern threat detection and response practices should look like.
   

  

* This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role, we believe building together in person drives better outcomes.


**What You Will Be Doing**
* Lead Legora's cyber security incident response efforts across any domain from external attacks to insider threats.
* Develop and manage tooling to enhance detection, investigation and response efforts.
* Develop and maintain detection capabilities, playbooks and workflows to quickly identify, respond to, and remediate potential incidents.

* Coordinate with teams across the whole organization to plan for, and resolve incidents.


**Who You Are**

* 10+ years of detection engineering, incident response or threat hunting experience
* Expert knowledge and understanding of cloud based environments and operations
* Strong self-drive and motivated to build best-in-class solutions
* Exceptional team-player thriving on cross-team collaboration

title: Biträdande kvalitetssamordnare till Klinisk kemi
description:
Publicerad 7 maj 2026
   

  

 📍
   

  

 Stockholm, Stockholm
   

  

 🗓
   

  

 Sista dag att söka: 31 maj 2026
   

  

 Tillsvidareanställning
   

  

 Omfattning: 100%
   

  

 Heltid
   

  

 Vill du få mail när det kommer in nya jobb?
   

  

 Vill du vara med och säkerställa hög kvalitet i en av Europas ledande laboratorieverksamheter? I rollen som biträdande kvalitetssamordnare inom Klinisk kemi får du ett utvecklande och ansvarsfullt uppdrag där du tillsammans med engagerade kollegor bidrar till att driva kvalitetsarbete och verksamhetsutveckling framåt i hela organisationen.
   

  

****Du erbjuds****
* en nyckelroll i kvalitetsarbetet med möjlighet att påverka och utveckla verksamheten
* ett varierat och självständigt arbete med breda kontaktytor inom laboratoriemedicin
* kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling inom kvalitetsledning, regelverk och laboratorieverksamhet
* delaktighet i förbättrings- och utvecklingsprojekt tillsammans med erfarna kollegor
* en dynamisk arbetsmiljö där samarbete, struktur och kvalitet står i fokus


 Självklart får du också ta del av våra generella förmåner som Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset erbjuder dig.
   

  

****Om tjänsten****
 Klinisk kemi är en del av Medicinsk Diagnostik Karolinska (MDK) och har cirka 650 anställda. Vårt arbete innefattar allt från närlaboratorie- och specialdiagnostik dagtid till omfattande diagnostik dygnet runt med korta svarstider, provtagning, funktionsundersökningar, multidisciplinär preanalytisk hantering, logistik samt kvalitetssäkringsstöd för patientnära analyser i vården. Vår verksamhet bedrivs vid Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset i Solna och Huddinge, på Södersjukhuset, Danderyds sjukhus, Norrtälje sjukhus och Södertälje sjukhus. Vi bedriver också verksamhet på ett stort antal närlaboratorier och provtagningscentraler i Region Stockholm.
   

  

 Du kommer att i samarbete med Kvalitetssamordnare för klinisk kemi arbeta med utbildning och stöd i vårt kvalitetsledningssystem, utformning och upprätthållande av dokumentation, avvikelsehantering/utredning, revisioner och behörighetsregister. Du kommer att planera och genomföra interna revisioner samt delta vid externa revisioner i verksamheten. Uppdraget som biträdande kvalitetssamordnare är utåtriktat, självständigt och kräver god samarbetsförmåga. Du har många samarbetsytor med alla yrkeskategorier inom den medicinska enheten. Du är en del av Klinisk kemis övergripande verksamhetsstöd vilket gör att även andra verksamhetsstödjande arbetsuppgifter kan förekomma.
   

  

 Vi jobbar med ständigt förbättringsarbete och verksamhetsutveckling, vilket kan innebära att både leda och delta i arbetsgrupper. För att lyckas med uppdraget krävs kontinuerlig kommunikation och avstämning medarbetare emellan. Du kommer att vara placerad på Karolinska i Huddinge eller Solna, men då verksamheten är mycket geografiskt spridd förekommer resor/möten på andra sjukhus i regionen.
   

  

****Vi söker dig som****
* tar ansvar för sin uppgift. Strukturerar själv sitt angreppssätt och driver sina processer vidare.
* arbetar bra med andra människor. Relaterar sig till dem på ett lyhört och smidigt sätt. Lyssnar, kommunicerar och löser konflikter på ett konstruktivt sätt.
* kommunicerar på ett tydligt sätt. Säkerställer att budskap når fram och att förväntningarna är klara för alla berörda parter. Påminner och följer upp.
* är noggrann och väl medveten om mål och kvalitetsstandard. Lägger ner stor vikt vid att man lever upp till dessa.


****Kvalifikationer****
**Krav**
* Biomedicinsk analytiker eller annan akademisk naturvetenskaplig högskoleutbildning (kandidatexamen eller högre) inom relevant område.
* Flerårig arbetslivserfarenhet inom ackrediterad Klinisk laboratorieverksamhet
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska språket
* God vana att kommunicera i skrift/tal
* Goda kunskaper i Officepaketet samt god förmåga att använda/ta till sig olika IT-verktyg/program


**Meriterande**
* Arbetslivserfarenhet inom Klinisk kemi
* Arbetslivserfarenhet av kvalitetsarbete inom klinisk laboratorieverksamhet
* Kunskap om författningar/ ISO-standarder som berör laboratoriemedicin
* Arbetsledande roller/uppdrag
* Magisterexamen eller annan högre utbildning inom området


****Om rekryteringsprocessen****
 Urval och intervjuer kan komma att ske löpande under ansökningstiden.
   

  

 I samband med din ansökan behöver du bifoga CV. Istället för ett personligt brev ber vi dig besvara urvalsfrågor samt kort motivera varför du passar för rollen. Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.
   

  

 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan – Tillsammans är vi Karolinska!
   

  

 Inför tillsättning av befattning som innefattar vård av barn och ungdom, kontrolleras den som erbjuds tjänsten mot misstanke- och belastningsregistret. Här kan du läsa mer om rekryteringsprocessen och om vilka kontroller som utförs på Karolinska.
   

  

 Som anställd på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset kan du komma att krigsplaceras. Läs mer om vad det innebär här.
   

  

 Om du är eller tidigare har varit anställd i Region Stockholm kommer vi att ta interna referenser från din nuvarande eller tidigare chef, om du blir aktuell för anställning.
   

  

 För tillsvidaretjänster kan provanställning komma att tillämpas.
   

  

**Om Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset**
 Vi på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset är stolta över att vi är ett av världens främsta universitetssjukhus. Förutom vårt särskilda ansvar för den högspecialiserade vården i Stockholmsregionen är huvuduppdraget att tillsammans med Karolinska Institutet utbilda framtidens vårdanställda och bedriva världsledande forskning samtidigt som vi fortsätter vårt arbete med att utveckla vården för patientens bästa. Vi arbetar inom en rad olika områden och professioner.
   

  

 Vår vision - Vi ska bota och lindra imorgon det ingen kan bota och lindra idag.
   

  

 Läs gärna mer om oss på karolinska.se och följ oss på sociala medier!

title: Advisory Consultant
description:
**You will**
* **Lead resilience‑focused consulting engagements across the EMEA region** 
 , supporting customers in strengthening their security and operational resilience.
* **Act as a subject matter expert in resilience solutions** 
 within the Security & Resilience Consulting Practice, including:
	+ Leading and managing large, complex customer engagements.
	+ Delivering technical and executive‑level resilience workshops.
	+ Interpreting, managing, and delivering against complex Statements of Work (SOWs).
* **Provide thought leadership on industry best practices** 
 , helping customers align resilience strategies with business and regulatory requirements.
* **Maintain full lifecycle ownership of resilience solutions** 
 , from requirements gathering and validation through solution design, implementation, and delivery.
* **Plan and execute implementations and forward‑looking resilience strategies** 
 , including pre‑sales support, advisory consulting, design workshops, implementation, customization, integration, and customer enablement.
* **Collaborate closely with internal teams and customer stakeholders** 
 to ensure a seamless transition from initial consultation to production and operational handover.


  

  

**Essential Requirements**
* Excellent technical knowledge and experience across Resiliency portfolios, including:
	+ Data Protection and Backup & Recovery
	+ Business Continuity
	+ Disaster Recovery
* Strong understanding of the wider IT landscape including Network, Storage, Compute and Replication.
* Strong understanding of physical and logical isolation and their role in resilience and security.
* Strong understanding of cyber security regulations such as DORA, NIS2, and other relevant EMEA regulatory frameworks.
* Strong understanding of operating models and their application in complex organisations.
* Strong understanding of application dependency analysis within complex IT environments.
* Strong understanding of recovery automation and its role in operational resilience.
* Advanced customer communication skills, demonstrating strong stakeholder awareness and the ability to influence decisions, align priorities, and drive outcomes.
* Advanced writing skills with proven capability to develop clear, accurate, and customer‑ready process documentation.
* Understand the application of project management principles and methodologies.
* Experience in Consulting Delivery.
* 8-10+ years’ experience with the above.


  

  

**Desirable Requirements**
* Experience in implementing or assessing physical and logical isolation across infrastructure, networks, applications, or environments.
* Good understanding of isolation concepts such as network segmentation, environment separation, and infrastructure independence.
* Good understanding of target operating model (TOM) design, including governance, processes, and capabilities.
* Experience working with or designing current‑state and target operating models.
* Experience identifying, analysing, and documenting application and system dependencies.
* Good understanding of logical and physical application dependencies across platforms and services
 *.*
* Experience designing and implementing automated recovery solutions for infrastructure, applications, or platforms.
* Good understanding of recovery automation techniques, including orchestration, scripting, and runbook automation.
* Good understanding of the wider IT landscape including Backup/Recovery Architectures, Active Directory and Syslog.
* Good understanding of TOGAF framework.

title: Product Marketing Generalist
description:
**Mission**
 Neko is redefining what prevention means, from treating illness when it arrives, to sustaining health before it's ever at risk. Our mission: make data-driven, preventative care accessible to more people, before symptoms appear.
   

  

 In a single, non-invasive visit under an hour, proprietary technology and direct clinical care combine to deliver personalised, actionable insights. It's a team that thinks in 10x, not 10%. Every role here plays a part in building a world where prevention is the norm, and where your work genuinely helps people live longer, healthier lives.
   

  

**Role Purpose**
 This is a product-embedded PMM role. You will sit directly inside product squads, working alongside engineers, designers, product managers and clinical leads to bring PMM thinking in at the point of creation, all the way through to launch.
   

  

 You will be assigned to one or two high-priority product projects at a time, running positioning, messaging, and marcomm strategy for each in sequence. You will use a shared playbook and feed your work back to the central PMM team.
   

  

 You will report to the Product Marketing Lead. This role is best suited to someone who likes to get close to the product — bringing empathy for the end user, curiosity for the technical details, and focus to see a project from inception to launch.
   

  

 This is a foundational role: you will be part of the early Product Marketing team, helping to build the function from the ground up and to shape how it operates.
   

  

**What You’ll Deliver in the First 6–12 Months**
* Be the embedded PMM presence within designated product squads, representing the member perspective, flagging positioning and messaging questions early, and building out the marcomm strategy to tell our members and the world about our latest innovation.
* Own the launch playbook for each product project you are assigned to: brief, positioning, messaging, marcomm strategy, Medical/Legal/Regulatory review, and post-launch debrief.
* Develop briefs for launch materials across the relevant touchpoints.
* Run two to three significant product launches in your first year, with clear ownership from kick-off through to post-launch.
* Develop robust research and reporting to enable continuous learning and improvement across the PMM function.
* Embed Medical/Legal/Regulatory review into all workflows, ensuring clinical accuracy and compliance before anything is published.


**Requirements**
* 3+ years in product marketing or product management with experience ideally in consumer technology, healthtech, or a subscription/membership business.
* Track record of owning product launches end-to-end, not just contributing to them.
* Genuine interest in the future of healthcare and the ability to move seamlessly between technical, medical, and consumer industry topics.
* Fast learner who can get up to speed on a new product area quickly and produce output without needing lengthy handoff periods.
* Comfortable working inside product squads and managing stakeholder relationships at pace across engineering, design, and clinical teams.
* Strong writer who can collaborate effectively with a copywriter to make clinical or technical information feel clear, actionable, and human for a general audience.
* Experience working within regulated or claim-sensitive environments is a plus (pharma, medtech, financial services).
* AI tool fluency is essential.


**About Titles At Neko**
 We use a simplified internal title framework that prioritises clarity over hierarchy, so internal titles may differ from market‑facing role titles. Scope, impact and level of the role are fully aligned and will be clearly discussed throughout the process.
   

  

**Hiring Process**
 Candidates progress from application and structured screening through thoughtfully designed interviews culminating in a formal offer and final pre-employment checks before joining the team.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Statement**
 Neko Health is committed to inclusive hiring and member-first care. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage you to request reasonable adjustments to support your application.

title: Manager People & Culture - Fixed term
description:
**Join us to deliver a smoke- free future as People & Culture Business Partner!**
 We’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future with one clear purpose – to deliver a smoke-free future. Swedish Match is an affiliate of PMI and has been a leader in the industry for more than 200 years.
   

  

 In the Nordic region, we are 1,800 employees based in Sweden, Denmark, Finland and Norway working in various functions and teams and together we are collaborating to deliver a smoke-free Nordics.
   

  

 We are seeking a dynamic and forward-thinking People & Culture Business Partner to join our HR team in Stockholm. In this pivotal role, you will lead the implementation of our People & Culture strategy and key initiatives across assigned business units and functional teams within the Nordics cluster. If you are passionate about shaping organizational culture, eager to drive meaningful change, and motivated by the opportunity to make a real impact, this is the perfect environment for you. As part of our ambitious journey toward a smoke-free future, you'll find endless opportunities for personal and professional growth as you help build the foundation for lasting transformation.
   

  

 Please note that this is a 12 month fixed-term contract.
   

  

**Your Main Responsibilities**
* Work with business leaders in assigned business units/functions and translate business strategies into organizational priorities and plans; identify future business demands/opportunities with a P&C aspect and deploy solutions that impact business success.
* Act as Change Enabler by providing proactive support to successfully plan and implement business transformations and other strategic organizational change programs.
* Based on current and future business needs, drive strategic talent planning in assigned business units/functional teams, deploy talent strategy sustaining a well-developed, diverse pipeline of talent delivering a strong succession for critical positions.
* Coach all levels of managers on people strategy, including organizational design, career development, coaching, recognition, performance management, etc.
* Develop a positive work environment in assigned departments through implementing effective interventions like cultural transformation, collaboration improvement, employee well-being, diversity and inclusion.
* Analyze and interpret key people data to identify trends and talent needs; communicate findings and recommendations to the business.
* In collaborations with subject matter experts, contribute to the development and roll-out of P&C policies and programs (e.g., talent, L&D, compensation, etc.).
* Contribute to productive labor relations by assisting people leaders within assigned business units with employee and labor relations matters, compliance with company policies and employment laws.


**Requirements**
* Minimum of a bachelor’s degree in human resources, Business or Organization Development or equivalent.
* At least 5 years of experience in an HR Business Partnering role within multinational organizations, ideally for a multi-functional, multi-line-of-business organization.
* Exceptional internal client relationship management skills, experience in building relationships with a diverse group of stakeholders, ability to influence decision makers.
* Well-versed in HR disciplines: talent acquisition, talent management, performance management, organizational development, change management, learning and leadership development, employee engagement, etc.
* Strong project management and execution skills.
* Proven analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze data, understand trends and develop recommendations for action.
* Sound knowledge of Swedish labor law, experience in trade unions negotiations.
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English; Swedish is an advantage.
* Advanced knowledge in MS Office applications.


 Please note that relocation support is not available for this role.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 Our success depends on the dedicated people who come to work every single day with a sense of purpose and an appetite for progress. Join us at Swedish Match and you too can:
   

  

* Seize the freedom to define your future and ours. We’ll empower you to take risks, experiment and explore.
* Be part of an inclusive, diverse culture, where everyone’s contribution is respected; collaborate with some of the world’s best people and feel like you belong.
* Enjoy flexibility of hybrid way of working - split your time between working from our office or the comfort of your home, where it works best for you in line with business needs.
* Pursue your ambitions and develop your skills with a global business – our staggering size and scale provides endless opportunities to progress.
* Take pride in delivering our promise to society: to deliver a smoke-free future.


 #SWMA
   

  

**19899**

title: Business Consultant
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Har du gedigen erfarenhet av digitalisering, verksamhetsutveckling och transformation? Vi söker en driven konsult som vill ta en nyckelroll i att leda strategiska initiativ där affärsstrategi möter teknisk innovation – och vara med och forma framtidens organisationer. Hos oss på CGI arbetar du nära ledande aktörer inom både privat och offentlig sektor. Du får möjlighet att driva komplexa transformationsprojekt, ge rådgivning på högsta nivå och skapa lösningar som gör konkret skillnad i en snabbt föränderlig omvärld.
   

  

 Vad du gör hos oss
   

  

* Ge strategisk rådgivning till ledande befattningshavare och organisationer.
* Leda och driva omfattande förändringsresor i olika branscher.
* Identifiera nya affärsmöjligheter och utveckla kundanpassade erbjudanden.
* Utforma och digitalisera processer, arbetssätt och verksamhetsmodeller.
* Coacha kollegor och bidra till att stärka vårt team och vårt framtida erbjudande.


 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Som Business Consultant blir du en central rådgivare för kunder som står inför större förändringsresor. Du hjälper dem att navigera i komplexa utvecklingsinitiativ och säkerställer att våra insatser skapar tydligt och mätbart värde. Rollen innebär också att du aktivt bidrar till att identifiera och vinna nya affärer, samtidigt som du utvecklar våra erbjudanden och anpassar dem till kundernas strategiska mål. Du får en dynamisk vardag där uppdragen varierar i omfattning och karaktär. Om du trivs i en miljö där analys, rådgivning och förändringsledning möts – och där du får påverka både kunders och CGI:s utveckling – kommer du att trivas hos oss.
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

* Flera års erfarenhet av verksamhetsutveckling, digitalisering eller transformationsarbete.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att driva komplexa projekt, gärna med koppling till IT eller förändringsledning.
* Stark affärsförståelse och vana att arbeta nära beslutsfattare.
* Relevant akademisk bakgrund eller motsvarande erfarenhet.
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.


 För att lyckas i rollen är du relationsskapande, analytisk och trygg i att ta dig an nya sammanhang. Du ser helheten men kan också fördjupa dig i detaljer när det behövs. Du är samarbetsorienterad, lyhörd och motiveras av att skapa värde – både för kunder och kollegor. Resor kan förekomma beroende på uppdrag. CGI erbjuder en hybrid arbetsmodell som ger dig flexibilitet och en hållbar balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Bakgrundskontroll kan förekomma.
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Legal Engineer - Law Firms EMEA
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**What You’ll Be Doing**
 Let's get the tricky part out of the way: this role doesn't fit neatly into a traditional box. It's part legal ops, part product specialist, part solutions architect, and part client whisperer. If you enjoy roles that stay static, this probably isn't it. Your mission is to help our clients, some of the most prestigious law firms in the world, get the absolute most out of our platform.
   

  

**Some Of What You'll Be Up To**
* Acting as a thought partner to law firm partners, associates, and innovation teams - building trusted relationships, addressing tricky pain points, and identifying high-value opportunities for them to scale their use of Legora across practice groups.
* Working together with Go To Market and Customer Success in taking full ownership of firm relationships - from running pilots with key practice areas, to onboarding new associates and partners, and ultimately guiding full-firm adoption across global law firms.
* Be the voice of law firm users inside Legora - sharing insights from M&A teams, litigation departments, and other practice groups that directly inform product development, roadmap priorities, and strategic direction.
* Delivering clear, confident product demos and training that bring the power of our platform to life - whether you're presenting to a single practice group or rolling out firm-wide.
* Documenting best practices from leading firms, contribute to the development of scalable playbooks, and help shape how we grow this function globally.
* Confidently helping law firms navigate one of the biggest shifts in how legal services are delivered - from evaluating the ROI and competitive advantage of AI to clearly mapping how AI will impact different practice areas, from corporate to disputes.


**What You Bring**
 You come from the world of Big Law; but you've never been content with how things have always been done. You may have trained and practised at a top-tier law firm, experiencing first-hand the pressures of billable hours, client demands, and operational inefficiencies. Perhaps you've moved into legal innovation, knowledge management, or legal tech within a firm. Maybe you've even left private practice to disrupt the status quo through a legal tech company, or ventures of your own. Either way, you know how law firms think, work, and (sometimes) struggle and you're ready to help them level up with technology.
   

  

**You'll Thrive In This Role If You**
* Have a background in law, with experience at a top-tier law firm - ideally across multiple practice areas or with exposure to firm-wide initiatives.
* Are tech-curious and product-savvy. Not necessarily a coder, but passionate about navigating technical conversations, especially around things like generative AI, technology implementation, innovation and productivity.
* Communicate clearly and confidently with diverse stakeholders, from senior partners to graduate trainees, and from lawyers to engineers. I.e. translating across disciplines is second nature to you.
* Bring structure to ambiguity and energy to complexity; and enjoy being the calm, credible presence in a fast-moving environment.
* Understand how to evaluate and improve existing processes as a direct result of new and emerging technologies - whether that's streamlining due diligence, contract review, or legal research.
* Are proactive, self-directed, and comfortable juggling multiple firm relationships at once.
* Care deeply about quality, but don't get stuck in perfectionism - you get things done, and you make them better over time.
* Understand the commercial realities of law firm life: billing pressures, client expectations, competitive positioning, and the delicate balance between innovation and risk management.


**A few more things**
 We're a fast-growing company with bold ambitions, so you'll be joining at a time when things are moving quickly - in the best possible way. You'll have a lot of autonomy, a lot of responsibility, and the chance to genuinely shape how this function evolves. Everyone here has a founder-mentality.
   

  

 If that sounds exciting rather than terrifying, we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Senior Specialist Solutions Architect - AI & ML Engineer
description:
**FEQ327R156**
**Mission**
 As a Sr Specialist Solutions Architect (SSA) - ML & AI Engineer, you will be the trusted technical ML & AI expert to both Databricks customers and the Field Engineering organization. You will work with Solution Architects to guide enterprise and strategic customers in architecting production-grade ML & AI applications on Databricks, while aligning their technical roadmap with the continually evolving Databricks Data Intelligence Platform. You will continue to strengthen your technical skills through applying cutting edge technologies in GenAI, MLOps, and ML more broadly, expanding your impact through mentorship, and establishing yourself as an AI thought leader.
   

  

**The Impact You Will Have**
* Architect production level ML & AI workloads for customers using our unified platform, including agents, end-to-end ML pipelines, training/inference optimization, integration with cloud-native services, MLOps, etc.
* Serve as trusted practitioner for enterprise GenAI solutions, including RAG architectures, agentic systems (tool-calling agents, multi-agent orchestration, guardrails), natural language querying of structured data, AI evaluation and observability, and monitoring systems
* Build, scale, and optimize customer AI workloads and apply best in class MLOps to productionize these workloads across a variety of domains
* Provide advanced technical support to Solution Architects during the technical sale ranging from feature engineering, training, tracking, serving to model monitoring all within a single platform, as well as participating in the larger ML SME community in Databricks
* Collaborate cross-functionally with the product and engineering teams to represent the voice of the customer, define priorities and influence the product roadmap, helping with the adoption of Databricks’ AI offerings


**What We Look For**
* 7+ years of hands-on industry ML experience in at least one of the following:
+ ML Engineer: Build and maintain production-grade cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP) infrastructure that supports the deployment of ML applications, including drift monitoring.
+ AI Engineer: Experience with the latest techniques in LLMs & agentic systems including vector databases, fine-tuning LLMs, AI guardrail systems, and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, and OpenAI
+ Experience with data engineering, or a good understanding of the concept of data engineering

* Experience communicating and/or teaching technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences alike
* Passion for collaboration, life-long learning, and driving business value through ML & AI
* [Preferred] 5+ years customer-facing experience in a pre-sales or post-sales role
* Can meet expectations for technical training and role-specific outcomes within 3 months of hire
* This role can be remote, but we prefer that you be located in the job listing area and can travel up to 30% when needed


**About Databricks**
 Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
   

  

**Benefits**
 At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region click here.
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion**
 At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
   

  

**Compliance**
**If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.**

title: Butikschef till Tele2 i Mall of Scandinavia
description:
Tele2 är en nordisk och baltisk telekomoperatör byggd på en stark utmanarkultur. Sedan 1993 har Tele2 utmanat etablerade normer i telekombranschen genom att göra uppkoppling enklare, smartare och mer kostnadseffektiv för våra kunder. Tele2 erbjuder mobila och fasta tjänster, TV och streaming samt IoT‑lösningar till konsumenter och företag i Norden och Baltikum.
   

  

 Hållbarhet är en integrerad del av hur vi arbetar. Tele2 har uppmärksammats som Europas klimatledare av Financial Times och utsetts till Sveriges mest hållbara bolag, vilket speglar vårt långsiktiga engagemang för att minska vår miljöpåverkan och driva cirkulära lösningar tillsammans med våra kunder.
   

  

 Våra värderingar – Be Brave, Take Action, Make it Simple och Act Cost-efficiently – vägleder vår kultur, hur vi samarbetar och hur vi omsätter idéer till handling.
   

  

*Vad händer när passion möter ledarskap? Hos Tele2 blir det en plattform för framgång! Vi söker en Butikschef till Stockholm, Mall of Scandinavia som brinner för att utveckla medarbetare och driva resultat. Du kommer att coacha ditt team till att överträffa alla mål i en butik där varje kundmöte räknas, och där energi och oslagbar laganda är nyckeln till vår gemensamma framgång.*
**OM ROLLEN**
 Som Butikschef har du det övergripande ansvaret för den dagliga driften av butiken. Du driver försäljning både genom att själv vara aktiv i säljarbetet och genom att inspirera och utveckla våra duktiga säljare så att de blir riktigt proffsiga i mötet med kund. Som person tror vi därför att du är en coachande och inspirerande ledare som har stor förmåga att arbeta strukturerat efter uppsatta mål. Du är duktig på försäljning och brinner för att se dina medarbetare utvecklas.
   

  

*Och du är inte ensam – du har ett helt team bakom dig.*
 Tillsammans skapar vi en butik där engagemang, nytänkande och kundfokus genomsyrar allt ni gör. Med din drivkraft och vårt stöd får du verktygen att bygga något riktigt stort – från första kundmötet till ett etablerat nav för service i världsklass.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder dig en spännande och utvecklande roll i en miljö där du har möjlighet att göra skillnad. Vi har en familjär stämning och med teamet i ryggen får du det bästa utgångsläget för att få butiken att prestera på topp. Vi drivs av intern utveckling och för den som har ambition och vilja ser vi detta som en start för en karriär på Tele2. Som Butikschef erbjuds du en gedigen introduktion samt vårt skräddarsydda ledarskapsprogram för att göra dig mer rustad inför att ta din butik till nya höjder!
   

  

**Benefits**
 Tjänstepension
   

  

 Möjlighet till interna karriärvägar och utveckling
   

  

 Rabatter på Tele2s produkter och tjänster
   

  

 Inkluderande arbetskultur
   

  

 Lön och försäkringar enligt kollektivavtal
   

  

 Gedigen onboarding och utvecklingsmöjligheter

title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 We’re hiring a Senior Product Marketing Manager to own a core product area at Legora. The role is based in Stockholm, Sweden.
   

  

 This is a high-ownership, high-influence role for a top 1% PMM. You will operate as the strategic and executional leader of your product area—defining positioning and narrative, driving go-to-market strategy, influencing product direction, and delivering measurable adoption and revenue growth.
   

  

 You’ll partner closely with Product, GTM, and executive leadership to shape category-defining AI products. The role demands rare range: technical rigor, strategic clarity, and creative excellence. It offers the opportunity to do the best work of your career.
   

  

 What You’ll Own
   

  

 Narrative & Positioning
   

  

* Define clear, differentiated positioning from first principles
* Turn complex, AI-driven products into sharp, compelling customer value
* Establish a high bar for clarity, taste, and storytelling across every touchpoint


 Go-to-Market Strategy
   

  

* Lead end-to-end GTM for a core product area, from ideation through adoption
* Partner with Product, Marketing, GTM to shape what gets built and how it’s brought to market
* Ensure launches showcase Legora’s differentiated value that translate into pipeline and revenue


 Revenue Enablement
   

  

* Enable sales with world-class assets: pitches, demo narratives, one-pagers, competitive strategy
* Increase win rates and deal velocity through sharp differentiation
* Partner with Growth and Design to drive conversion across web, campaigns, and lifecycle


**Web Strategy**
* Own product storytelling across the website and key growth surfaces.
* Continuously optimize for clarity, differentiation, and conversion while maintaining narrative consistency across touchpoints.


 Market & Customer Intelligence
   

  

* Synthesize customer conversations, usage data, win/loss analysis, and competitive intelligence into actionable insight.
* Influence product prioritization and roadmap decisions


 What You Bring
   

  

* Experience: 6–8+ years in Product Marketing or Product Strategy in high-growth AI or B2B SaaS environments with a track record of marketing and driving adoption of AI products.
* Strategic Thinking: You operate from first principles, form strong points of view, and know how to separate durable signal from short-term noise.
* Leadership & Influence: Strong executive presence and ability to influence senior stakeholders. You lead those around you, challenge thinking, elevate quality, and provide clear strategic direction across Product and GTM teams.
* Storytelling & Craft: Outstanding writer and communicator with the ability to make technical, AI products feel clear, differentiated, and compelling.
* Technical Fluency: Strong grasp of technical AI concepts and the ability to engage credibly with Product and Engineering.
* Ownership & Drive: High agency, low ego, and a bias for action. You thrive in ambiguous, cross-functional environments and push work forward without waiting for perfect clarity.
* Creativity & Taste: You hold a high bar for messaging precision, product storytelling, and visual presentation. Details matter.
* Data-Driven Rigor: You use data to make decisions, measure impact, iterate quickly, and anchor outcomes.
* Speed & Quality: You move incredibly fast while delivering extremely high quality work. You don’t view speed and quality as tradeoffs.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: AML Senior Fullstack Engineer
description:
**About The Position**
 Verified is a Nordic-based RegTech company dedicated to building trust and adding value for our customers. We secure and automate our clients' most advanced workflows and onboarding processes using our API-first SaaS & PaaS products. Our platform enables businesses with powerful electronic signing, electronic identification, Anti-Money Laundering, and Smart Forms capabilities. We are implementing an “AI first” mindset in the company and you will be implementing AI agents and services both for internal usage and in our products.
   

  

 You will be working on both front-end and back-end application services residing in AWS cloud. You get to work with a bunch of great people, where the whole team owns the project together.
   

  

 As a Senior Fullstack Engineer, you won't just write code; you will own the lifecycle of our core services. You will join a high-autonomy team responsible for decoupling dependencies, reducing time-to-market, and integrating cutting-edge AI capabilities. You’ll work across the full stack - from modern frontends to AWS serverless backend microservices - ensuring our platform remains secure, scalable, and resilient.
   

  

**What are you going to do?**
* Agile Development: Design, plan, estimate, and develop software in an agile environment
* Scalable Systems: Build and maintain scalable systems with a strong focus on security and reliability
* AI Integration: Play a key role in our AI roadmap, implementing advanced patterns like RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and AI Agents to enhance our product's intelligence
* AI in SDLC: Implement AI in all steps of the development lifecycle, from requirements to running the services and collect metrics for feedback
* API & Web Development: Develop and maintain REST APIs and web applications
* Cross-functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely with product owners, designers, QA, and architects to deliver high-quality solutions
* Influence: Contribute to architecture decisions and technologies used within the team
* Issue Resolution: Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve production issues
* Engineering Excellence: Drive high standards for code maintainability, security, and performance. You’ll lead code reviews, mentor peers, and refine our CI/CD pipelines.
* System Evolution: Contribute to making the platform more scalable by decoupling dependencies and reducing time to market
* Team Growth & Culture: Support the growth of the engineering team and foster a culture of collaboration, learning, and innovation


****What will you bring to the team?****
* Experience: 10+ Years: You are a seasoned Fullstack Engineer with a track record of building cloud-first SaaS solutions
* AI Skills: Knowledge and experience from how AI is used in the development lifecycle and the pros and cons of different LLM and tools
* Technical Versatility: You have extensive experience with Node.js, Typescript, Vue.js, MongoDB, Git and CI/CD workflows
* API Development: You have extensive experience building and maintaining REST APIs
* Cloud Expertise: You have hands-on experience with AWS managed services (S3, SQS, Lambda, CloudFormation, etc.)
* Scalable Systems: You have experience building large-scale, API-first systems
* Architecture Knowledge: You have a solid understanding of microservices and serverless architecture
* Security Awareness: You are knowledgeable about modern security mechanisms and authentication
* Testing: You are experienced in writing unit and integration tests and familiar with various testing frameworks and methodologies
* Observability: You have worked with observability platforms like Datadog, Prometheus, Grafana before
* The "Ownership" Mindset: You are autonomous, business-oriented, and believe that a ticket isn't "Done" until it delivers real customer value
* Team Collaboration: You are a collaborative team player who enjoys sharing knowledge
* Learning: You have a genuine interest in programming and staying up to date with new technologies
* Language: You are professionally proficient in English, both written and spoken
* Domain knowledge: From FinTech or RegTech and working with regulations and compliance (we are ISO27001 certified)


**This Experience Is An Asset**
* Integrating with third party data sources


**Other Information**
* Start: According to the agreement
* Extent: Full-time
* Location: Our Stockholm office is currently based in Solna


 Hybrid - culture and innovation is best forged by working closely together in our cross-functional environment, but we have a hybrid approach to create a good work-life balance.
   

  

**The recruitment process**
 We do not accept applications via email, but if you have specific questions about the position, these will be answered by sindre.slungaard@verified.eu during office hours.
   

  

 Our selection process is continuous, and the advert may close before the recruitment process is completed if we have moved forward to the screening or interview phase.

title: Management Assistant/ Office Manager till SPK
description:
**Om rollen**
 I denna roll rapporterar du till VD och ansvarar för ledningsadministration samt kontorets dagliga drift. Du fungerar som spindeln i nätet mellan ledning, medarbetare och externa kontakter.
   

  

**Dina Huvudsakliga Arbetsuppgifter Inkluderar**
* Stöd till VD och ledning i administrativa frågor, inklusive resebokningar och kalenderhantering.
* Protokollföring vid ledningsmöten samt andra VD-assistentsysslor.
* Administration och koordinering kring styrelsearbete: materialframtagning, möteskallelser via portal samt koordinering.
* Planering och koordinering av fullmäktigesammankomster.
* Organisering av konferenser, personalkonferenser och interna som externa aktiviteter, event och liknande.
* Sammanställa månadskommentarer från externa förvaltare och hantera administrativa uppgifter mot våra externas förvaltares behov av information om SPK
* Ansvar för uppdatering av hemsidan.
* Hantering av leverantörskontakter inklusive externa kapitalförvaltare i vissa fall, avtal och uppföljning av tjänster. SLA för kontoret, exempelvis för städ, fastigheten, kontorsmaterial och fruktleveranser.
* Administrativ hantering av fakturaflöden och betalningsunderlag.


**Kvalifikationer och egenskaper**
 Vi söker dig som är strukturerad, serviceinriktad och trivs i en roll med många kontaktytor. Du har en hög integritet och är van att arbeta självständigt i en förtroendefull position.
   

  

**Vi Ser Att Du Har**
* Flerårig erfarenhet av att stödja ledningsgrupp eller VD.
