* Mycket goda kunskaper i Officepaketet.
* Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig på svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
* Stark samordningsförmåga och vana att hantera flera parallella uppgifter.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med externa kontakter såsom leverantörer och partners i assisterande roll.
* Förmåga att ställa krav och följa upp avtal och leveranser
* Intresserad av att lära sig vår bransch, organisation och vara nyfiken på att utveckla kunskapen om Pension och Kapitalförvaltning


**Om företaget**
 SPK grundades som en pensionskassa år 1944 och fungerar nu som en tjänstepensionsförening. Vårt huvudsakliga mål är att säkerställa och garantera kollektivavtalad tjänstepension inom ramen för BTP-avtalet till våra medlemmar. SPK är en förening och har därför inte något vinstintresse utan enda fokus är att så effektivt som möjligt skapa värden för medlemmarna.
   

  

 Det är viktigt att medlemmarna har förtroende för oss och att vi har olika sätt att säkerställa att de som går i pension (eller blir sjuka) får de förmåner som bestämts på våra föreningsstämmor.
   

  

 Vad gör SPK?
   

  

 Vi har tre huvuduppgifter. Vi tar in premier från arbetsgivarna, skapar mervärde genom kapitalförvaltning och betala ut pensioner och andra ersättningar till förmånstagarna.
   

  

 SPKs strategi har sedan länge varit att ha en oberoende, och effektiv förvaltning av pensioner för att optimera medlemsnyttan. Med en liten organisation, snabba beslutsvägar och ett agilt tänkande strävar vi hela tiden att anpassa oss till nya förutsättningar.Vår storlek ger oss många fördelar. Vi är tillräckligt stora för att ha ekonomiska resurser men tillräckligt små för att agera med snabba förändringar i praktiken.
   

  

**Om Amendo**
 Amendo har arbetat med framtidens kompetens sedan 2001 och är landets ledande konsult- och rekryteringsföretag inom ekonomi, bank, finans, försäkring, IT och tech. Vi hjälper företag att växa och människor att utvecklas. vi är din trygga och engagerade kompetenspartner.
   

  

 Följ oss gärna på
 **Linkedin** 
 ,
 **Instagram** 
 och
 **Facebook** 
 .
   

  

**Frågor och kontakt**
 I denna rekrytering samarbetar SPK med Amendo. Om du har frågor är du välkommen att söka kontakt med ansvarig rekryterare, Erika Brännström. erika.brannstrom@amendo.se 076-133 51 41.
   

  

 Då vi träffar kandidater löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdatum, (27/5), är du varmt välkommen att söka tjänsten så snart som möjligt. Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
   

  

 #administration
   

  

 Amendo har arbetat med framtidens kompetens sedan 2001 och är landets ledande konsult- och rekryteringsföretag inom ekonomi, bank, finans, försäkring, IT och tech. Vi är ett expansivt bolag verksamma över hela landet med lokala kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg och Örnsköldsvik.
   

  

 Vår vision att utveckla världen med kompetens når vi genom att arbeta nära våra kunder och kandidater, vara nyfikna på omvärlden - och hela tiden vilja framåt.
   

  

 Läs mer på amendo.se.

title: Android Developer
description:
*Vill du bygga appar som definierar hur hundratusentals kunder upplever sin tv- och mobilvardag? I TV Client Apps-teamet på Tele2 jobbar vi med att skapa förstklassiga Android-upplevelser – från vårt flaggskepp TV Hub till våra mobilappar. Vi söker nu dig som vill vara med och forma framtidens tv-upplevelse.* 




  





 Är du redo att stå i framkant av den digitala innovationen? Välkommen till vårt framåtlutade team på Tele2, där Digital Capabilities & Technology (DCT) utgör kärnan bakom våra obegränsade produkter och tjänster. DCT möjliggör automatisering och enkelhet till telekommunikationsbranschen och revolutionerar sättet som vi kopplar upp oss på och kommunicerar.
 



  





 Inom vår organisation finner du allt från teknikens grundläggande infrastruktur och IT-system, som förser både våra externa och interna kunder, till avancerad analys, AI och front-end-utveckling. Vårt team har en avgörande roll när det gäller att säkerställa pålitliga och högkvalitativa tjänster genom att leverera exceptionella kundupplevelser till både konsumenter och företag, samtidigt som vi ger stöd till våra kollegor inom Tele2.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Android Developer på Tele2 blir du en del av TV Client Apps, ett tvärfunktionellt team som ansvarar för Tele2s appar på Android TV och mobil. Vi arbetar med moderna verktyg och tekniker, och du har stort inflytande över arkitektur och tekniska besl
 **ut.** 




  





**Dina arbetsuppgifter inklude** 
 rar:
 



  





 ·Utveckla Android-appar i Kotlin med Jetpack Compose, både för TV och mobil
 



 ·Äga och förvalta vår Launcher App (TV Hub) – den app som möter kunden när de startar sin Android TV-box
 



 ·Driva tekniska förbättringar och aktivt bidra till kodbas och arkitektur
 



 ·Delta i hela utvecklingskedjan – från kravfångst och design till release och drift
 



 ·Arbeta med automatiserade tester och CI/CD via GitLab CI
 



  





**Vem är du?** 




 Du trivs i en miljö där du får frihet under ansvar och gillar att jobba nära kollegor med olika kompetenser. Att du är kommunikativ och lösningsorienterad är viktigt, eftersom vi arbetar i korta cykler med tät dialog mellan utveckling, design och produkt.
 



  





**För att du skall trivas och bli framgångsrik i denna roll ser vi att du har:** 




  





 ·God erfarenhet av Android-utveckling i Kotlin·Vana att arbeta med Jetpack Compose eller starkt intresse att lära dig
 



 ·Förståelse för etablerade arkitekturmönster och principer som MVVM, SOLID eller liknande – och en uppskattning för varför de gör skillnad i en växandekodbas
 



 ·Förmåga att ta ansvar för tekniska lösningar i ett autonomt team
 



  





**Meriteranade (ej nödvändigt)** 




 ·Erfarenhet av Android TV, Leanback eller Compose for TV
 



 ·Erfarenhet av mediauppspelning och ExoPlayer (eller liknande mediaspelare)
 



 ·Kunskap om CI/CD (GitLab CI, Jenkins eller liknande)
 



 ·Erfarenhet från andra plattformar (iOS, Web, Chromecast)
 



  





**Några ord från rekrytera** 
*nde chef* 




*Hos oss i TV Client Apps bygger vi apparna som gör Tele2s tjänster tillgängliga för kunderna – allt från Android och iOS till Chromecast och Smart TV. Vi är ett sammansvetsat team av utvecklare, testare, designers och produktägare som alla brinner för att skapa enkla och snygga produkter. Vi söker nu en Android-utvecklare som vill vara med och ta vårt flaggskepp TV Hub och våra mobilappar till nästa nivå. Om du gillar att jobba med modern teknik i ett öppet och framåtlutat team – tveka inte att söka!* 




  





**Varför Tele2** 




 ·På Tele2 främjar vi en kreativ och flexibel arbetsmiljö. Hos oss har vi en kultur där teamwork och inkludering leder vägen framåt och du uppmuntras att vara dig själv som den unika person som just du är
 



 .·Varje medarbetare är viktig och varje individs arbete har en stor påverkan för företagets framgång. Vi vill att du skall växa och du ges möjligheter till utveckling genom exempelvis interna karriärvägar
 



 .·På Tele2 arbetar vi målmedvetet varje dag för att fortsätta vara ledande inom hållbarhet, vilket har lett till att vi blivit utnämnda klimatledare i Europa av Financial Times. Tele2 är också Sveriges mest hållbara företag och rankades 37:a globalt av Time Magazine och Statista på deras första lista över världens mest hållbara företag med 500 företag från över 30 länder. Vi fortsätter att uppnå nya mål och vi hjälper våra kunder att uppnå sina hållbarhetsmål eftersom vi åtagit oss att övergå till en cirkulär ekonomi.
 



  





**Intresse och ansökan** 




 Vill du bli en del av Tele2s värderingsstyrda och inkluderande företagskultur? Tryck då på “ansök” och låt oss ta reda på om vi är en match!Vänligen notera dock att i enlighet med hantering av personuppgifter (GDPR) har vi inte möjlighet att ta emot ansökningar via e-post.
 



  





 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan! För att säkerställa en inkluderande och rättvis rekryteringsprocess fokuserar vi på dina erfarenheter och kompetenser. Därför har Tele2 valt att inte använda personliga brev och vi ber dig istället besvara de urvalsfrågor som ingår i ansökningsformuläret.
 



*Som ett steg i rekryteringsprocessen utför Tele2 en bakgrundskontroll på aktuella slutkandidater.*

title: Business Developer
description:
We’re looking for a curious and commercially minded Business Developer who wants to help shape Elevate’s next chapter across the Nordics. If you enjoy working where production and digital marketing meets automation and AI-driven workflows — and you love turning possibilities into partnerships — this might be your place.
   

  

 As our Business Developer, you’ll be right at the heart of how we grow. You’ll help bring our offering to market, strengthen our position within the NoA network, and build relationships that turn into long-lasting client collaborations. No two weeks will look the same — and that’s exactly what makes the role exciting.
   

  

**Most Days, You’ll Be Working With**
**70% – Sales & client relationships**
* Building and managing pipeline
* Meeting clients and identifying new opportunities
* Leading pitches and driving new business


**20% – Strategy & offering development**
* Shaping and refining Elevate’s commercial offering
* Exploring market expansion opportunities
* Supporting marketing efforts (LinkedIn, Elevate Website 2.0)


**10% – Creative collaboration**
* Working with production and creative teams
* Turning ideas and briefs into clear commercial solutions


**To Thrive Here, You’re Probably Someone Who**
* Has a few years of experience from business development, production, content or media sales, consulting or partnerships
* Understands production processes — and knows how to translate them into clear commercial opportunities
* Naturally builds trust and strong relationships and are a
* “people's person”
* Enjoys collaborating with both commercial, prodcution and creative teams
* Communicates clearly in Swedish (bonus if you know another Nordic language)


**Why Elevate?**
 As part of the The North Alliance (NoA) network—a collaboration platform of 27 specialist agencies across Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Poland and Germany—NoA Elevate leverages strategy, creativity, data and tech to secure customer-driven growth. We help our clients strengthen and expand their customer base, build loyalty and contribute to success and profitability, all grounded in the Nordic values of sustainability, equality, diversity and collaboration.
   

  

**Our recruitment process**
 No need for a personal letter or a CV-document. Just provide us with the link to your LinkedIn profile and answer a few initial questions - that's it.
   

  

 We’ll try to get back to you as soon as possible to set up an initial chat about what we can offer and how you could contribute to our team.
   

  

 After that, you’ll meet with the hiring manager for a more in-depth interview.
   

  

 If you move forward to the final stage, you’ll have the chance to show off your technical skills by presenting a case to us.

title: Grocery Associate - Youth Workers
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

*Tycker du om att arbeta i en dynamisk arbetsmiljö, har ett kundorienterat tänk och älskar att arbeta med ett härligt team?
 **Är du mellan 15 och 17 år gammal?** 
 Om ditt svar är ja - läs då vidare!*
**Bakgrund**
 Wolt är ett finskt techbolag och vi är mest kända för vår matleverans-plattform. Matleveranser var bara början, och nu utforskar och utökar vi vår plattform med andra områden inom detaljhandel och dagligvaror. Vi har vuxit avsevärt under de senaste tre åren och utökat vår verksamhet från bara fyra länder i Norden till 28 länder i Europa och Asien idag. Och vi har bara börjat.
   

  

 Aktuell roll är en del av Wolt Market - våra alldeles egna livsmedelsbutiker som levererar bekvämlighet och en världsledande kundupplevelse. Wolt Market är vårt fantastiska koncept - människor i alla Wolt-länderna kan beställa sina matvaror och få dem levererade med ljusets hastighet! 🤩
   

  

 Vi söker deltidsanställda butiksmedarbetare för att bli en del av vårt fantastiska team i Stockholm och du kan bli en del av vårt team så fort du fyllt 15 år! I denna roll befinner du dig i hjärtat av vår dagliga butiksverksamhet, en sk Virtual Store - du kommer att se till att alla beställningar är plockade och redo att hämtas av våra kurirpartners eller kunder.
   

  

 Vår butik är öppen 7 dagar i veckan så denna roll kräver även att du kan arbeta helger och i olika skift enligt Wolt Markets öppettider. Om du är en lagspelare, tycker om att arbeta självständigt och har ett gott sinne för humor, så är du kanske precis den person vi söker!
   

  

**Vad du ska göra**
* Ta emot beställningar via vår Merchant-app
* Plocka och packa matvaror och lämna dem till kunder och kurier.
* Hantera våra interna system som inkluderar exakt information om näringsämnen och allergener.
* Upprätthålla ordning och reda i butiken.
* Delta i att utveckla vår dagliga drift i butiken.


**Vem du är**
* Du är snabblärd och har förmågan att multitaska
* Proaktiv och serviceminded är ord som beskriver dig bra
* Du har en kan-göra-attityd och tycker om idén att arbeta i en fartfylld entreprenörsmiljö
* Du är tekniskt kunnig
* Du kan arbeta både dagtid, sena kvällar och helger
* Erfarenhet från tidigare arbete i livsmedelsbutik är meriterande
* Du har mycket goda kunskaper i engelska, svenska är ett plus
* Du har rätt att arbeta i Sverige
* Du är 15-17 år gammal


 Om du gillar att arbeta i en miljö med hög tillväxt där du kommer kunna ta ägarskap och vara en del av ett extremt ambitiöst team, klicka nedan för att ansöka!
   

  

 Att arbeta på Wolt Market är inte bara roligt, det är dessutom tryggt och säkert! Som anställd på Wolt Market omfattas du av Handels kollektivavtal för Lager och E-handel.
   

  

 Vi kommer att granska ansökningarna löpande, så vänta inte, skicka in din ansökan snarast! 🤩
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Cash Flow Analyst
description:
*Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hesitate to apply unless they meet every requirement. At Stegra, we’re building an impact-driven, sustainable company - and we need a diverse, inclusive team to make it happen.*
*If you share our passion but don’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You might be the perfect fit for this role, or another as we grow.*
 At Stegra, we’re not just building a plant, we’re proving that sustainable industry is possible. And to do that, we need people like you.
   

  

 Join a team where safety comes first. Where we support each other, learn as we go, and make space for everyone to grow and contribute. This is your chance to be part of something big - while being closer to nature and leading a balanced, purposeful life.
   

  

 Cash Flow Analyst
   

  

 As we scale one of the most ambitious industrial projects in Europe, we’re now looking for a Cash Flow Forecasting Analyst who wants to play an important role in how we plan, steer, and deliver our project.
   

  

 In this role, you will own the short-term cash flow forecasting process across our construction activities. You will work closely with teams across project delivery, procurement, finance, and treasury, turning complex inputs into clear, reliable insights that drive decision-making.
   

  

 As the project evolves, this role also offers the opportunity to transition into an operational cash flow role, supporting the business beyond the construction phase.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

* Own and continuously improve our rolling 13-week cash flow forecast across multiple construction areas
* Partner with stakeholders across the project (EPCM, cost leads, procurement, contract managers, accounts payable, and suppliers) to gather, challenge, and validate inputs
* Translate contracts, payment terms, and project progress into clear cash flow projections
* Track actual vs forecasted cash flow and explain key movements and risks
* Identify upcoming shifts in cash demand and proactively flag risks and opportunities
* Build and develop robust, scalable cash flow models in Excel
* Turn data into clear decision-making material for stakeholders across the organisation
* Contribute to reporting towards project leadership and group treasury
* Improve how we collect, structure, and use financial data across the project


 Who you are
   

  

 We believe you are someone who combines analytical sharpness with strong drive and curiosity.
   

  

* You have a relevant background within finance, controlling, or similar (experience is important, but we value the right mindset just as highly)
* You are highly skilled in Excel and comfortable building models and working with large datasets
* You have the ability to translate data into clear insights and decision support for different stakeholders
* You are used to working in complex, project-based environments


 But more importantly, you are:
   

  

* Structured and detail-oriented, with a strong sense of ownership for accuracy
* Someone with confidence and integrity - you don’t just accept inputs, you challenge and ask why
* Proactive and outgoing in your communication; you enjoy reaching out, building relationships, and solving problems together with others
* Comfortable navigating ambiguity and incomplete information, while still delivering reliable outputs
* A problem-solver who keeps things moving forward, even when it’s not straightforward


 Why Stegra?
   

  

 At Stegra, you’ll be part of a team with a clear mission: to accelerate the transition to sustainable industry.
   

  

 You’ll work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment where:
   

  

* Your work has real, visible impact on a large-scale project
* You are trusted with ownership and responsibility from day one
* You get to shape both processes and ways of working
* You have the opportunity to grow into future roles as the company scales


**Benefits At Stegra**
 At Stegra, you’ll join a company where your work has real impact on the climate, the industry, and future generations. We’re building a culture rooted in safety, trust, and inclusion, where people feel supported to grow and thrive.
   

  

 You’ll receive fair, competitive compensation aligned with collective agreements, along with benefits like up to 30 days of paid vacation, occupational pension, parental benefits, and insurance. You can also enjoy perks like subsidized gym memberships and bike leases.
   

  

**Read More About Benefits At Stegra Here.**
**This is Stegra**
 Stegra is on a mission to change the global steel industry by producing green hydrogen, iron, and steel - with the goal of eliminating CO₂ emissions. Instead of coal, we use green hydrogen and fossil-free electricity, meaning our primary emissions will be water and heat.
   

  

 By 2030, our ambition is to produce 5 million tonnes of green steel annually in our fully integrated, digitized, and sustainable plant in Boden, Northern Sweden - currently under construction. But this is just the beginning. Our expertise in green hydrogen will enable us to decarbonize industries beyond steel, paving the way for a cleaner future.
   

  

**Our Stockholm Office**
 Our modern Stockholm office is located on Norra Stationsgatan, close to Karolinska University Hospital in central Stockholm. Here, you’ll find a bright, collaborative workspace where cross-functional teams connect, share ideas, and drive progress. Whether you're visiting regularly or working hybrid, you'll be part of a vibrant office culture with easy access to the city’s energy and amenities.
   

  

*Founded in 2020, Stegra (formerly H2 Green Steel) was created with the purpose to decarbonize hard-to-abate industry. Construction of our first project – an integrated plant producing green hydrogen, green iron and green steel – is well underway in Boden, Sweden. Slashing emissions using green hydrogen and renewable electricity, we’re starting a clean industrial revolution. Steel is just the beginning.
   

  

 We are building a diverse and rapidly growing team spread across our site and offices in Boden and Stockholm, targeting close to 1500 employees by the end of 2025. Together, we focus on delivering green impact – for our people, customers, investors, society and planet.*

title: Agile Practice Lead
description:
**Are you our new Agile Practice Lead?** 




  





**As a market-leading credit management company, Intrum has a broad competence in debt collection services and we do business in 20 countries in Europe. We have more than 10,000 experienced employees in our group.** 




  





 Our business is about helping others. Helping indebted individuals to get out of difficult situations. Helping businesses get paid so they can grow, hire and prosper. Which in turn is positive for the entire economy. You become an essential part of this circle with us, which gives your job at Intrum a deeper meaning. Regardless of your role or title
 **.** 




**Being at the forefront is everyone's job.** 
 As the market leader, it is up to us to be at the forefront. Shaping the industry. Setting new standards. And wherever in the world your Intrum career path takes you, whatever role you play, you will contribute to growth, innovation and impact that makes a real difference.
 



**To have the opportunity to grow.** 
 In our organization there are many opportunities to advance, specialize and rotate. Or even try your wings abroad. It's up to you to make it happen. The fact that our business is about helping others – individuals and companies, means that your work also develops on a more personal level. Simply put, you will grow by making a difference.
 



  





**The role** 




**The role will report to Head of TMO** 




**Location for this position is Stockholm (Sickla)** 




 Hybrid work. We see that hybrid work, where we as employees can work either from home or from the office, creates opportunities to perform and work effectively in virtual and real environments.
 



  





**Purpose of role** 




 In this role, you will shape and elevate the Agile ways of working across the entire Swedish practice, guiding how nine Agile teams collaborate, deliver, and continuously improve. You’ll lead the adoption of Lean‑Agile ways of working - grounded in SAFe, Scrum, or similar frameworks while building the structures, behaviours, and culture that enable teams to deliver value faster and with greater clarity. You’ll strengthen cross–value stream collaboration, remove delivery friction, and help teams make smarter, data‑driven decisions. Working closely with leaders and teams, you’ll identify improvement opportunities, drive change, and raise Agile maturity across the organization. As a strategic partner to senior leadership, you’ll influence how the organization scales, prioritizes, and evolves its ways of working to meet future needs.
 



  





**Responsibilities of role** 




  





 Establish and Lead Agile Ways of Working across the Swedish Practice:
 


* Own and continuously evolve the Agile Operating Model, drawing on SAFe, Scrum, and Lean‑Agile principles.
* Drive consistent adoption of Lean‑Agile principles across teams, leaders, and value streams.
* Define and refine governance structures, roles, ceremonies, and metrics that enable scaled agility.
* Measure and report on Agile maturity and transformation progress.



 Lead and coordinate the virtual Lean‑Agile Centre of Excellence (LACE)
 


* Lead the LACE as the central hub for Agile standards, training, capability building, and continuous improvement.
* Develop and maintain Agile playbooks, guidelines, and best‑practice assets.
* Establish and facilitate Communities of Practice for Product Owners & Team Coaches.



 Orchestrate Cadence and Synchronization across value streams:
 


* Plan and facilitate key scaled‑agile ceremonies and cross-team synchronization events.
* Ensure alignment on dependencies, risks, priorities, and delivery commitments across value streams.



 Coach Teams and Leaders in Doing and Being Agile
 


* Provide hands-on coaching to Product Owners, Team Coaches and Agile teams to strengthen execution and collaboration.
* Mentor leaders in Lean-Agile leadership behaviors that foster empowerment, flow, and continuous improvement.



  





**Your Background** 




  




* 5–10 years of experience in Agile environments, ideally in roles such as RTE, Scrum Master, Product Owner, or Product Manager.
* Experience leading Agile or Lean‑Agile transformations at scale.
* Strong coaching capability with experience guiding both teams and senior leaders through change.
* Hands‑on experience facilitating large‑scale planning and cross‑team synchronization.
* Deep understanding of Agile frameworks (SAFe, Scrum, Kanban) and Lean/DevOps principles.
* Excellent facilitation, communication, and stakeholder‑management skills.
* Strong analytical and problem‑solving abilities.



  





**Who are you** 




 You are a hands-on change driver who knows how to turn Agile principles into real, everyday practice. You bring clarity and structure into fast‑moving, complex environments and stay steady when priorities shift. With a people‑first mindset and a strong coaching approach, you elevate teams and leaders to collaborate smarter and deliver real, measurable impact. Most importantly, you’re on the ground with the teams - removing obstacles, driving momentum, and making meaningful change happen where it matters most
 



  





**You act according to our values. Our values are not words. They are everything.** 




 Behind our global expansion and major digital transformation are four simple but powerful values. Ethics. Devotion. Solutions. Empathy. It is the values that drive our everyday life, regardless of how big we become. Others may think they are small things. But for us, they are everything.
 



  





**Are you curious or have questions?** 




 You are welcome to contact the recruitment manager for more information: Simon Wazel.
 



 Only applications received through our online system will be considered.

title: Field Sales Agent
description:
**Job Description**
 Why join us in 2026?
   

  

 Think of the digital tools we use daily - from virtual shopping to contactless payments. They're shaping our future. Join us in influencing the future of digital payments in an innovative environment. With our global merger, the opportunities for personal growth and collaboration are immense.
   

  

 If you join us in the role of Field sales agent, this is what you can expect from us :
   

  

* Attractive salary and an overall competitive package
* Well-structured training for the job and constant support from your manager
* Hybrid way of working
* Home equipment allowance
* Equipment needed for the role (laptop and mobile phone, including monthly mobile phone subscription)
* Private health insurance
* Sponsored sports activities
* Referral bonuses for recommending new team members to join the company


**About Your Future Role**
 As a Field Sales Agent at Nexi, you will play a crucial role in promoting our digital payment solutions to new and existing clients. Working in a dynamic and fast-paced environment, you will collaborate with a dedicated sales team and contribute to the growth of our business in the region. This position is based in Stockholm, with opportunities for travel and client-facing interactions.
   

  

 Your key responsibilities in this role will be:
   

  

* Identify and engage new business opportunities within the assigned territory
* Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and key stakeholders
* Present and demonstrate Nexi’s payment solutions to clients
* Achieve sales targets and contribute to the overall success of the team
* Provide market feedback and insights to help shape future product offerings
* Manage sales processes from prospecting to closing deals
* Collaborate closely with internal teams to ensure seamless customer experience


 What makes you the best person for this job?
   

  

 As you read about the role, are you eager to learn more? If so, review the qualifications that fit you for the position. Remember, beyond your skills and experience, it's your potential that sets you up for success in your daily tasks.
   

  

 Qualifications needed to be successful in this role:
   

  

* Proven experience in field sales or similar customer-facing roles
* Strong communication and negotiation skills
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team
* Self-motivated with a results-driven approach
* Fluent in Swedish and proficiency in English
* Drivers license and car


 Additional skills and experience that would make you stand out:
   

  

* Experience in the digital payments or financial technology sector
* Previous experience in B2B sales
* Strong networking and relationship-building skills


 Life at Nexi
   

  

 Discover more about us and what it's like to be part of Nexi by visiting this page: https://fa-ewwx-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX\_1/jobs/pre… . Learn about our values, meet our communities, and see how we are shaping the future of digital payments together.
   

  

 Apply now!
   

  

 If this opportunity resonates with you, we'd love to hear from you!
   

  

 Please apply at your earliest convenience. We'll be reviewing applications continuously and closing the position once we've found the right fit. We are committed to creating a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life can feel like they truly belong.
   

  

 By exploring this job opening, you're one step closer to joining us. We're excited to learn more about you!

title: Postdoc (1+1 year scholarship) in Molecular Genomics
description:
**Postdoc (1+1 year scholarship) in Molecular Genomics** 

 KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm has grown to become one of Europe’s leading technical and engineering universities, as well as a key Centre of intellectual talent and innovation. We are Sweden’s largest technical research and learning institution and home to students, researchers and faculty from around the world. Our research and education covers a wide area including natural sciences and all branches of engineering, as well as architecture, industrial management, urban planning, history and philosophy
   




**The workplace** 

**The research will be conducted at:** 




 KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Department of Gene Technology, Campus Solna, Stockholm.
 



 Science for Life Laboratory (SciLifeLab), Campus Solna, Stockholm.
 



 Laboratory of Assist. Prof. Anniina Vihervaara (Molecular Genomics lab).
 



 Department of Gene Technology: 
 



 www.kth.se/gte/gene-technology-1.783283
 



 Vihervaara laboratory: 
 



 https://www.scilifelab.se/fellows/anniina-vihervaara/
 



 Please note that the position is financed as 1 + 1 year stipend from Carl Tryggers Foundation.
 



**www.carltryggersstiftelse.se/this-is-the-carl-trygger-foundation/**



**Project description** 

 We are seeking for a highly motivated postdoctoral researcher to investigate mechanisms of mitochondrial RNA synthesis during stress and differentiation. The postdoc position is fully funded as 1 + 1 year stipend from Carl Tryggers Foundation. The project focuses on mitochondrial responses and RNA synthesis upon cell stress, differentiation, and disease. In the Molecular Genomics lab, we use and develop methods that track RNA synthesis across the genome with nucleotide resolution. Monitoring coordination of RNA Polymerases is coupled to analysis of chromatin organization and cellular processes. The project focuses on investigating how mitochondrial RNA polymerase (mtPol) is regulated, how the mitochondrial genome is transcriptionally organized, and how transcriptional responses are coordinated between the nucleus (RNA polymerases I–III) and mitochondria (mtPol).
 



 We primarily seek a postdoc who has prior experience in mitochondrial research and (or) cell state transitions, such as cellular stress responses, differentiation, drug responses or disease. Strong expertise in genomics and cell biology is expected. Applications are welcomed from candidates with wet-lab and bioinformatics backgrounds alike; ability to combine both is considered an advantage.
   




**Qualifications** 

**Requirements,** 



* A doctoral degree or an equivalent foreign degree, obtained within the last three years prior to the application deadline.
* According to the instructions from the Carl Tryggers Foundation, the doctoral degree must have been obtained at a university other than KTH.
* Documented experience in mitochondrial research, genomics, cell biology or molecular biology.



**Personal qualities:** 



* Strong collaborative skills
* Motivated and self-driven
* Ability to analyze and work with complex research questions
* Professional and ethical research approaches
* Support for equality, diversity and equal opportunities



**Meriting:** 



* Published articles in mitokondrial research
* Published articles describing RNA synthesis, including mechanisms of Pol I-III, mtPol and RdRP.
* Published articles in molecular biology and/or genomics.
* Experience with microscopy and spatial analysis of cells, tissues and/or small organisms
* DNA/RNA FISH (fluresent insitu hybridisering) and high resolution microscopy
* Computational genomics, such as RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, Cut&RUN, Hi-C



 We place great emphasis on personal skills.
 



 The final project will be formed together will the postdoctoral researcher.
   




**Application** 

 Apply to this scholarship by e-mail to 
 



 viher@kth.se
 



 . You are the main responsible to ensure that your application is complete according to the ad. Your complete application
 **must be received at KTH no later than 2026-06-05** 
 . 
 **Mark your application with reference number PA-2026-1239. For easier handling of the applications, please include “Carl Tryggers postdoc” in the email heading.** 




 The application must include the following documents:
 


* Curriculum vitae
* Cover letter (detailing both your previous scientific work experience and your interest in this position)
* Research statement (max 2 pages). Here, please feel free to describe how your background fits to research on mitochondrial RNA synthesis.
* Contact information for 1-3 references.



*Please observe that you apply for a scholarship, not an employment.* 




  





 It may be the case that a scholarshipcandidate at KTH is classified as a security-sensitive role in accordance with the Protective Security Act (2018:585). If this applies to the specific position, a security clearance will be conducted for the applicant in accordance with the same law with the applicant's consent. In such cases, a prerequisite for scholarshipcandidate is that the applicant is approved following the security clearance.
   




**About the position/positions** 

 Period: Start date during 2026 (the 1+1 year funding must occur during 2026-2028)
 



 Extent: Full-time (for 1+1 year)
 



 Number of positions: 1
 



 scholarship: Carl Tryggers postdoctoral researcher
 



 Location: Campus Solna, Stockholm, Sweden
 



*EU Citizens on scholar or private funding need to bring a certificate showing EHIC insurance (a comprehensive medical- and health insurance) from the home country, which must cover during the full stay in Sweden.* 




*Without this certificate you may only remain three months in Sweden and will not be able to register as a residence in Sweden.* 




*The responsibility for the comprehensive insurance is strictly on the applicant.*



**Contact** 

 viher@kth.se
 



  





*We firmly decline all contact with staffing and recruitment agencies and job ad salespersons.*

title: Arkitekt/Senior Utvecklare Dynamics 365
description:
Vårt Dynamics 365-område växer kraftigt! Vi har ökad efterfrågan från både befintliga och nya kunder och söker nu dig som brinner för att designa och bygga moderna, värdeskapande lösningar i
 **Microsoft Dynamics 365** 
 och
 **Power Platform.** 




  





**Om rollen:** 




 Som
 **Dynamics 365 Arkitekt/Utvecklare** 
 hos oss får du en central och inflytelserik roll i kundprojekt. Du designar och utvecklar driftsäkra, integrerade lösningar som maximerar värdet av hela Microsofts ekosystem. Du arbetar nära kunden, agerar rådgivare och ser till att tekniken verkligen stödjer affärsprocesserna och skapar mätbar nytta.
 



 Du kommer att integrera Dynamics 365 med bland annat
 **Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, Azure, Power Apps, Power Automate** 
 och kundens övriga system - alltid med fokus på enkelhet, skalbarhet och långsiktig hållbarhet.
 



  





**Hos oss får du:** 



* Arbeta i ett riktigt duktigt, hjälpsamt och framåtlutat team
* Vara med i en snabbrörlig miljö med tydlig satsning på kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling
* Ta stort eget ansvar och påverka både lösningar och kundresultat
* Jobba med de senaste teknikerna inom Microsofts molnplattform
* Bidra till projekt som gör skillnad på riktigt – för samhället och miljön



  





**Vi söker dig som:** 



* Har
 **gedigen erfarenhet** 
 av att utveckla och arkitekturera lösningar i
 **Dynamics 365** 
 (särskilt CRM/ CE) och
 **Power Platform**
* Är stark inom
 **C#, .NET, SQL, WebAPI, Plugins, Dataverse**
* Har praktisk erfarenhet av integration och Azure-tjänster som
 **Azure Functions, API Management, Service Bus, Logic Apps** 
 m.m.
* Är van vid (eller nyfiken på)
 **Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power Virtual Agents, Power BI, Customer Insights**
* Har en god förståelse för hur teknik kan stötta och förbättra affärsprocesser
* Trivs i konsultrollen – är kommunikativ, trygg, rådgivande och kundfokuserad
* Har erfarenhet av digital transformation och förändringsledning



**Meriterande** 



* Högskole-/universitetsexamen inom systemvetenskap, datateknik, informationsteknik eller motsvarande
* Relevant
 **Microsoft-certifiering** 
 inom Dynamics 365 och/eller Power Platform
* Tidigare erfarenhet av rådgivande roller hos kunder i större transformationsprojekt



  





**Om Softronic:** 




 Vi är ett ledande nordiskt IT-bolag som stärker våra kunders IT-förmåga i en allt mer komplex digital värld. Med bred och djup kompetens hjälper vi organisationer att skapa stabila, säkra och moderna lösningar från applikationsutveckling och integration till infrastruktur, drift och säkerhet.
 



 Vi tar ett helhetsansvar för IT och arbetar långsiktigt, både med våra kunder och med varandra. Hos oss är gemenskap, engagemang och utveckling en självklar del av vardagen. När vi trivs och växer tillsammans skapar vi också större värde.
 



  





**Låter det intressant?** 
 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan! Har du några frågor är du varmt välkommen att kontakta mimmi.larsson@softronic.se

title: Head of Partnerships
description:
**About Curoflow** 




 Curoflow is building the operating system for European private healthcare. Our platform gives SME clinics everything they need to run their practice — from digital patient intake and video consultations to EHR, e-prescriptions, lab integrations, and AI-powered clinical support — in a single, CE-marked, GDPR-compliant solution.
 



  





 We are 25 people today, operating customers across 9 geographic markets and we are getting ready to scale to ~70 employees across 5 core markets — and beyond — within ~18 months.
 



  





**We Are Hiring Across the Organisation** 




 This job ad is one of many we are releasing simultaneously. We are building multiple teams in parallel — product, engineering, commercial, operations, and leadership — as we enter the most defining chapter in our company’s history.
 



  





 While we are publishing these roles now (May 2026), we expect most hires for this wave to begin working between September and November 2026. We will take the time we need to find the right person for each role. We would rather wait for the right fit than rush to fill a seat.
 



  





**What Kind of Person We Are Looking For** 




 Let’s keep it simple. Curoflow is a start-up that will be having growth pains — with everything that entails. You will be judged first and foremost by your character. If you are applying for this job thinking it is just any other job, you would be wrong.
 



  





 We will demand a growth mindset and someone who is deeply committed to our goals. You will be compensated accordingly, but we must stress that we are looking for the type of person whose motivation primarily comes from earning stock options and being part of building a great company.
 



 In return you will get that rare, once-in-a-lifetime opportunity that can mark a lifelong memory of what you were part of building.
 



  





**The Role: Head of Partnerships** 




 Curoflow’s growth strategy relies on three channels: inbound, self-service, and partnerships. You will own the third — and it is projected to grow from 10% to 24% of our total pipeline by 2030.
 



 As Head of Partnerships, you will build Curoflow’s partnership strategy and execution from the ground up. This means identifying, negotiating, and closing deals with EHR consultants, healthcare associations, integration partners, and distribution partners across multiple European markets.
 



 This is a hands-on leadership role — and we mean it. You will not manage a team from a distance. For the first 12+ months, you ARE the partnerships team. You will personally identify targets, make the calls, run the meetings, negotiate the terms, and close the deals. You will build the model by doing it yourself, and then scale it with the Partner Enablement Coordinator and Partner Managers who join after you. If you see 'Head of' and think 'strategy and delegation', this is not the right role. If you see it and think 'I get to build this from zero with my own hands', keep reading.
 



  





 You report to the CRO and work closely with the CEO, CMO and Country Managers.
 



  





**What You’ll Do** 




***First 6–12 Months***




 • Develop and present the partnership strategy and target list within 60 days of starting
 



 • Personally identify, approach, and close your first 3–5 large strategic partnerships: EHR consultants, healthcare associations, and integration partners
 



 • Build the partnership operating model: deal structures, partner onboarding, co-marketing frameworks, and revenue-sharing models
 



 • Work with the Partner Enablement & Ops Coordinator to create partner toolkits, training materials, and enablement programmes
 



 • Coordinate with Country Managers to identify and activate market-specific partners
 



 • Set up partner pipeline tracking in HubSpot and establish reporting cadence with the board
 



  





***As We Scale (12–24 Months)***




 • Grow partner-sourced leads to 15%+ of total pipeline by H1 2028
 



 • Close at least one white-label or co-branded deal with a major healthcare player
 



 • Build and lead the partnerships team
 



 • Scale partnership revenue contribution to 10+ MSEK contracted ARR by 2028
 



 • Expand partnership programme to new markets
 



  





**Who You Are** 




 • 7+ years in partnerships, business development, or channel sales, with at least 3 years building a partnership function in a SaaS or platform company
 



 • Proven track record of closing strategic partnerships that generated measurable revenue — not just logos on a partner page
 



 • Hands-on: you have personally negotiated and closed partnership deals, not just managed a team that did
 



 • Experience working across European markets with understanding of how partnership dynamics differ by country
 



 • Commercial mindset: you think in terms of pipeline contribution and revenue, not just relationship building
 



 • Strong communicator and negotiator; comfortable in boardrooms and at trade shows alike
 



 • Fluent English; Swedish or another Scandinavian language is a plus
 



  





**Preferably** 




 • Background in healthtech, medtech, or healthcare SaaS — you have existing relationships with EHR vendors, healthcare associations, or digital health players
 



 • Experience with distribution, reseller, or white-label partnership models
 



 • Network in the Nordic and European healthcare ecosystem that you can activate immediately
 



  





**Why This Role Matters** 




 Partnerships are how Curoflow reaches clinics we could never reach through marketing alone. The right partners will recommend Curoflow to thousands of clinics. You are the person who makes that happen.
 



  





**What We Offer** 




 • A seat at the leadership table from day one — direct involvement in shaping how Curoflow scales across Europe
 



 • The rare opportunity to join a company at its most pivotal moment, where your work has outsized impact
 



 • A product and mission that genuinely matters — we are making healthcare more accessible across Europe
 



 • Equity participation (stock options) post probation period, reflecting the strategic importance of every hire we make
 



 • A collaborative, low-ego culture where people care deeply about what they’re building
 



 • Expected start date: September–November 2026
 



  





**How to Apply** 




 Send your CV and a short cover letter explaining why this role excites you. We value substance over polish — tell us what you’ve built, not just where you’ve worked.
 



  





*FYI: We are hiring for many roles simultaneously and are committed to finding the right person for each one — not the fastest. Please bear with us if our response times are longer than usual; we promise every application will be reviewed.*

title: Agency & Client Manager - Digital
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for an
 **Agency & Client Manager - Digital** 
 to our
 **Stockholm office** 
 – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

 The role
   

  

 As an Agency & Client Manager - Digital, your primary responsibility is to manage, grow, and optimize Viaplay's digital ad sales business, focusing on programmatic advertising. You will work directly with media agencies, trading desks, and clients to drive revenue growth, improve campaign performance, and strengthen relationships with key stakeholders.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Build and manage relationships with media agencies, programmatic trading desks, and clients.
* Act as the main contact for digital advertising inquiries and campaign support.
* Ensure campaigns deliver on client objectives and KPIs.
* Analyze campaign data, optimize performance, and provide insights to clients.
* Identify upselling opportunities and support new business development.
* Collaborate with internal teams (ad operations, tech, and product).
* Participate in client presentations, pitches, and negotiations.
* Stay updated on trends in programmatic and digital advertising


 What we're looking for
   

  

 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* Experience with programmatic advertising platforms (DSPs, SSPs, ad exchanges).
* Good understanding of digital advertising and media buying (display, video, audio, OTT).
* Experience working with media agencies or advertisers.
* Familiarity with analytics and reporting tools (e.g., Google Analytics).
* Strong communication and analytical skills.


**It’s a bonus if you have:**
* Degree in Marketing, Business, Digital Media, or a related field.
* Interest in sports.


 What we offer you
   

  

**We’ve got you covered!** 
 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
   

  

**Your wellbeing matters.** 
 We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
   

  

**A safe space to grow and up-skill.** 
 Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
   

  

**Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do.** 
 So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including series & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.
   

  

 Hit play today
   

  

 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: PhD position in epigenetic and metabolic interplay
description:
****Do you want to contribute to top quality medical research?****




*To be a doctoral student means to devote oneself to a research project under supervision of experienced researchers and following an individual study plan. For a doctoral degree, the equivalent of four years of full-time doctoral education is required.*




****The Department****




 The Department of Laboratory Medicine has a strong translational focus and housing groups with leading expertise in tools spanning epigenomics and transcriptomics, functional experimental animal models, clinical samples and state-of-the-art knowledge in genome engineering and delivery. Overall ambition to address both fundamental clinically relevant questions in autoimmune conditions, monogenic diseases, and cancers.
   

  





****The research group****




 Metabolites and subcellular protein compartmentalization ultimately regulate epigenetic remodeling, transcription, and cell function. In metabolically highly active cells, this regulation can become disrupted since metabolites and proteins are simultaneously required in parallel but separate cellular compartments. The Carlström laboratory investigates the fundamental mechanisms underlying metabolic–epigenetic interplay and the role of subcellular protein compartmentalization. To do this, we use various tools, including iPSCs, primary cells, and animal models, with a specific focus on glia cells and their roles in regeneration and cancer.
   

  





 To unravel these intricate interactions, we employ a wide range of cutting-edge technologies for cell manipulation and high‑resolution profiling, helping to overcome the current lack of suitable methodologies in this emerging field.
   

  





****The doctoral student project and the duties of the doctoral student****




 We are looking for a highly motivated and enthusiastic candidate with a strong interest in protein biology, genome engineering tools and single-cell multi-omic profiling tools to study subcellular protein distibution, genome regulation, and basal molecular mechanisms with a focus on glial cell biology.
   

  





 Main responsibilities include:
 


* CRISPR-based genome/transcriptome/epigenome engineering and perturbation-screens
* Application of advanced single-cell multi-omic profiling technologies (incl Cut&Tag, ATAC-seq)  iPSC cell culturing
* Proteomics
* Molecular cloning, Viral transduction, Flow cytometry, Microscopy, and other molecular technology tools
* Sharing insights via internal/international presentations and peer-reviewed publications
* Supervision of BSc and MSc Writing reports, manuscripts, and documentation



****What do we offer?****




 A creative and inspiring environment full of expertise and curiosity. Karolinska Institutet is one of the world's leading medical universities. Our vision is to pursue the development of knowledge about life and to promote a better health for all. At Karolinska Institutet, we conduct successful medical research and hold the largest range of medical education in Sweden. As a doctoral student you are offered an individual research project, a well-educated supervisor, a vast range of elective courses and the opportunity to work in a leading research group. Karolinska Institutet collaborates with prominent universities from all around the world, which ensures opportunities for international exchanges. You will be employed on a doctoral studentship which means that you receive a contractual salary. Employees also have access to our modern gym for free and receive reimbursements for medical care.
   

  





****Eligibility requirements for doctoral education****




 In order to participate in the selection for a doctoral position, you must meet the following general
 **(A)** 
 and specific
 **(B)** 
 eligibility requirements at latest by the application deadline.
   

  





 It is your responsibility to certify eligibility by following the instructions on the web page Entry requirements (eligibility) for doctoral education.
   

  





**A) General eligibility requirement**




 You meet the general eligibility requirement for doctoral/third-cycle/PhD education if you:
 


* have been awarded a second-cycle/advanced/master qualification (i.e. master degree),
 **or**
* have satisfied the requirements for courses comprising at least 240 credits of which at least 60 credits were awarded in the advanced/second-cycle/master level,
 **or**
* have acquired substantially equivalent knowledge in some other way in Sweden or abroad.\*



 Follow the instructions on the web page Entry requirements (eligibility) for doctoral education.
   

  





 \*If you claim equivalent knowledge, follow the instructions on the web page Assessing equivalent knowledge for general eligibility for doctoral education.
   

  





  
   

  





**B) Specific eligibility requirement**




 You meet the specific eligibility requirement for doctoral/third-cycle/PhD education if you:
   

  





 - Show proficiency in English equivalent to the course English B/English 6 at Swedish upper secondary school.
   

  





 Follow the instructions on the web page English language requirements for doctoral education.
   

  





*Verification of your documents* 
 Karolinska Institutet checks the authenticity of your documents. Karolinska Institutet reserves the right to revoke admission if supporting documents are discovered to be fraudulent. Submission of false documents is a violation of Swedish law and is considered grounds for legal action.
   

  





**(A)** 
 and
 **(B)** 
**can only** 
 be certified by the documentation requirement for doctoral education.
   

  





****Skills and personal qualities****




 We welcome applications from candidates with previous training in Molecular Biology, Biomedicine, Computational Biology, Bioinformatics, or other related fields. The successful candidate is a curious, highly motivated, and responsible scientist with excellent communication and organizational skills who enjoys working with people and take pride in delivering work of good quality.
   

  





 The work requires:
   

  





 Expertise in various molecular biology techniques including Experience with human induced pluripotent stem cells and organoids, genome engineering, and sc-omic approaches are a merit. Experience in Computational Biology e.g. single cell multiomics analysis is a merit. Proficiency in English and excellent communication skills.
   

  





 The applicant must be able to integrate well into the international and multidisciplinary environment. Personal suitability is therefore of great importance.
   

  





****Terms and conditions****




 The doctoral student will be employed on a doctoral studentship maximum 4 years full-time.
   

  





****Application process****




 Submit your application and supporting documents through the Varbi recruitment system. Use the button in the top right corner and follow the instructions. We prefer that your application is written in English, but you can also apply in Swedish.
   

  





 Your application must contain the following documents:
   

  





 - A motivation letter (<1page)
   

  





 - Contact information for ≥2 references
   

  





 - Curriculum vitae (containing degree projects, previous publications and current position)
   

  





 - Any other documentation showing the desirable skills and personal qualities described above
   

  





 - Documents certifying your general eligibility (see 
 **A** 
  above)
   

  





 - Documents certifying your specific eligibility (see 
 **B** 
  above)
   

  





****Selection****




 A selection will be made among eligible applicants on the basis of the ability to benefit from doctoral education. The qualifications of the applicants will be evaluated on an overall basis.
   

  





 Karolinska Institutet uses the following bases of assessment:
   

  





 - Documented subject knowledge of relevance to the area of research
   

  





 - Analytical skill
   

  





 - Other documented knowledge or experience that may be relevant to doctoral studies in the subject.
   

  





 All applicants will be informed when the recruitment is completed.
   

  





*carlstromlab.com*




****Want to make a difference? Join us and contribute to better health for all****

title: Global Mobility Manager
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**Global Mobility Manager**
 At Legora, People Operations are on a journey. We are building scalable, people-centred practices that support our growth as an international company and create great experiences for our employees. Global mobility is a key part of this journey, and we are now looking for a Global Mobility Specialist to help us further develop and operate this area.
   

  

 In this role, you will work hands-on with cross-border mobility cases while contributing to the continued shaping of Legora’s global mobility framework, processes, and ways of working. You will collaborate closely with People Operations, leaders, Finance, Legal, and external partners to ensure compliant, smooth, and employee-friendly mobility across markets.
   

  

**You Will**
**What you will do**
* Manage end-to-end global mobility cases, including relocations, secondments, and short- and long-term international assignments
* Own the administrative and operational execution of mobility cases, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and a high-quality employee experience
* Advise People Partners, leaders, and employees on mobility-related questions, providing clear guidance aligned with policies, best practices, and business needs
* Coordinate and collaborate with external vendors (relocation, immigration, tax advisors) to ensure effective delivery and follow-up
* Monitor changes in immigration, tax, and social security regulations and assess potential impacts on Legora’s mobility setup
* Contribute to the ongoing development and improvement of global mobility policies, processes, and guidelines to support a growing organization
* Maintain mobility data and reporting to support transparency, insights, and informed decision-making


**Who You Are**
 You are an People Operations professional with experience in global mobility who enjoys working close to the details while understanding the bigger picture. You are structured, service-oriented, and comfortable taking ownership of operational work while contributing to improvements over time.
   

  

**We Believe You**
* Have hands-on experience in global mobility, international assignments, or cross-border employment
* Have experience from an international organization, scale-up, or advisory/consulting environment
* Are comfortable owning both case work and administrative processes end-to-end


 Communicate clearly and build trust with employees and stakeholders across functions and levels
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Projekt Manager to Karolinska University Hospital
description:
Do you want to contribute to an international collaboration that makes a real difference? Are you motivated by coordinating initiatives and supporting healthcare development across borders? We are now looking for a Project Manager to join an important collaboration with Ukrainian partners.
   

  

**You are offered**
* a unique opportunity to contribute to an international healthcare collaboration
* meaningful work supporting Ukrainian colleagues during challenging times
* a coordinating role with broad contact surfaces and varied tasks
* the possibility to develop your skills within project management and international cooperation
* a flexible part-time position (50%) with potential extension


**About The Role**
 Karolinska University Hospital is engaged in an important collaboration with Ukrainian healthcare partners. The project aims to support knowledge exchange, coordination, and development initiatives during an ongoing challenging period.
   

  

 As a Project Manager, you will play a key role in coordinating activities and maintaining communication between stakeholders. You will manage multiple parallel initiatives, support planning and follow-up of project activities, and ensure smooth collaboration between involved parties.
   

  

 The role includes close interaction with both internal teams and international partners, where structure, communication, and coordination are central parts of your daily work.
   

  

 The position is a fixed-term employment from August 2026 to December 2026, with the possibility of extension
   

  

**We Are Looking For Someone Who**
* is flexible and able to adapt to changing needs and priorities
* has strong relationship-building skills and enjoys working with diverse stakeholders
* is structured, communicative, and skilled at coordinating multiple activities simultaneously
* has strong planning and organizational skills


 Great importance will be placed on personal suitability.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 Requirements:
   

  

* Good knowledge of standard office and project management tools
* Ukrainian: Native or mother-tongue level
* English: Professional working proficiency


**Meritorious**
* Swedish language skills
* Background in healthcare or a medical environment
* Experience working in international projects, healthcare collaborations, or humanitarian programs


**About The Recruitment Process**
 Selection and interviews may be conducted on an ongoing basis during the application period.
   

  

 In connection with your application, you are required to submit your CV. Instead of a cover letter, we ask you to answer a set of screening questions and briefly describe why you are a good fit for the role. Completing these questions is required for your application to be considered complete.
   

  

 Apply today and join Karolinska!
   

  

 Inför tillsättning av befattning som innefattar vård av barn och ungdom, kontrolleras den som erbjuds tjänsten mot misstanke- och belastningsregistret. Här kan du läsa mer om rekryteringsprocessen och om vilka kontroller som utförs på Karolinska.
   

  

 Som anställd på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset kan du komma att krigsplaceras. Läs mer om vad det innebär här.
   

  

 Om du är eller tidigare har varit anställd i Region Stockholm kommer vi att ta interna referenser från din nuvarande eller tidigare chef, om du blir aktuell för anställning.
   

  

 För tillsvidaretjänster kan provanställning komma att tillämpas.
   

  

**Om Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset**
 Vi på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset är stolta över att vi är ett av världens främsta universitetssjukhus. Förutom vårt särskilda ansvar för den högspecialiserade vården i Stockholmsregionen är huvuduppdraget att tillsammans med Karolinska Institutet utbilda framtidens vårdanställda och bedriva världsledande forskning samtidigt som vi fortsätter vårt arbete med att utveckla vården för patientens bästa. Vi arbetar inom en rad olika områden och professioner.
   

  

 Vår vision - Vi ska bota och lindra imorgon det ingen kan bota och lindra idag.
   

  

 Läs gärna mer om oss på karolinska.se och följ oss på sociala medier!
   

  

**Kommunikation**
 För att stödja sjukhusets alla kliniska verksamheter arbetar jurister, ekonomer, kommunikatörer, HR, Inköpare och IT för att vi tillsammans ska generera vård i världsklass och vara en verkligt Attraktiv Arbetsgivare! Här kan du läsa mer om oss.
   

  

**Att söka jobb i Region Stockholm**
 Vi eftersträvar jämställdhet och jämlikhet på vår arbetsplats och ser gärna sökande med olika bakgrund och förutsättningar.
   

  

 Vi tar endast emot ansökningar via detta system. Ansök genom att klicka på knappen ”Ansök”. Ansökningar per brev eller e-post beaktas inte. Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.
   

  

 Vårt rekryteringssystem kan inte hantera anonyma ansökningar eller sökande med skyddade personuppgifter. Om du har skyddade personuppgifter ber vi dig att kontakta den kontaktpersonen som finns angiven i annonsen. Din ansökan kommer då att hanteras utanför rekryteringssystemet. Du bör även vara försiktig med vilken information du lämnar i din ansökan och endast ta med information som är relevant för den aktuella befattningen.
   

  

 Region Stockholm ansvarar för hälso- och sjukvård, kollektivtrafik, regional utveckling och bidrar till kulturlivet. Varje dag, dygnet runt. I landets snabbaste växande region. Tillsammans skapar vi Europas attraktivaste storstadsregion.
   

  

 Läs mer om hur det är att jobba hos oss i Region Stockholm (öppnas i nytt fönster)

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 As
 **FP&A Manager** 
 at Legora, you will play a central role in strengthening and scaling our financial planning capabilities as we grow globally. You will join a high-performing finance team and help deepen how we budget, forecast, model scenarios, and measure performance across revenue, opex, and headcount.
   

  

 This is a hands-on, high-impact role: you will improve existing foundations while building new structures as complexity increases. Your work will shape capital allocation, hiring plans, product investment decisions, and international expansion. This role is about turning ambition into numbers we trust, then using those numbers to drive clearer decisions. The quality of our planning and performance visibility will directly influence how efficiently and intelligently we scale.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Run and continuously improve our company-wide budgeting and rolling forecast processes
* Build and refine scalable financial models that reflect the underlying economic drivers of the business
* Develop long-range planning and scenario frameworks to support strategic and operational decisions
* Help define KPI structures and dashboards that make performance transparent across teams
* Partner with leaders on pricing, hiring plans, product investments, and market expansion decisions
* Identify risks and opportunities early, and translate them into clear, actionable recommendations
* Drive automation and systems improvements across forecasting and reporting workflows


**Who You Are**
* You have experience improving or scaling budgeting and forecasting processes, ideally in a growing company
* You bring advanced financial modeling skills, including scenario planning and sensitivity analysis
* You combine analytical depth with sound commercial judgment, and you connect numbers to decisions
* You are effective partnering with cross-functional leaders and supporting decision-making at pace
* You communicate clearly and move from insights to actions and follow-through
* You show strong analytical judgment and attention to detail, and you raise the bar for financial rigor while staying pragmatic


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience.  We’re hiring a Global Payroll Specialist to help build and scale payroll as Legora continues to grow internationally. Working closely with our Global Payroll Manager, you’ll help ensure payroll runs smoothly across multiple countries while also shaping the processes, controls, and ways of working that will support our continued expansion.
   

  

 This is not a role where you’ll spend most of your time processing payroll yourself. Instead, you’ll coordinate with external payroll providers, review and quality-check their work, and help improve how we work as we enter new markets. As the function develops, there will also be room to shape parts of the role based on your strengths and interests.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Coordinate monthly payroll cycles across multiple countries
* Act as a key contact for external payroll providers
* Make sure payroll is delivered accurately and on time
* Carry out validations, reconciliations, and checks before payroll is finalized
* Help ensure payroll follows local regulations and internal processes
* Support audits by keeping documentation clear and up to date
* Help build and improve payroll processes as we grow into new countries
* Create clear workflows and documentation so our ways of working can scale
* Work with payroll data from HiBob and ensure inputs are accurate and complete
* Partner closely with HR, Finance, and external vendors
* Step in as backup to the Global Payroll Manager when needed


**Who You Are**
 What matters most is your mindset: you are curious, eager to keep learning, and excited by the opportunity to grow in a global payroll role. We are looking for someone who brings structure, ownership, and a willingness to figure things out in a fast-moving environment.
   

  

**You Also Bring**
* 5+ years of payroll experience
* Strong understanding of payroll controls and processes
* Detail-oriented with strong analytical skills
* Comfortable working with HRIS and payroll systems
