title: System Engineer
description:
*Are you ready to shape the future of mining equipment systems in a rapidly evolving industry? Join our dynamic team as a System Engineer and play a crucial role in optimizing the performance, reliability, and efficiency of the complete machine system integrated into our products.*  



At Epiroc, innovation is at the core of everything we do. Our vision is clear: we aim to accelerate the transformation of mining towards a greener and more sustainable industry. In this role, you’ll get the opportunity to work with state-of-the-art mobile machines, electric drivetrains, and control systems that are moving towards higher degrees of automation and sophistication.  


**Join our team**

Our aspiration is to produce the world’s greenest machines using the world’s greenest materials. We are leading the charge towards a more sustainable future through battery-electric, zero-emission mining equipment. Are you ready to be part of this journey? Join our fantastic team of engineers and play a pivotal role in advancing mining technology while pushing boundaries on performance, reliability, and efficiency.  


**Your mission**

Your mission as a system engineer is to develop and adapt our machine systems in terms of hardware, system architecture, and functionality to fulfill requirements regarding e.g., performance, availability, and safety. Drive concept development from ideation through requirements decomposition and specification, including trade-off studies, to design, implement, and execute verification and validation of the technical solution, ensuring an efficient and traceable end-to-end development loop.

You will primarily work in project form with new product development projects, product maintenance, technology development, and customer orders. You will have close contact with other departments in Örebro, for example service, purchasing and marketing, as well as contact with customers and suppliers around the world. Furthermore, you will contribute to the building of modularized solutions, process improvement, and team competence assets.  


**Your profile**

You have a university degree in a relevant area. We believe you have professional experience within system engineering or other highly qualified engineering background. You are used to bringing ideas from concept to serial production and are familiar with requirement management tools such as Doors or Polarion or similar. You have proven experience in modularization and systems engineering practices such as MBSE (Model based system engineering), and hands-on experience with Matlab tools and Hopsan. You also have a solid understanding of software, including control systems and system architectures, as well as CAN communication protocols (J1939, CAN Open). Knowledge of software development and electrical systems, and experience with CAD, simulation, and other software tools are considered a plus.

To enjoy the work you are self-propelled, responsible, and your work is effectively planned to meet deadlines with the right quality. You have a structured and analytical approach, enjoy combining theory and practice, and strive to get the job done to our customers' satisfaction. Furthermore, you have good interpersonal skills, enjoy teamwork, and thrive in a collaborative environment where communication is key. Technical project management experience or R&D leadership experience is a plus. Since your work includes many local and global contacts, it is required that English skills (verbal and written) are fluent and that you understand Swedish.  


**Life at Epiroc**

By joining our team, you can expect an atmosphere of creativity, innovation, and workplace diversity. You will be a part of a group of skilled and helpful colleagues who live by our core values: Collaboration, Commitment, and Innovation. We work in a global environment with 120 different nationalities!

In addition to the fact that we have a culture that is characterized by development combined with having a good balance between work and leisure, there are some things that makes us a little extra proud to work at Epiroc:  


* Global career opportunities
* Epiroc University, for your own competence development
* Community involvement
* Benefits package, which amongst other things include flexible working hours and bonus.

  



Life at Epiroc can include the possibility for a hybrid workplace. It is a way of working that offers flexibility to partly work from home, based on your role, responsibilities, and individual conditions.  


**Location and travel**

This position is located in Örebro, Sweden.  


**Application and contact**

We encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible by creating an account in our recruitment system. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position may be filled before last application date. The last day to apply for this position is May 25th, 2026.  



Hiring manager: Xi Yang, Group Manager R&D,

For questions about the recruitment process or application please contact recruitment specialist:

Maria Roseen, +46702590507, maria.x.roseen@external.epiroc.com,  


**About Epiroc (automatically added in HR Link)**

Epiroc is a global productivity partner for mining and construction customers and accelerates the transformation toward a sustainable society. With ground-breaking technology, Epiroc develops and provides innovative and safe equipment, such as drill rigs, rock excavation and construction equipment and tools for surface and underground applications. The company also offers world-class service and other aftermarket support as well as solutions for automation, digitalization, and electrification. Epiroc is based in Stockholm, Sweden, had revenues of more than SEK 60 billion in 2023, and has around 18 200 passionate employees supporting and collaborating with customers in around 150 countries. Learn more at www.epirocgroup.com.  

**It all starts with people.** The world needs metals and minerals for the energy transition and our cities and infrastructure must be developed to serve a growing population. To succeed, we need to speed up the shift towards more sustainable mining and construction industries. We at Epiroc accelerate this transformation, together with customers and business partners in more than 150 countries, by developing and providing innovative and safe equipment, digital solutions, and aftermarket support.
All new thinkers are welcome. We are looking for those who want to develop, grow, and dare to think new. In Epiroc we attract, develop, and retain diverse talent valuing authenticity and unique perspectives, driving our spirit of innovation. We foster an inclusive culture where diversity isn't just a goal but a part of our values and way of working. This is how we do business for a sustainable future. Learn more at www.epiroc.com

title: Technical Strategist - Energy
description:
At Toyota Material Handling Europe, we create the technology that keeps the world moving. Diverse businesses across Europe depend on our logistics and material handling solutions. By pioneering technology like automation, connectivity and productivity solutions, we enable our customers' logistics operations to be as efficient as possible.


We firmly believe in setting ideas in motion. At Toyota Material Handling Europe, we are over 13,500 colleagues passionate about supporting companies of all sizes with todays and tomorrow’s material handling challenges. Because we know that our business and our industry are essential and sometimes even critical for them, for daily life and society at large.


In our respective headquarters in Mjölby, Sweden, Willebroek, Belgium, in our Sales companies across Europe, or in our factories in France, Italy and Sweden, you can be part of an extraordinary journey. And together, we will Move the world towards easy and sustainable.

  

The Position


We are seeking a highly skilled and versatile Senior Engineer / Technical Strategist to act as a crucial link between our Research & Innovation (R&I) and Research & Development (R&D) teams. This role is perfect for an individual who thrives on understanding the big picture and shaping technical direction while remaining deeply connected to technology. You will be instrumental in ensuring that cutting-edge Energy technologies are strategically integrated into the strategic technology roadmap. It is verry important that you will have deep knowledge in the Energy storage arena both technical but also with commercial and customer. You have visited and worked with customers and understand the need from the customer when they use Energy solutions.


You will work closely with the R&I and R&D organization to prioritize technology development and advance the TRL (Technology Readiness Level) before introduction into production. The position could be based in either Mjölby or Gothenburg.


Your Responsibilities

* Translate strategic R&I concepts into concrete development plans and technical roadmaps.
* Lead and contribute to the development of Minimum Viable Products (MVPs), offering hands-on support and deep product knowledge.
* Analyze architectures, trends and competitors.
* Serve as the key technical link between R&I researchers and R&D engineers, ensuring smooth communication and knowledge transfer.
* Provide critical input to system design to ensure scalability, reliability, and maintainability.
* Stay current with trends in Energy, evaluating new technologies for their impact and relevance to our products.

  


Your Profile


We’re looking for an experienced and forward-thinking candidate - someone who not only understands innovation but knows how to turn it into real, working solutions. You have a strong track record of driving ideas from concept to successful implementation, and you thrive at the intersection of strategy, technology, and product development.

* Extensive experience in technology roles, with a proven ability to lead innovation from early-stage concepts all the way to launched products.
* Deep understanding of current and emerging technologies, particularly in area, like Charing, Energy storage solution and Cell technologies.
* Strong background in R&D or product development, with hands-on experience bringing ideas to life and introducing successful products to the market.
* Experience in the Energy domain, both hardware and software. Preferable experience in Energy solutions in electrical vessels.
* Familiarity with R&D methodologies, along with the ability to evaluate vendors and build reliable, scalable solutions.
* A bachelor’s degree in computer science, Computer or Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or a related field is required; a master’s degree is a strong plus.
* Vast experience of relevant professional experience.


You’re also someone who:

* Communicates complex technical ideas clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
* Thinks strategically and can connect business goals with the right technical approach.
* Keeps the customer at the center of every decision.


Our Offer


At Toyota Material Handling Europe, we don’t just offer jobs—we offer careers with purpose. In our dynamic, diverse, and international environment, we offer a workplace where you can truly thrive and bring your ideas to life.


We’re committed to supporting you with an attractive benefits package, including a yearly bonus and flexible work arrangements that allow you to balance your personal and professional life. With clear goals and direction from senior leadership, you’ll also find abundant opportunities for career growth and development within the company.


Our organisational support ensures that you can maintain a healthy work-life balance while pursuing meaningful work that shapes the future of our industry. We believe learning never stops. Whether you’re developing new skills or leading ground-breaking projects, this is a place where continuous growth is celebrated. Collaboration is at the heart of everything we do, and we take pride in fostering a supportive, open environment where every voice is heard. If you’re ready to MOVE the future with us, we’d love to hear from you!


Your Application


Send your application, CV, and Cover letter in Swedish or English, no later than the 24th of May, 2026.


For more information regarding the recruitment process, please contact: Patrik


Schultzén, Talent Acquisition Specialist, Patrik.schultzen@toyota-industries.eu


#EUROPE

title: Software Sustainment Engineer – Customer Case Management
description:
**Bli en del av något helt livsavgörande**


Att arbeta på Cytiva inom Life Science industrin innebär att vara i framkanten när det gäller att utveckla nya lösningar för att förbättra människors hälsa. Våra kunders verksamhet är livsavgörande och inkluderar allt från grundläggande biologisk forskning till utveckling av innovativa vacciner, nya läkemedel samt cell- och genterapier.


På Cytiva kommer du att ständigt kunna utveckla dig själv och oss - genom att arbeta med utmaningar som verkligen spelar roll tillsammans med människor som bryr sig om varandra, våra kunder och deras patienter. sikt.

För frågor rörande tjänsten kontakta rekryterande chef Petter Lönnstedt, petter.lonnstedt@cytiva.com.


#LI-HJ2


#LI-Onsite


Join our winning team today. Together, we’ll accelerate the real-life impact of tomorrow’s science and technology. We partner with customers across the globe to help them solve their most complex challenges, architecting solutions that bring the power of science to life.


For more information, visit www.danaher.com.

title: Centre of Excellence Engineer
description:
Job Summary:


The **Centre of Excellence Engineer** provides highly visible, on-site customer support through installation, preventive and remedial maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of complex laboratory equipment and systems. You will verify and approve the operational quality of system equipment, instruct customers on operation and maintenance, and act as the company's liaison on administrative and technical matters for assigned projects. This role spans system hardware, software, PCs, networking/wireless networking, and laboratory instrumentation.


It is a customer support role, not a research position, though scientific literacy is beneficial to understand lab processes.


Key Responsibilities

* Preventive and corrective maintenance: Perform scheduled and unscheduled maintenance to ensure uptime and compliance, documenting all work to quality standards.
* Troubleshooting and diagnostics: Systematically diagnose issues on complex electro-mechanical instrumentation and associated software/controls; perform root-cause analysis and implement corrective actions.
* Installation and qualification: Install equipment and check out/approve operational quality; support IQ/OQ/PQ where applicable.
* Customer liaison: Serve as the primary interface with customer stakeholders on technical and administrative matters; interpret needs and coordinate with sales, customer support, or engineering as appropriate.
* Planning and coordination: Plan, schedule, and follow up with customers and suppliers on work status, parts availability, and timelines; ensure effective maintenance programs in a dynamic environment.
* Documentation and compliance: Maintain accurate service records, adhere to safety, GxP where applicable, and company/site policies.
* Knowledge sharing: Instruct end users on operation and maintenance; contribute to team know‑how and continuous improvement.
* Teamwork and autonomy: Work both independently and within small and large teams, taking personal responsibility for outcomes across a diverse instrument portfolio.

Equipment Portfolio Support will cover a wide range of laboratory systems, including:

* NMR, LC/MS and SFC systems
* Äkta system
* Tecan and other robot systems
* Freeze dryers (lyophilizers), rotary/roller evaporators, and vacuum pumps
* Centrifuges and balances/scales
* Clean/purified water systems
* Electro-mechanical subsystems with controls and rule-based fault correction, electronics, PCs, and networking/wireless networking

Candidate Profile Required:

* Relevant education in automation and/or a technical discipline, or equivalent years of maintenance/service experience in the technical industry
* Experience in the pharmaceutical or chemical industry
* Professional-level English
* Demonstrated team player mindset with a focus on daily delivery quality
* Alignment with PerkinElmer core values

Preferred/Advantageous

* Background in chemistry or related science
* Leadership experience (e.g., Team Leader, Supervisor)
* Continuous improvement experience (e.g., 5S, Lean)
* Coordination/planning and strong organizational skills
* Customer service experience

What We Offer

* Collaborative and supportive team environment
* Exposure to cutting-edge technologies
* Opportunity to support impactful scientific work
* Competitive compensation and benefits
* Ability to make a meaningful impact #ForTheBetter
* Professional development across a broad instrument landscape

Additional Information

* This is a hands-on service/maintenance technician role, not a research position; scientific literacy is advantageous.
* Roles based at our client site in Gothenburg require a drug test and background screening.
* All roles at PerkinElmer Sweden include a standard 6‑month probationary period.

title: Database Engineer
description:
**About Justera Group**  

Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.  

  

**About the Role:**  

We are seeking a highly skilled Database Engineer to join a dynamic team in Stockholm. This role requires a strong blend of database expertise and application development experience, with a focus on designing, optimizing, and maintaining high-performance data systems. The ideal candidate is analytical, proactive, and capable of solving complex technical challenges while collaborating across diverse teams.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable database solutions
* Optimize database performance through indexing, query tuning, and efficient schema design
* Collaborate with development teams to integrate database solutions with .NET-based applications
* Troubleshoot and resolve complex database and application issues
* Ensure data integrity, security, and reliability across systems
* Analyze existing systems and recommend improvements for performance and scalability
* Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to best practices

**Requirements:**

* Strong hands-on development experience with C# and the .NET framework
* Deep expertise in relational databases, particularly SQL and PostgreSQL
* Proven experience in database design, performance tuning, and optimization
* Solid understanding of data modeling, indexing strategies, and query optimization techniques
* Demonstrated ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues
* Strong analytical thinking and structured problem-solving skills
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical teams
* Self-driven, proactive, and able to take full ownership of tasks and deliverables

**What We Offer:**

* A dynamic and innovative workplace driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: Test Engineer
description:
**About us**  

At **Techrytera AB**, recruitment starts with you. We are a staffing and recruitment company with deep expertise and a clear focus on IT and Engineering. With our industry knowledge and close dialogue with both candidates and clients, we create precise matches where the right skills meet the right needs.


We believe that a truly great match is not just about a job description – it’s about your experience, your potential, and what you genuinely want to develop in. That’s why we always focus on the individual. By understanding your background, motivations, and ambitions, we can connect you with assignments and roles at our clients where you have the right conditions to grow and make an impact.


Our goal is to build long-term and meaningful collaborations – for both you as a candidate and for our clients.

 **Job Description**  

A Test Engineer consultants role and tasks is to plan, design, architect, and deploy effective test suites in order to ensure that developed systems fulfill defined end user requirements. The consultant will apply proven analytical and problem-solving skills to help validate, verify, communicate, and resolve systems/software issues through careful testing in order to maximize the benefit of investments and initiatives.

**The work may include the following tasks:**
  

* execute test cases according to test plan;
* develop test cases in coorporation with development personnel;
* developing test processes and procedures;
* report problems or failures;
* integrate, configurate and maintain automated test environments.

  

**Application**
When you apply through **Techrytera AB**, you’re not just applying for a job – you’re starting a dialogue about your career path. We want to get to know you, your experience, and your ambitions so that we can match you with the right opportunities at our clients.


Does this sound interesting? Submit your application or get in touch with us to learn more. We look forward to discovering the next step in your career together with you.

title: Electronics Engineer - Illumination & Power Outlet
description:
We are looking for a **Electronics Engineer - Illumination & Power Outlet** for a global automotive company in**Gothenburg.**

**Start is in May 2026, 12-month limited contract to begin with, possibility of extension after that.**

**Assignment description**


Illumination & Power Outlet team belongs to Interior System department where we develop the Displays, Wireless Phone Charger, USB, Interior Lights, Switches & Overhead Console.


Illumination & Power Outlet teams are now looking for a HW responsible with electronics experience for our development project. The team consists of members with competence within lead engineering, test, software and hardware with a clear ambition to work cross-functional.


We need talented people onboard. People with passion, energy, and drive to deliver good products for our customers. We are an organization that will support you to develop and reach your full potential.

**What you will do**

**You will work as HW responsible within our development teams.**


As a HW responsible, you will lead the HW and electronic development by having close cooperation with our supplier, to secure that they deliver and follow our requirements to deliver a product with high quality. You will also be part of, for example, creating requirement specifications, evaluate HW performance in rig and actual vehicles, support in test activities as well as being part of packaging discussions to secure required space in future vehicles. In this role you will have close collaboration with the project leader, SW responsible and test engineer within your team. You will also have many other interfaces for example, Interior, Design, EMC, Ergonomics, Electrical Performance, Perceived Quality, Vehicle Engineers and so on. This requires good ability to communicate with other people as well as being proactive, driven and dare to take your own initiatives.

**Are you:**

* Are structured and have a good sense of details.
* Are driven and result oriented.
* Are happy to share your best practices and equally happy to learn from others.
* Great communication and collaboration skills
* Have interest in cars and driver experience

**And have:**

* A degree of M.Sc or B.Sc in electrical or computer science, or equivalent technical training/experience
* Min 3-4 years of Experience in electronic development.
* Experience in electronic requirement management.
* Experience from Automotive is meritorious
* Good English skills, verbally and in writing
* Driving License: B (valid)
* Then you may be the new team member they are looking for!

  

This is a full-time consultant position in**Gothenburg** through Incluso. Start is in **May 2026, 12 months limited contract**to begin with. This role is 100% onsite in**Gothenburg.**


Please submit your CV as soon as possible since we will review the applications on an on-going basis.


For more information about this role, please contact **Kanika Singhal**recruiter at Incluso.

title: Machine Vision Engineer (Location Gothenburg)
description:
We're looking for a **Machine Vision Engineer** who thrives at the intersection of optics, software, and manufacturing intelligence. You won't just maintain systems — you will **shape how machines see the world**. If you have mastered Cognex VisionPro, love debugging stereo vision setups, and write clean C# code, this role is built for you.

* Develop, configure, and maintain machine vision systems using **Cognex VisionPro** for inspection and measurement
* Write, modify, and optimize **C#** scripts to power and enhance vision applications
* Set up, calibrate, and **debug 2D stereo (binocular)** positioning systems from scratch
* Tune and troubleshoot vision systems at measurement and **inspection stations** on live production lines
* Integrate vision solutions into production environments and ensure stable, reliable performance
* Work alongside automation, mechanical, and software teams to deliver end-to-end solutions
* Document **system configurations, calibration procedures, and debugging processes** for long-term maintainability

#### **Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in Automation, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a closely related field
* **Hands-on experience** with **Cognex VisionPro** or a comparable industrial vision platform
* Strong proficiency in **C# scripting** and software development within vision applications
* Solid understanding of **2D stereo vision principles** and end-to-end calibration workflows
* Proven experience debugging vision systems in **inspection or measurement** environments
* Familiarity with the **DCCK framework** or a similar software architecture
* Must be **legally eligible to work in Sweden** (valid work permit or EU/EEA citizenship required)
* **Good communication skills in English**, both written and verbal.
* **Proficiency in Chinese (Mandarin) language is an added advantage**

#### **Nice to Have:**

* Experience in **industrial automation or manufacturing environments**
* Knowledge of camera calibration, image processing, and optical systems
* Strong problem-solving instincts and spot root causes, not just symptoms
* Self-starter who communicates clearly and thrives within cross-functional teams


In case you have any questions, please feel free to reach out on shivangi@minnovation.se

title: Software Engineer Intern
description:
We're a small team of investors and builders running one of the Nordics' most active early-stage funds. We've built most of our own tooling, and we're hiring an engineer to help extend it.
 



  





**About the role** 




 We are looking for a software engineer with at least one year remaining of their studies to build, improve and ship the tools we use every day: sourcing systems, research agents, MCPs and data pipelines that connect the fast-moving outside world to our CRM, research stack and other systems.
 



  





 We've built our own sourcing infrastructure - signal aggregation, research agents, CRM automations across email and Slack, and MCP integrations across all our internal systems. You'll be working shoulder to shoulder with the investment team - in and extending that stack.
 



  





 We use AI aggressively in our own work, and we expect you to do the same, and to push us further.
 



  





**What you’ll work on** 



* Build and ship internal tools in Python and TypeScript to find and evaluate startups; support our portfolio, automate our work, and re-architect and re-design parts of our stack
* Refine and iterate on existing internal tools and agents
* Work side-by-side with the investment team to identify and automate repetitive processes
* Assist in the investment process through technical research and due diligence
* Engage with our growing portfolio of early-stage startups



  





**What we offer you** 



* Paid internship on-site in Stockholm, ~10-20 hrs/week with the option for full-time during the summer, with flexible work arrangements to accommodate exams and other commitments
* High trust and real ownership, your work ships and gets used
* Close collaboration with investors who have backgrounds as founders and operators, and a front-row seat to the Nordic startup ecosystem



  





**What we’re looking for** 



* Pursuing a MSc or BSc at a top university in CS or adjacent field, with at least one year left
* Solid Python & TypeScript fundamentals, and comfortable with agents, web scrapers, databases, and building things end-to-end
* Forward-leaning on coding agents – you have opinions about context engineering and you’re curious to try new tech to build faster and better
* Genuine passion for startups and early-stage tech, coupled with a strong curiosity
* Prior engineering experience building in a startup is a strong plus
* Based in Stockholm and fluent in English; Swedish is a plus



  





**Start date** 




 As soon as possible.
 



  





**Application process** 




 We review applications on a rolling basis.
 



  





 Alongside your CV, highlight what you’ve built previously. We care more about what you’ve shipped than your GPA.

title: Software Engineer - Internal Tooling
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 As a
 **Software Engineer in Internal Tooling team** 
 at Legora, you will build and own the systems that power our internal operations for Customer Enablement at scale. This team sits at the intersection of Engineering, and Operations, designing and developing the backoffice platforms, automation workflows, and AI-driven tools that enable our teams to deliver world-class support to our clients.
   

  

 You’ll create mission-critical systems that increase efficiency, reduce manual work, and embed AI directly into our customer support and operational processes. Your work will have immediate, measurable impact across the company.
   

  

*This is a Stockholm-based, 5-day in-office role. We believe building together in person drives better outcomes in a fast-scaling environment.*
**What You Will Be Doing**
* Own and evolve our internal backoffice systems, including support tooling, admin interfaces, and operational platforms.
* Design and implement automation workflows that eliminate manual processes and improve reliability across customer support and operations.
* Build AI-powered internal tools that leverage LLMs and intelligent workflows to assist support agents, automate triage, surface insights, and improve response quality and speed.
* Work with technologies such as React, Node.js, AWS, Azure, and Kubernetes, leveraging the right tools to solve complex challenges.
* Architect and maintain integrations between third-party systems (CRM, ticketing platforms, billing systems, analytics tools, etc.) and our core product.
* Develop scalable internal services and APIs that support operational needs while maintaining high standards for security, observability, and reliability.
* Partner closely with Customer Enablement, Engineering and Product teams to deeply understand pain points and translate them into robust technical solutions.
* Take end-to-end ownership from problem definition and system design to deployment, monitoring, and iteration.
* Continuously identify opportunities to apply AI and automation to unlock efficiency gains across the organization.


**Who You Are**
* An experienced engineer who enjoys building systems that enable other teams to move faster and operate smarter.
* Comfortable working across the stack, with strong backend fundamentals and a pragmatic approach to frontend and internal UX.
* Experienced with APIs, integrations, data pipelines, and cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS/Azure, Kubernetes).
* Excited about applying AI/LLMs in practical, production-grade workflows, especially in operational or support contexts.
* Passionate about automation, system design, and reducing complexity through thoughtful engineering.
* A proactive problem solver who thrives in fast-moving startup environments and enjoys owning systems end-to-end.
* Motivated by impact, you care about building tools that directly improve how the company operates at scale.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Fullstack Product Engineer
description:
**About Mynt**
 Mynt is an all-in-one corporate card and spend management platform on a mission to reinvent business banking. By combining smart software with cards and payments infrastructure, we remove manual, time-consuming admin from how companies manage spending.
   

  

 We’re growing fast across the Nordics and have secured significant funding to support our continued expansion in Sweden and internationally.
   

  

 Mynt is a tech-first, product-driven company where teams are empowered to solve real problems and build things that matter. We’re a licensed e-money institution, card issuer, and payment processor, operating under the supervision of the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority. Through strong partnerships with leading players such as Fortnox and Nordea — and many more on the way — our solutions are now live across all Nordic countries.
   

  

 Joining Mynt means helping shape both a category-defining product and a company that’s still early in its journey. With 100+ colleagues (and growing), we’re building teams that value ownership, momentum, and customer impact.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Mynt is now looking for a top performer with a strong drive and creative goal-oriented mindset. You will be expected to work closely with the CPO, operations and product teams to analyze data, ship features and drive new initiatives.
   

  

 You need to have a strong interest in working in a dynamic and ambitious scale-up environment and have strong analytical and technical skills.
   

  

**What you’ll be diving into:**
* Build product features end to end, from frontend through backend to data.
* Use our customer, transaction, and product data to figure out what to build next, and measure whether it worked.
* Partner closely with the CPO, product, and operations teams to understand customer problems deeply.
* Take full ownership of problems, not just tickets.


**What you bring to the table:**
* Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or equivalent experience
* Comfort working across the stack, from frontend to backend to data
* You think in products and customers, not just code
* Fluent in Swedish and English


**What we can offer you:**
* Be part of an amazing team in a fast-growing scale-up.
* Work closely with a fun, high-performing team.
* Enjoy a flexible working environment with a great office location in central Stockholm.
* Shape your own development and responsibilities based on your passion and interest.


*The position will be filled as soon as we find a suitable candidate. Please reach out to us as soon as possible if you believe this for you!*
